content: MCP gobby tool integration (mcp__gobby_* tools) fails with 'Invalid arguments' errors during parameter passing. The `call_tool` MCP proxy also requires a 'server_name' parameter that is not obvious from its interface—omitting it produces a 'server_name Field required' validation error. Direct Bash CLI commands are reliable for gobby operations (e.g., 'gobby tasks reopen TASK_ID', 'gobby agents list', 'gobby agents kill RUN_REF'). Use Bash CLI instead of call_tool proxy to avoid discovery-time debugging.

Gobby daemon's embedding service can timeout during create_memory operations with ReadTimeout errors. The daemon itself remains stable (continues running) but the memory creation fails. Logs show 'Retrying request to _base_client.py:1621' when this occurs.

To set config values in Gobby's DB-first config store at runtime: PUT http://localhost:60887/api/config/values with {"values": {"key.path": "value"}}. Returns {"ok": true, "requires_restart": true} when a daemon restart is needed.
User preference: Always choose the proper fix over the quick and dirty fix. If it wouldn't pass a code review, don't write it. No shortcuts, no hacks — write code you'd be proud to have reviewed.

Database is authoritative source of truth for skills. Templates are install-time defaults that seed DB only when row doesn't exist on first install. Sync behavior differs by environment: dev mode syncs DB → template files (overwrites SKILL.md); production skips overwrite if DB row exists (preserves user modifications). Gobby-tagged bundled skills overwrite on every sync in dev mode only.

Neo4j service uses unified docker-compose.services.yml with --profile neo4j instead of separate docker-compose.neo4j.yml (which pinned neo4j:5.26 and caused installer bugs). Related files: neo4j.py, docker-compose.services.yml

Bundled skill YAML updates currently auto-propagate to users automatically; Rules and Workflows do not auto-propagate. If moving skills to filesystem model like workflows, must preserve auto-propagation behavior specifically for bundled/platform-authored skills to avoid breaking current update delivery mechanism.

yaml.YAMLError string representation includes descriptive formatting with source location (format: 'error message\n  in "<source>", line X, column Y'). This is suitable for including in HTTP error responses for workflow import failures.
The close_task API requires a changes_summary parameter—omitting it causes a 'missing_changes_summary' error. This is a required field when closing tasks in gobby.

Daemon race condition: stop_daemon_util() (line 838) only calls kill_all_gobby_daemons() in stale-PID branch, not after successful daemon kill. Orphaned watchdog processes survive, auto-restart daemon via restart_daemon(), then start command (line 192) finds active PID file and fails with 'already running' error.

In maintenance.py subprocess gcode calls, must pass `--project <root_path>` flag to ensure gcode resolves project context from target path rather than daemon's CWD; missing this flag causes cross-project path confusion

The `memory-recall-on-prompt` workflow rule is missing the required `inject_result: true` parameter when calling mcp_calendar tools. Other similar memory-related rules using mcp_calendar don't have this issue, identifying it as a specific bug isolated to that single rule.

Test-battery skill: both reset step (line 55) and cleanup step (line 262) must use `list_running_agents` with no filter, not `list_agents` with `parent_session_id` filter. These steps must remain consistent.

Two commits address transcript processing: c2cf58cda (Sync worker for deferred Neo4j/Qdrant writes) and 1c9737fe7 (Stop retrying transcript processing for missing files). Both target session deferral loops caused by missing transcript files.

Claude Code's file API enforces client-side read-before-write validation during tool use. Write operations are rejected with error 'File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.' if the file hasn't been read in the current session. This is Claude Code's safety mechanism, not a filesystem or rule engine issue.

Refactoring scope for skills system: (1) Eliminate source='template' rows, (2) Sync bundled skills directly as source='installed', (3) Overwrite on sync for gobby-tagged skills, (4) Remove SkillTemplatesMixin and related code. Decision: Keep existing project scoping unchanged—it works and bundled skills install globally by default.

MCP enforcement uses graduated strategy: `require-schema-before-call` configured with `inject_context` (soft nudge, non-blocking) while `require-server-listed-for-schema` remains as `block` (hard enforcement fallback)

sessions.py:251 - transcript_manager requires lazy initialization pattern to avoid crashes when session_manager is None at initialization time. Use lazy loading rather than eager initialization in sessions router.
memory skill documentation is incomplete: only 9 of 19 actual subcommands documented. Becomes stale when new tools added. Requires comprehensive documentation refresh.

When merging dev into main on gobby, .gobby/memories.jsonl and .gobby/tasks.jsonl frequently cause merge conflicts because multiple sessions write to them concurrently. Workaround: stash local changes before pulling, merge, push, then pop stash and resolve conflicts in the gobby metadata files before committing.

MODEL_OPTIONS is an exported constant array with structure: [{ value: 'modelname', label: 'Display Label' }, ...]. Currently includes at least { value: 'opus', label: 'Claude Opus' }. Settings panel was hardcoding models instead of using this constant—needs refactoring for multi-provider support.

Task claim state management is in src/gobby/workflows/task_claim_state.py with pure helper functions add_claimed_task() and remove_claimed_task(). These return merge dicts without mutating state directly.

Task #8836 migration progress: v117 migration function works (10/10 tests pass). All consumer updates done (sync.py, definitions.py, lifecycle.py, routes/agents.py). agent_definitions.py deleted. One remaining issue: sync_bundled_agents fails for 'coordinator' and 'generic' agents because workflow_definitions has UNIQUE constraint on (name, COALESCE(project_id, '__global__')) that doesn't include workflow_type. Both names exist as bundled workflows AND bundled agents. Fix: in sync_bundled_agents, when existing entry has workflow_type != 'agent', skip with warning. Also need to fix the test assertion to not require 'generic'. After fixing, run regression tests for agents/test_definitions.py, agents/test_sync.py, and servers/routes/test_agents_routes.py.

SafeExpressionEvaluator in safe_evaluator.py does NOT support IfExp (ternary: x if cond else y) — or didn't until bafc3d51 added visit_IfExp. Pipeline conditions that use ternary expressions silently fail (ValueError caught by should_run_step, defaults to True). Always test new AST node types if pipeline conditions use Python expressions.

Handoff markdown distinction: `compact_markdown` is for internal compaction (agent's own processing), while `summary_markdown` is for cross-session handoff and is agent-authored (not system-generated). The context injection mechanism uses these differently and the distinction affects how delivery templates should be structured.

Arguments passed to gobby tools must be valid JSON objects. URL-encoded format (e.g., 'task_id=#10454&sess') fails with 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter' error. Always use proper JSON formatting, not query-string style parameters.

close_task MCP tool requires commit_summary (changes_summary) field as a mandatory parameter. Omitting it throws 'missing_changes_summary' error. Must be provided even if task had no substantive changes.
features.py: TestSummarizerConfig dataclass includes timeout field for explicit timeout control. Added to prevent indefinite hangs in test failure summarization during run_check execution.

When syncing bundled DB records that have a UNIQUE index (e.g., agent_definitions with idx_agent_defs_name_scope_project), do NOT use import-then-delete to achieve atomicity — the import will fail with UNIQUE constraint violation because the old record still exists. Instead, use manager.update() for in-place atomic updates, which issues a single UPDATE SQL statement that can't leave the DB in an inconsistent state. The delete-then-import pattern is also risky because if import fails after delete, the old record is permanently lost. The manager.update() approach solves both problems.

When the user asks for analysis, give direct answers to their questions and nothing else. Don't jump ahead to investigating, fixing, or taking action. Answer simply and wait for them to drive the conversation until they're satisfied. Only act when they explicitly ask for action.

_resolve_provider helper: mypy cannot narrow str | None unions through conditional 'in' membership checks. Even when logic is correct (p=None early-returns), mypy fails to infer terminal else branch always receives str. Requires explicit type cast/annotation to satisfy strict type checking.
The call_tool endpoint in gobby requires a 'server_name' field as a mandatory parameter. Calls without this field fail with validation error 'server_name: Field required'.

When mcp__gobby_* tools fail with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors, switch to Bash CLI with gobby commands directly (e.g., `gobby tasks reopen TASK`, `gobby agents list`, `gobby agents kill RUN_REF`). The MCP tool proxy has parameter mapping issues that don't affect the CLI.

Shift+Enter in Claude Code under tmux fails because tmux doesn't pass through the escape sequence (\x1b[13;2u or kitty-protocol equivalent) to the terminal by default. Solution requires adding a keybinding to ~/.tmux.conf and reloading with 'tmux source-file', but the specific binding varies by terminal emulator (Ghostty, iTerm2, Kitty, WezTerm, Terminal.app, Antigravity).

MCP tools for gobby operations (mcp__gobby_* commands like reopen_task, kill_agent) fail with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors when parameters are nested inside an 'arguments' object. Root cause: call_tool parameters must be flattened at the top level, not nested in an arguments object. Also, arguments must be valid JSON, not URL-encoded format. Workaround: use the Bash CLI directly instead (e.g., `gobby tasks reopen #<ID>`, `gobby agents kill <RUN_REF>`, `gobby agents list`).

kill_agent tool can fail with error "No agent found for session #<number>". Spawned agents may disappear from session state (timeout, early termination, or session cleanup). Multi-agent workflows need error handling for kill failures rather than assuming they always succeed.

is_plan_file() function returns True for any .md file under .gobby/plans/, intentionally exempting these files from the require-task-before-edit rule—plan files are safe-to-write without a task context by design

Chat UI components (MessageList, MessageItem, ToolCallCard, Markdown) lack error boundary wrapper—render failures (malformed markdown, unexpected tool results, syntax highlighting errors) crash entire app

set_variable and get_variable are top-level proxy tools (mcp__gobby__set_variable, mcp__gobby__get_variable) that are called directly, NOT routed through call_tool. The require-schema-before-call rule only fires on call_tool invocations, making it inapplicable to direct variable tool access.

State machine approach to TDD enforcement (toggle `tdd_test_written` flag, check on code write, reset) breaks when agent requires multiple code writes after a single test write. Path tracking with `tdd_covered_paths` list is more flexible but requires language-specific convention inference logic.
Tree-sitter query execution API lives on Node/QueryCursor, not Query object. Use QueryCursor.matches() to execute queries for matching nodes.

The gobby call_tool proxy function requires a 'server_name' parameter. Omitting it causes validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]' (Turn 46). This is a non-obvious required field when calling tools via the proxy.
The close_task operation in gobby requires a 'changes_summary' parameter. Omitting it fails with error 'missing_changes_summary'. This field is mandatory even for tasks with no code changes.

Plan/expand skill content preservation: The plan skill (src/gobby/install/shared/skills/plan/SKILL.md) now uses a heading-based template format (### N.N sections) instead of checklist bullets. Each section's content becomes the subtask description verbatim during expansion. The expand skill (src/gobby/install/shared/skills/expand/SKILL.md) Phase 3 now has anti-summarization rules: TDD header (3-4 lines) + full plan section content below a --- separator. Key principle: the implementing agent only sees its own subtask, so each plan section must be self-contained with file paths, code examples, schemas, and behavioral specs. The /gobby:x invocation syntax was also fixed to /gobby x throughout both files.

The workflow_definitions table contains 163 stale rows with source='template' that shadow and disable their enabled installed counterparts. This affects all workflows (not just grep). These disabled templates nullify the installed rule versions and must be deleted for rules to behave correctly.
hook_dispatcher.py contains an outdated `gobby start` suggestion that needs to be updated or removed during code cleanup.

The gobby MCP `call_tool` function requires a `server_name` parameter that is not exposed in initial tool discovery via `list_resources`. Arguments must be passed as valid JSON in an 'arguments' parameter. Multiple format attempts (object, string, concatenated) failed with 'Unknown parameter' or 'Invalid JSON' errors (Turns 13-42) before a validation error finally revealed server_name as a required field (Turn 46).

When `memory-review-gate` blocks session exit after saving memory, the `pending_memory_review` variable persists and must be manually cleared: `set_variable('pending_memory_review', False, 'session')`. Gate is defined in src/gobby/install/shared/rules/deprecated/stop-gates.yaml and checks this variable—it won't automatically clear after successful memory save.

The gobby call_tool requires a mandatory 'server_name' parameter in its Pydantic-validated arguments (turn 46). Without this field, the tool fails with 'Field required' validation error. Multiple attempts with other parameter names failed before discovering this requirement.

Hash-skip optimization in tasks.py removed because: saved only microseconds (single file write) while hash computation cost and tasks_meta.json tracking exceeded benefit. Replaced with unconditional read/merge/write pattern from _export_memories_sync() (line 389)—simpler and more reliable.

Bash output compression threshold optimization: 1000 chars is optimal min_length—captures medium-length outputs (20-30 test runs with -v, medium git diff, lint runs) where compression provides value. Thresholds below 1000 have diminishing returns due to regex/pipeline overhead (~5% savings). compress-bash-output rule verified at 99.2% savings on verbose pytest output.

rule_engine.py has _clear_edit_write_state(variables) helper that sets edit_write_pending=False and edit_write_stop_blocks=0. Used at 3 sites: circuit breaker tripped, no-rules after_tool success, with-rules after_tool success. Keeps the clearing logic DRY.

The call_tool MCP proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter as a required field. Validation fails with 'Field required [type=missing]' if this parameter is omitted. Must be provided when calling call_tool.

SQLite table-recreate pattern in gobby migrations: When adding CHECK constraints (which ALTER TABLE cannot do), use the table-recreate pattern. IMPORTANT: Must disable FK checks with `db.execute('PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF')` before the transaction and re-enable after in a finally block, because LocalDatabase enables `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` per-connection (database.py:171) and CREATE TABLE with REFERENCES will fail if the referenced table doesn't exist. Also, when patching LocalDatabase in tests, patch at `gobby.storage.database.LocalDatabase` not `gobby.agents.definitions.LocalDatabase` since it's imported locally inside methods.
Stale daemon instances cause sync errors with effect types (e.g., rewrite_input, compress_output not recognized). Always restart the daemon when effect type validation fails.

require-task-close rule fix: The stop-gate rule at install/shared/rules/stop-gates/require-task-close.yaml had two bugs. (1) The Jinja template used {{ task_ref }} which is never set in the rule engine's eval context — only in commit_policy.py's context. Fixed to use {{ claimed_task_id[:8] if claimed_task_id else '(unknown)' }} which is available via _build_eval_context() flattening workflow variables. (2) The when condition didn't guard against empty claimed_task_id, causing the rule to fire with blank task IDs. Added `and variables.get('claimed_task_id')` guard.

Pre-existing issue: 14 DB-based tests in tests/workflows/test_task_enforcement_rules.py fail because _sync_bundled() + manager.get_by_name() returns None for all task-enforcement rules (block-native-task-tools, require-commit-before-close, block-skip-validation-with-commit, block-ask-during-stop-compliance, track-task-claim, track-task-release, and require-task-before-edit). The sync function doesn't find rules from the split file structure under task-enforcement/. Only condition evaluation tests (which don't use DB sync) pass.

The 76 PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning failures stem from async cleanup issues where discover_lifecycle_workflows() coroutines are created but never awaited. This affects multiple test modules: hooks (30 warnings), skills (44), mcp_proxy tools (20, 13, 12, 9), and test_import_path (17). The fix requires adding 'await' to all discovery calls or using pytest.mark.asyncio appropriately.

Sessions can currently claim at most ONE task at a time. This is enforced by the `track-task-claim` rule which sets the `task_claimed` variable, preventing multiple task claims per session. This is a known limitation tracked in bug task #9537 for future support.

Skill template refactor spans 15 files across storage, sync, MCP tools, HTTP routes, CLI, frontend, tests, and database layers. Core deletions: `_templates.py` and `install_from_template.py`. Key rewrites: `sync.py` (420→~100 lines, highest risk) and `_metadata.py`. Requires DB migration converting template rows. Plan documented at `docs/plans/skill-template-refactor.md`

Task lifecycle tools in _lifecycle.py must return success: False on errors so that _process_tool_proxy_result can correctly identify and handle task failures. Returning success: true with an error is a bug that masks failures.

The correct MCP call effect format in Gobby workflow definition_json is: {"type": "mcp_call", "server": "server-name", "tool": "tool_name", "arguments": {...}, "inject_result": true}. The 'inject_result: true' field is required for the result to reach the agent.

Tool block clearing in rule_engine.py (~lines 220 and 250): ANY successful AFTER_TOOL event now unconditionally clears tool_block_pending, regardless of which tool succeeded. Previously required the exact blocked tool to succeed. Changed in both early-return and main evaluation paths (commit 15d57369).

Gobby tool system expects flattened parameter structures, not nested objects. Multiple attempts to pass parameters in structured formats failed with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameters' errors (Turns 5-24) until parameters were flattened at the top level.

The gobby web UI has a split styling approach: the chat UI (web/src/components/chat/) uses Tailwind CSS with its own styles.css importing tailwindcss plus additional non-Tailwind styles from the index.css decomposition. All other pages use component-scoped CSS files (e.g., SessionsPage.css, TerminalsPage.css, tasks-page.css) imported by their React components, with shared CSS custom properties (--font-size-base: 16px, --font-mono, --font-sans) and global styles in web/src/styles/index.css (703 lines). index.css was decomposed from 13,849 lines into 16 component-scoped files. When matching styles between chat and non-chat components, map Tailwind classes to their CSS equivalents (e.g., text-xs = 0.75rem, py-1.5 = 0.375rem, px-4 = 1rem).

Migration tests that simulate a pre-migration DB state (e.g., test_migration_103.py setting schema_version=102) must also create tables that later migrations expect. Migration 107 alters agent_definitions (created by migration 92), so any test starting at v102 must include agent_definitions in its manual setup.

Bundled rules split from 11 group YAML files into ~80 individual files in group subdirectories under src/gobby/install/shared/rules/<group-name>/. Old group files moved to deprecated/. Individual files use tags: [group-name] instead of group: field. sync_bundled_rules() skips deprecated/ via path filter and has orphan cleanup for removed rules.

When invoking gobby MCP tools via 'call_tool', the 'arguments' parameter must be valid JSON (e.g., {"task_id": "value"}), not URL-encoded strings (e.g., task_id=#10454&...). Malformed argument encoding triggers 'Invalid JSON in arguments' errors.
Task-sync numbering system can create duplicate [gobby-NNNN] commit message prefixes in history for completely different tasks/work items - this is a known artifact, not a merge conflict or duplicate

User preference: Questions whether LLM-assigned 'importance' fields are reliable for memory filtering, suggesting uncertainty about using AI-generated metadata as filtering criteria. User interrupted the session when asked about this implementation detail.

P2P Agent Infrastructure: AgentCommandManager lives in src/gobby/storage/agent_commands.py. It manages parent-child session commands with lifecycle: pending → running → completed/failed/cancelled. InterSessionMessageManager now has get_undelivered_messages() and mark_delivered() for delivery tracking. LocalSessionManager.is_ancestor() walks parent_session_id chain with cycle guard. Migration v116 added message_type, metadata_json, delivered_at columns to inter_session_messages and created agent_commands table.

Bug #9064: close_task in _lifecycle.py failed to clear task_claimed/claimed_task_id session variables after successful close. Root cause: the claimed_task_id comparison and clearing logic was outside the `if state:` block where claimed_task_id was defined, and the outer `except Exception: pass` silently swallowed any errors. Fix: moved comparison+clearing inside `if state:`, added UUID normalization for robust comparison, replaced silent exception swallowing with logger.warning(). File: src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/tasks/_lifecycle.py lines ~250-290. The observer fallback in observers.py intentionally does NOT clear for Claude Code (no tool_output in AFTER_TOOL hooks), so CC users are entirely dependent on this MCP proxy clearing code.
`gobby index` is a standalone CLI utility that operates independently of the daemon process; does not require daemon to be running

When using merge_worktree tool, pass the worktree ID (e.g., 'wt-6aaf99' from list_worktrees output), not the branch name. Passing branch name (e.g., 'task-9463') results in error: 'Worktree not found'. Must call list_worktrees first to get the actual worktree ID.

WebSocket disconnect during streaming (_messaging.py lines 315, 742, 762; useChat.ts) leaves backend processing with orphaned SDK events while client reconnects, causing race conditions and dropped messages

In gobby's source_control.py, `_resolve_project` must fall back to the first non-global project when the GitHub tab selection is 'All Projects' (or no project is selected). Without this fallback, source control operations fail because GitHub APIs require a specific project context.

In the gobby project, a pre-tool-use hook at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py blocks file Write operations (including plan files) until a task has been created and claimed via the gobby-tasks MCP server's create_task tool. The agent must create a task first before any file writes will succeed.

ToolSearch tool triggers 'PreToolUse' hook errors in the gobby environment. Workaround: Use mcp__gobby_* MCP tools directly (e.g., mcp__gobby_get_tools_info, mcp__gobby_call_tool) for tool discovery and invocation instead of ToolSearch.

gobby agents kill requires RUN_REF as a positional argument, not flag-based parameters. Correct format: 'gobby agents kill [OPTIONS] RUN_REF' (--session flag causes 'No such option' error). Discover RUN_REF from 'gobby agents list' output.

QA agents can commit to tasks while they're in `needs_review` or `review_approved` states—either making fixes themselves or returning work to the original agent. Both `mark_task_needs_review()` and `mark_task_review_approved()` in `_lifecycle_status.py` must handle commits that occur during review phases, not assume these states are transition-only with no intermediate changes.

The gobby project's ./reports/ directory contains lint and audit output files (pip-audit-*.txt, ruff-*.txt) but NOT coderabbit reports. CodeRabbit reports are not stored locally in the reports directory.

Gobby memory access is controlled via three enforced hook blocks: block-claude-memory-read (prevents reading ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/), block-claude-memory-search (prevents searching memory), block-claude-memory-write (prevents writing memory). UserPromptSubmit hook redirects memory operations to gobby-memory MCP as fallback.

Merge agent removed from autonomous dev-loop and orchestrator pipelines (commit c0508c2c). Merge is now manual — the pipeline terminates when all tasks reach review_approved status. The merge agent definition still exists but is not spawned by any pipeline. Reason: pushing to remote and closing tasks autonomously is the highest-risk operation and should be tested on a test project with packaged gobby before enabling. The merge agent also has a no-push conflict with worker-safety rules that needs resolution (push should be an MCP tool, not raw Bash).

Two-part fix for template enabled status: (1) Fix template YAML files to set `enabled: false` (prevents future auto-enables on install), and (2) toggle existing installed instances via `toggle_rule()` function (fixes current database state). These are separate operations because YAML only affects new installs.

SQLite VACUUM operations should be executed outside of active gobby sessions to avoid interrupting the daemon connection mid-operation. Run VACUUM as a separate standalone sqlite3 command, not during an active session.

Gobby has no standard `db` pytest fixture that auto-runs migrations. Individual test modules create test DBs independently (e.g., `db_loader` in `tests/workflows/conftest.py`). When adding schema columns, test fixture SQL CREATE TABLE statements in multiple conftest files must be manually updated in parallel with migrations—there's no auto-sync mechanism.

The mcp__gobby_call_tool endpoint requires 'server_name' as a mandatory parameter in the arguments object. Validation errors will occur with 'Field required' if missing. Parameters should be flattened rather than nested.

Critical bug: If hook_dispatcher.py is deleted or missing while Claude Code hooks are configured, the Stop hook failure creates an infinite loop. Each response triggers the stop hook, which fails and generates a new 'Stop hook feedback' message, which triggers another response, ad infinitum. The only way to break the loop is for the user to Ctrl+C and fix the hook file externally. The agent cannot fix it because PreToolUse hooks also fail, blocking all tool calls. Root cause in this case was unclear — the file existed at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py but uv run couldn't find it temporarily (possibly during venv rebuild or daemon restart).
BMAD is a complete system (agents + skills + pipelines) not just a skill collection. It currently uses emoji markers and CRITICAL=TRUE XML tags as workarounds for system-level configuration.

In renderer.py, build_render_context() flattens step outputs as top-level template variable names, enabling both scan_open.output.tasks and steps.scan_open.output.tasks notation. This pattern supports backward-compatible Jinja2 template access to step outputs.

The discover_lifecycle_workflows method in gobby's HooksManager has a signature issue causing 30+ PytestWarnings. The method is called throughout tests without the project_path parameter, but grep shows it's defined with `project_path: Path | str | None = None` at line 625-626 in the implementation. This suggests a parameter mismatch between the method signature and how tests are calling it.

Unsloth installation on macOS (as of 2026-04-02): use `uv pip install unsloth --torch-backend=auto` with automatic MPS detection. The earlier `[mps]` extra syntax is outdated; `--torch-backend=auto` is the current method. Setup: `uv venv unsloth_env --python 3.13 && source unsloth_env/bin/activate`

runner.py: _summarize_failure() LLM call requires asyncio.wait_for(timeout=30) wrapper to prevent indefinite hangs. Pattern: all LLM calls in async contexts should have explicit timeout wrapping to prevent run_check timeouts.

MCP daemon logs include sender_pid and source fields in shutdown events. When investigating daemon unavailability, check daemon logs (startup sequence, shutdown events, health checks, SIGTERM signals, watchdog, hook manager logs) for shutdown events with these fields—they identify which process triggered shutdown (e.g., mcp_stop command from a previous Claude Code session).

The plugin system was removed per the workflows-v2 plan (docs/plans/workflows-v2.md). MCP tools replace plugins as the extension mechanism. However, the removal was incomplete — CLI commands (src/gobby/cli/extensions.py), ClaudePluginsProvider (src/gobby/skills/hubs/claude_plugins.py), and registrations in __init__.py and runner.py still exist. Task #9067 tracks the source removal.

UX convention: All text inputs in the web UI should use Shift+Enter for newline and Enter for submit. This applies to AskUserQuestion 'Other' input, PlanApprovalBar feedback textarea, and any future text inputs. Josh confirmed this is the expected behavior pattern.

The A2UI canvas plan (docs/plans/a2ui-canvas.md) was hardened with CodeRabbit security recommendations in commit 8b61e9fd. Key design decisions: ownership check uses server-side session context (self._get_conversation_id(websocket)) instead of client-provided conversation_id, resolve_interaction uses per-canvas asyncio.Lock for atomicity, WebSocket disconnect cancels pending canvases via cancel_conversation_canvases(), nh3 (not Bleach) for server-side HTML sanitization, three-state optimistic UI flow (active/pending/completed), payload depth validation (max 10 levels) on both backend and frontend. The canvas system is still unimplemented — this is plan-only.
Gobby v0.2.25 baseline test coverage: 2 vitest unit tests + 4 Playwright integration specs. Reference this baseline when expanding test suite to verify no duplication.
Frontend-only React hook fixes (like useTmuxSession changes) do not require daemon or WebSocket server restarts—they only require browser reload or hot refresh.

The `call_tool` MCP tool requires a `server_name` parameter as a required field in arguments. Missing this parameter results in Pydantic validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'. This was discovered after many failed attempts.

sync_bundled_rules() root cause: Tasks #9025, #9051, and (closed duplicate) #8998 all share a single root cause — sync_bundled_rules() is not correctly inserting bundled YAML templates into the workflow_definitions DB table. Tests that call sync_bundled_rules() then query for rules by name get None. This affects 27+ tests across test_memory_lifecycle_rules.py, test_tool_hygiene_rules.py, and test_sync-related tests. Uncommitted changes to storage/workflow_definitions.py and storage/migrations.py appear to be in-progress work toward a fix but are not resolving it yet. The workflow_definitions storage layer is the critical path.

Gobby's `require-error-triage` enforcement rule blocks session termination and all subsequent actions (commits, file modifications, task closures) until error triage is complete. Agent must run CI/test commands to identify pre-existing failures, then either fix failures in touched code or explicitly set `set_variable(name="pre_existing_errors_triaged", value=true)` to confirm no orthogonal fixable errors exist.
Summary generation in daemon experiencing consistent error: 'str' object has no attribute 'get' - type handling bug in generation pipeline; failures deferred on retry (non-fatal but persistent)

The call_tool MCP tool requires 'server_name' as a mandatory parameter. Omitting it triggers validation error: 'Field required [type=missing, input_value={...}]' (Turn 46). This field must be included in all call_tool invocations.
Silent failure risk: Deleted or missing session JSONL files cause resume to fail silently with no error message or logging—no fallback mechanism exists
LocalSessionManager (storage/sessions.py) uses get() not get_session() to fetch a single session by ID. The sessions/manager.py SessionManager has get_session() but the storage layer does not.
The `call_tool` MCP function requires `server_name` and `tool_name` as mandatory parameters (not just positional arguments). Validation will fail with 'Field required' error if either is missing.

`wait` steps are implemented via `_execute_wait_step()` at `src/gobby/workflows/pipeline_executor.py:553`. They block pipeline execution until external events signal continuation. Event signaling is managed by `CompletionEventRegistry` in `src/gobby/events/completion_registry.py`. Wait steps are intentional design for sequential workflows requiring external triggers (e.g., user confirmation before proceeding).

Orchestrator core logic verified working: task expansion, dependency resolution, QA approval all passed. Test failures traced to agent lifecycle/terminal layer, not orchestration design. Failures are daemon resilience gaps, not architectural flaws.

Gobby daemon hit 'OSError: [Errno 24] Too many open files' despite fd limits already at system maximum (soft=9223372036854775807, hard=9223372036854775807, i.e., max int64). This indicates the issue is NOT a simple ulimit problem—investigate file handle lifecycle/resource leaks instead.

User preference: Workflows page should have tabs — Rules, Transitions, Pipelines, Agents — not separate pages. All definition types share workflow_definitions table with workflow_type discriminator. Step/lifecycle type distinction is dead (tech debt in naming). Auto-task is the only step workflow in active use and is critical for overnight autonomous work — must have rule-equivalent functionality after refactor.

Task closure tool constraint: skip_validation flag cannot be used when a commit is attached to the closure request. If validator fails with commit attached, resubmit closure without skip_validation flag to allow validator retry.

`listed_servers` is pre-seeded at startup with 15 internal gobby servers. The proxy `"gobby"` itself is NOT in this list; it contains only downstream servers (`gobby-tasks`, `gobby-sessions`, `gobby-memory`, etc.)
Gobby hook dispatcher enforces [require-task-close] rule that blocks stop actions on tasks. Workaround discovered: reopening the task can bypass this block when no work has started

When closing a gobby task via mcp__gobby__call_tool, the tool requires a 'changes_summary' parameter. Omitting it returns error {"success":false,"error":"missing_changes_summary"}. The task close call also requires first setting session flags (e.g., skip_hooks) via mcp__gobby__call_tool with scope 'session' before proceeding.

Gobby Web UI test infrastructure baseline (2026-03-05): Vitest ^4.0.18 with minimal config (only `test.include` set). Current: 2 tests, 333 lines, 0% coverage across 238 source files. Missing dependencies for expanded testing: jsdom, coverage config, @testing-library/react, test utilities.
Watchdog process supervision was removed and migrated to OS-level launchd in v0.3.2

gcode's Rust source codebase contains zero references to 'gobby'. gcode operates independently using its own SQLite storage, not gobby-hub.db. Full codebase search confirmed no integration between gcode and gobby-hub storage exists.

To kill a gobby agent: first run `gobby agents list` to get the RUN_REF (e.g., 'run-713d1038'), then use `gobby agents kill RUN_REF`. The `--session` flag does not exist; it requires the RUN_REF positional argument.

EnterPlanMode and ExitPlanMode are CLI-internal tools in the Claude Agent SDK. They bypass both the can_use_tool permission callback and SDK hooks (PreToolUse/PostToolUse). Neither PreToolUse nor PostToolUse hooks fire for these tools, so they cannot be intercepted via the normal hook system.
MemoryConfig class in src/gobby/config/persistence.py has temporal_decay_half_life_days field (default 30 days); setting to 0 disables decay

Proposed expansion redesign: two decoupled phases. Phase 1: Research Agent explores codebase, produces expansion spec via `save_expansion_spec` (constrained to spec authorship only). Phase 2: Mechanical Builder creates task tree deterministically via `execute_expansion` with no LLM involvement. Spec is the contract between phases; eliminates compaction-dependent task generation.

Production example scope for CLAUDE.md documentation: agents use default.yaml only, workflows use hello-world.yaml only, rules use auto-task/ only. Other examples in those directories are not considered production examples.

In Dart tree-sitter grammar, nested methods generate duplicate function_signature nodes (parent method def + nested signature). Dedup via seen_ids set prevents extracting same method multiple times during parsing.

In session_control.py:809, web chat soft-delete changes status from 'handoff_ready' to 'expired' because orphaned web chats won't be picked up by child sessions, making 'handoff_ready' semantically incorrect.

Each agent session has a unique session ID (format: alphanumeric hash, e.g., 427ce548..., f296f433...). Stop hook validation should filter tasks by session ID ownership rather than treating all project in_progress tasks as blocking constraints.

When close_task validation fails (e.g., 'Unable to access filesystem'), Gobby has a validation override mechanism that automatically routes the task to 'review' status instead of blocking closure. This is triggered implicitly—no special parameter needed. The response includes 'routed_to_review: true' and 'validation override' in the message.

Broken hook system from dispatcher removal: `.claude/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py` was removed in a recent codebase consolidation, but Claude Desktop still attempts to execute pre-tool-use hooks that reference this missing script. This breaks ALL tool execution (Bash, Glob) with repeated `hook_dispatcher.py` errors. Workaround: delete hooks via `/hooks` command in Claude Desktop, though this may need to be repeated.
ToolSearch tool fails consistently with PreToolUse hook errors in gobby context. Workaround: use mcp__gobby_* prefixed tools directly for gobby operations.

create_code_index_router function signature was intentionally preserved but internal logic gutted during MCP refactoring (commit 79c1dc895) to maintain API compatibility. The function still exports but is now invalidate-only. Do not remove entirely in future refactoring tasks.

Pipeline completion uses push-based event notification via `completion_registry.wait()` blocking, not polling. The executor blocks on this call and automatically notifies the session when dependent tasks complete. Do not implement periodic status polling.

before_tool rule priority table established with no conflicts: priority 5 (messaging/command-tool-restriction), priority 10 (block-claude-memory-{read,search,write}, handle-plan-mode-exit), priority 20 (block-edits-plan-mode), priority 21-22 (progressive-discovery). before_agent has handle-plan-mode-entry at priority 10.

After modifying MCP hook code in src/gobby/servers/routes/mcp/hooks.py, the Gobby daemon must be restarted for changes to take effect. Changes to hook code are not hot-reloaded. On macOS, restart via `launchctl kickstart -k ...`. This requirement is not obvious and would take 10+ minutes to debug through trial-and-error testing.

workflow_definitions table has FK constraint: project_id TEXT REFERENCES projects(id). SQLite FK enforcement is ON (database.py line 171). Tests using project-scoped workflows must create a real project via LocalProjectManager.create() first, then use project.id as project_id. Raw path strings like '/my/project' will cause IntegrityError.

Watchdog circuit breaker state is now persisted to ~/.gobby/watchdog_state.json. The _load_state()/_save_state() methods read/write restart_times as JSON. This means the circuit breaker survives watchdog process restarts — new watchdog instances inherit previous restart counts instead of starting fresh. Tests must mock get_gobby_home during Watchdog construction to avoid loading production state. failure_threshold default changed from 3 to 5.
sdk_compat.py has no dedicated test file. Test changes indirectly via streaming integration tests in claude_streaming.py and chat_session.py. Run full llm test suite to verify no regressions.

LLM generate_text max_tokens parameter: Added max_tokens parameter to LLMProvider.generate_text() abstract interface and all 4 implementations (claude, gemini, litellm, codex). For Claude SDK path, max_tokens can't be passed to ClaudeAgentOptions, so post-truncation is used (result[:max_tokens*4]). For LiteLLM/OpenAI paths, it's passed directly to acompletion/create. Default remains 8000 when not specified.

ClaudeExecutor in claude_executor.py has high-severity sync/async bridge deadlock risk when SDK calls tools from event loop thread. Do not modify tool execution flow without addressing this architectural issue.
.gobby/plans/ directory must exist in the filesystem before plan files can be written—write failures to this path occur at the I/O level when directory doesn't exist, not at the rules level

Upstream issue anthropics/claude-agent-sdk-python#634 filed for rate_limit_event parsing error in SDK v0.1.20. SDK's parse_message() raises MessageParseError on unknown message types, killing async generator.
C# language definitions use 'csharp' as the namespace key, not 'c_sharp'. This naming convention appears in multiple C# language definition files.

wait_for_completion is appropriate only for blocking waits on expansion pipelines when subtask IDs are needed before proceeding. Not appropriate for orchestrator-level runs where notifications handle sequencing automatically.

plan_mode variable (boolean 'are edits blocked?' flag) has 7 rule condition usages across 5 rule groups: block-edits-plan-mode, require-task-close, codex-review-gate, require-epic-tree-close, require-memory-review-before-status. Implemented as string literal in YAML/Python (not symbol reference) limiting automated blast-radius tooling.

EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode hooks don't fire, breaking rule dependencies: block-edits-plan-mode, handle-plan-mode-entry, and plan skill injection rules all depend on variables.get('plan_mode') which is never set because these hooks don't trigger. Root cause of cascading rule failures in plan-mode rule system.

Gobby's `pending_memory_review` pre-stop gate can be cleared via `set_variable(name="pending_memory_review", value=false, session_id="...")` to allow shutdown without creating redundant memories. Useful when memory review determines existing memory already covers the content.
The gobby call_tool proxy requires 'server_name' as a mandatory field. Tool calls without this parameter fail with validation error: 'Field required'.

`_generate_text_sdk` function fails with exit code 1 during background LLM operations (summary generation) with opaque error messages; Claude CLI stderr output is not captured in logs, limiting root cause analysis. Pattern: failures begin post-startup (~1-2 minutes after daemon initialization complete).

Claude Code now includes built-in worktree support via `isolation: "worktree"` flag and concurrent session orchestration with `--tmux`, plus experimental Agent Teams feature with shared task lists and P2P agent messaging. This directly overlaps Gobby's multi-agent orchestration layer functionality.

The refactoring plan addressed 3 core issues: (1) `safe_eval_expr` lacks docstring and example usage, (2) MCP registry lacks caching causing repeated instantiation, (3) Tmux death monitor polls every second in tight loop without async context. Issue #2 was confirmed by grep showing `get_default_registry()` called in observer detection loop.
`gobby agents kill` requires a RUN_REF parameter (not --session flag). Get RUN_REF from `gobby agents list` output (e.g., 'run-713d1038'). Correct syntax: `gobby agents kill RUN_REF`

_launchctl_bootout() in _macos_restart() (line 419) is fire-and-forget (asynchronous) and doesn't wait for launchd to fully unload the service domain entry. When bootstrap runs immediately after, launchd still sees the stale entry, triggering launchctl error 5 (I/O error).

Playwright tests for the gobby web UI: The Files page (useFiles.ts hook) now uses relative URLs through the Vite proxy (getBaseUrl returns ''). Use page.route('**/api/files/*') to intercept and mock API calls in tests. Navigation requires opening the sidebar first via .hamburger-button click, then clicking the .sidebar-item. The terminal-colors.spec.ts test is broken because it skips this step. Run Playwright tests from web/ directory: cd web && npx playwright test.

The mcp__gobby__close_task tool requires a `changes_summary` parameter. Attempting to close a task without it returns error `missing_changes_summary`. Schema must be inspected via mcp__gobby__get_tool_schema to discover this requirement.

Rules YAML editor save pattern: The web UI YAML editor sends `{ definition: {...} }` to `PUT /api/rules/{name}`. The `RuleUpdateRequest` model must accept this `definition` field, validate it with `RuleDefinitionBody`, extract row-level metadata (description, enabled, priority, tags) into update fields, strip non-body keys (name), and serialize the remainder as `definition_json`. This was fixed in commit c6e289cb.
Gobby hook enforcement on 'gcode index --stop' was triggered by --project context resolution failure; root cause identified and fixed in Task #11157

SQLite's `datetime('now')` returns UTC time, not local/system time. This affects `spans` table timestamps and any time-based queries filtering on `created_at`. The user observed traces only through 10PM because `datetime('now')` was returning UTC (22:00), not local time.
Gobby's create_task API has a bug where the depends_on parameter is inverted — specifying depends_on creates the dependency relationship in the wrong direction. Filed as bug #8929 with priority 1.

Variable functions in `src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/workflows/_variables.py` (`set_variable()`, `get_variable()`, `set_session_variable()`) are tested in `tests/mcp_proxy/test_top_level_variables.py` (integration) and `tests/mcp_proxy/tools/workflows/test_variables.py` (unit tests)—saves hunting for test files when modifying these functions.
hook_dispatcher.py enforces [require-error-triage] gate rule: task closure requires successful lint and test execution before stop is allowed. Affects workflow for all task completions.

User strongly objects to over-engineering rule deletion logic. Prefers inline soft-delete implementations at call site rather than creating separate functions or blocks for single-use deletion operations.

When calling gobby MCP tools via call_tool, the 'server_name' field is required in the arguments object. Omitting it causes a validation error: '2 validation errors for call_toolArguments - server_name Field required'. This is not obvious from tool descriptions and requires being passed as part of the arguments JSON.

Bundled content sync architecture (commit 7f7b319b): Five DB-synced content types — skills, prompts, rules, agents, workflows — live in src/gobby/install/shared/{type}/ and sync to SQLite via sync_bundled_content_to_db() in src/gobby/cli/installers/shared.py. This function accepts skip_types: set[str] | None to selectively block types. Dev mode auto-syncs on daemon startup in runner.py (after DB init, before PromptManager/SkillManager). Production mode uses git integrity verification (src/gobby/sync/integrity.py) checking git diff + ls-files on the shared content dirs. The reload_cache MCP tool in workflows/__init__.py now also re-syncs bundled workflows to DB by passing _db to reload_cache(). CLI command: gobby sync with --force/--verify-only/--type/--verbose.

Version numbers are maintained in two places: pyproject.toml (line 3) and src/gobby/__init__.py (line 9). The uv.lock file also contains the version but updates automatically via uv sync. The branch naming convention matches the version (e.g., branch 0.2.22 for version 0.2.22). Merge flow: version branch → dev → main.

In tool invocations, `cd` commands don't persist across separate shell operations. When working in subdirectories (e.g., web/), use `git -C <repo-root>` flag to execute git commands from the repository root instead of relying on cd for directory context.

Skill template frontmatter fields: name, description, category (e.g., 'core'), alwaysApply (boolean), injectionFormat, audience, format_overrides.autonomous. DB-installed skill records (e.g., skl-f0317007) preserve metadata structure exactly matching template source.
Gobby v0.2.25 baseline test coverage: 4 Playwright integration specs. Vitest suite has grown to 25 test files / 352 tests / ~11% global coverage (see memory ac041d31 for details).

Skills refactor — finalized sync model (2026-04-02):

Templates are install artifacts, not runtime sources. DB is always source of truth.

- First install: templates seed DB as source='installed', enabled=True
- Production upgrade: gobby-tagged DB rows updated from new templates (we own them)
- Production: templates are protected/read-only after install
- Dev mode: DB → templates sync (overwrite SKILL.md from DB) so we can maintain templates in source control
- User skills: never gobby-tagged, never touched by any sync
- Drift detection: compares DB against templates, informational
- install_from_template eliminated — bundled skills are directly installed
- project_id scoping stays (already works, no reason to remove)
- SkillTemplatesMixin removed (no more template/installed split in DB)

Bundled items as hidden templates feature: Bundled pipelines/agents/rules (source='bundled' in workflow_definitions table) are now hidden by default in the Workflows UI with a 'Show bundled' toggle. Bundled items never have enable/disable toggles. In dev mode (detected via /admin/status dev_mode field, backed by is_dev_mode()), bundled items get full CRUD. In non-dev mode, bundled items are read-only with 'Use as Template' (creates a custom copy, disables bundled original) and 'Download YAML' actions.

Key backend additions:
- workflow_definitions.py: use_as_template() and use_all_bundled_as_templates() methods
- workflow_definitions.py: get_by_name() now has ORDER BY to prefer custom over bundled
- admin.py: status response includes dev_mode field
- workflows.py: POST /{id}/use-as-template and POST /use-all-bundled-as-templates endpoints
- agents.py: POST /definitions/{name}/use-as-template endpoint

Frontend: showBundled state + devMode fetch from /admin/status in WorkflowsPage.tsx, threaded to RulesTab and AgentDefinitionsPage. CSS: .workflows-card--bundled (dashed border, 0.75 opacity), .workflows-card-badge--template.

`test_cli_events_simulator_works` fails in CI with `httpx.ReadError: [Errno 104] Connection reset by peer` due to flaky daemon connection. Test halts entire pipeline before publish step. Needs stabilization. (Encountered v0.2.24 Run 22649486734)
In gobby, migration versions should not be hardcoded in plans or code — use dynamic version detection instead. The user rejected a plan that referenced a specific migration version (v112).

Pipelines are orchestration frameworks without independent reasoning capability. When an agent with `_assigned_pipeline` starts and `session_start` rule fires `run_pipeline` via mcp_call, the pipeline executes server-side. Intelligence comes from spawned agents or direct tool calls, not from the pipeline itself

Neo4j service in services-neo4j-1 creates an anonymous volume for /logs due to the base Neo4j Dockerfile declaring VOLUME /logs without explicit mapping in docker-compose.yml. This is a common Docker gotcha with third-party images. Prevention: Add explicit named volume mapping for /logs in docker-compose.yml template for Neo4j service to replace implicit anonymous volumes with managed named volumes, enabling explicit cleanup and volume reuse.

Claude Code hook event field names differ between session-start and session-end: session-start uses 'source' (values: 'clear', 'compact', 'startup'), session-end uses 'reason' (values: 'clear', 'logout', 'prompt_input_exit', 'other'). The hook_dispatcher.py confirms this at lines 373-375. The normalization layer (normalization.py) does NOT map between these fields — it only handles tool-related fields.
task-transitions skill refactored to use direct set_variable() call instead of call_tool('gobby') invocation pattern
Windows: `gobby service install` command is not yet implemented. Only macOS (launchd plist generation) and Linux (systemd unit file generation) are implemented.

E2E tests and production daemon don't conflict on ports (E2E uses `find_free_port()`). The actual issue is false-positive orphan detection: `_cleanup_orphan_gobby_processes` in `runner.py` (around line 952) can misidentify the E2E test daemon as an orphan and kill it, or vice versa. The guard logic is in `hook_manager.py` lines 296-303.

Pipeline error handling: non-zero exit codes from pipeline_executor.py now raise RuntimeError with JSON stdout parsing for structured data. Errors propagate through fail_pipeline() guard tool (returns {success: false, error: msg}) rather than uncaught exceptions. This pattern applied across orchestrator.yaml, coordinator.yaml, expand-task.yaml, command-listener.yaml.

The Skills page UI was converted from a table/row layout (SkillsTable.tsx) to a card grid layout (SkillsGrid.tsx) matching the Rules page pattern. Key reusable CSS classes from WorkflowsPage.css: .workflows-grid, .workflows-card--template, .workflows-card-badges, .workflows-card-type, .workflows-card-footer, .workflows-toggle*, .workflows-card-actions, .workflows-action-btn/icon. The skill type variant uses .workflows-card-type--skill (sky-blue #38bdf8 on #1a2a3a). Skills now surface source taxonomy (template/installed/project), soft deletes with restore, and scope management (move-to-project/move-to-global) in the UI. The useSkills hook exposes installFromTemplate, moveToProject, moveToGlobal, restoreSkill actions. Note: the backend /skills route does NOT yet accept include_deleted param (only include_templates); the storage layer supports it but the API endpoint needs updating.

The `close_task` tool in gobby requires a `commit_summary` parameter (not `changes_summary`); omitting it returns the error `missing_changes_summary`. Note the mismatch between parameter name and error message. This applies to all closes, including administrative ones.

Session registration on gobby-sessions service lacks register_session MCP tool. Currently works only via SessionStart hooks in daemon's internal lifecycle, blocking hookless clients (Antigravity). Solution: add MCP tool accepting session metadata, returning session ID, to bypass hook-based pathway.

Summarizer removal requires coordinated changes across: code_index/summarizer.py (main module), code_index/maintenance.py, code_index/indexer.py, runner_init.py, runner_lifecycle.py (integration points), summary_enabled flag on code index config object (not app.py), and summary column in code index database schema.

Uninstall CLI tests (TestUninstallCommand) that test --all or default behavior must monkeypatch Path.home() to a fake_home directory. The uninstall code checks Path.home() for global paths, not CWD. Pattern: fake_home = temp_dir / 'home'; monkeypatch.setattr(Path, 'home', lambda: fake_home); then create .claude/.gemini dirs under fake_home. See tests/cli/test_cli_install.py.

In Gobby skills, attributes are accessed via getter methods, not direct properties. Example: `category` is accessed via `get_category()` method, not as a direct attribute. Verify method-based access pattern when debugging skill attribute access issues in test scripts.

Session_id requires full UUID in tool calls; task_id accepts shorthand (#N). Root cause: task tools invoke via gobby-tasks MCP tool which calls resolve_task_reference() with built-in resolution. Session_id goes through different hook path without shorthand resolution.

Baseline v133 (set in _MIN_MIGRATION_VERSION in migrations.py) guarantees auth_sessions table existence. The _ensure_table() method was removed from auth.py because table creation is no longer needed at runtime—baseline migration initialization provides this guarantee. This eliminates the need for runtime table-creation guards.

Never describe an approach as "simplest" or optimize for simplicity. Always choose and present the best, most correct, most complete fix. Rank by correctness and completeness first. Don't mention simplicity as a virtue. If an approach happens to be simple, frame it as "correct" or "complete", not "simple".

The mcp__gobby call_tool requires a server_name field as a required parameter. Calls without this field fail with Pydantic validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'. This is a required parameter that must be included when invoking call_tool to make proxied tool calls.

SessionVariableManager in test mocking: use fully qualified patch target `gobby.workflows.state_manager.SessionVariableManager` (patch where used) not where imported, because the class is lazily imported inside method bodies, not at module level. Applied in test_session_events_coverage.py.
Session summary write call sites are in hook_manager.py (_dispatch_session_summaries()) and lifecycle.py - modify both when changing summary behavior
DefinitionSource Literal type constrains sources to: installed, agent, project, custom
tree-sitter package must be separately installed in the uv tool install Python environment; it is not automatically included and will cause ImportError if missing when code_index is used

Code index maintenance loop executes every 300 seconds (5 minutes). Symbol summarization is triggered during this loop execution, not immediately after startup or migration completion. First execution occurs approximately 5 minutes after loop initialization.
Verified Gobby hooks in system: SessionStart, SessionEnd, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, Stop (6 total) - remained intact after Codex plugin installation

MCP-proxied gobby tools (via mcp__ prefix) consistently fail with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors across multiple tool types (reopen_task, kill_agent, etc.). Use Bash CLI commands directly instead (e.g., 'gobby tasks reopen TASK_ID', 'gobby agents list', 'gobby agents kill RUN_REF') - this works reliably.

Vite dev server (web/vite.config.ts) requires `host: '0.0.0.0'` to be accessible over Tailscale. Without it, Vite defaults to localhost-only binding. The host is now configurable via GOBBY_UI_HOST env var (default: localhost). spawn_ui_server in cli/utils.py sets this env var from config.ui.host. For Tailscale access: set ui.host: 0.0.0.0 in config.yaml. IMPORTANT: Vite 6 also enforces allowedHosts checks when host is 0.0.0.0. The config now sets allowedHosts: true when bound to 0.0.0.0 to allow raw Tailscale IPs (100.x.y.z). Without this, Vite returns a 403/blank page for hosts not matching the allowlist.

Internal LLM validation (task_validator.validate_task() at src/gobby/storage/ line 212) executes mandatorily on every task close. External validation (run_external_validation() line 238) only executes when TaskValidationConfig.use_external_validator=True, which has never been enabled in practice.

Test battery skill instructions like 'walk through each section' and 'report results as you go' are being misinterpreted as advisory (requiring confirmation pauses) rather than directive (automatic execution). Root cause: procedural language in skill text is not compelling agent execution flow correctly. Fix direction: either add explicit 'proceed without confirmation' language to skill instructions, or review how skill text drives agent execution behavior without user gates.

gobby-code index tools (get_file_tree, get_file_outline, get_symbol, search_symbols, search_text, get_imports, summarize_symbol, project_summary) are MCP-backed and complementary to existing CLI tools (Read, Grep, Glob, LSP)—not replacements. They provide structured symbol-aware search that unstructured grep cannot deliver (e.g., 'find all classes implementing interface X').

invoke_pipeline step returns wrapper object {pipeline, execution_id, status} but does NOT unwrap child pipeline's outputs into parent step's output field. Child pipeline's actual outputs (e.g., orchestration_complete: True) exist only in child execution record, not accessible via parent.output.field references. This breaks orchestrator conditions like 'dev_loop.output.orchestration_complete' - the field doesn't exist in wrapper.

LiteLLMProvider in llm/litellm.py sets os.environ from config api_keys. Previously had 'if value and key not in os.environ' guard which meant env vars always won over config. Changed to unconditionally set from config since config is now the source of truth (resolved from secrets store > env vars). This ensures secrets-store priority propagates to LiteLLM.

hook_dispatcher.py --cli=claude --type=stop command has [require-task-close] rule that incorrectly flags in_progress tasks from other sessions as belonging to the current session; hook auto-retries automatically; issue resolves when current session's own tasks are properly closed

Launchctl error 5 (Input/output error) specifically indicates attempt to bootstrap a domain entry that still exists in the shutdown sequence. The fallback restart mechanism succeeds because it bypasses launchctl and directly terminates the process.

WorkflowHookHandler.handle() now delegates directly to evaluate() (rule engine). The step-based workflow engine (engine.handle_event) is dead code — 0 workflows in DB use it. Before this fix, handle() ran evaluate() then ran the dead engine and returned its empty response, discarding rule metadata including mcp_call effects. This caused title synthesis and other mcp_call-based features to silently fail.

Event handlers (_agent.py, _tool.py, _session.py) no longer call _evaluate_workflows() — rules are evaluated once in hook_manager.handle() via workflow_handler.handle(), not twice. The _apply_debug_echo() method no longer takes a wf_response parameter; it reads debug_echo_context from WorkflowConfig only.

In `cli/utils.py` and `daemon_control.py`, the `write_shutdown_source` isolation pattern ensures SIGTERM always triggers graceful shutdown. Apply this pattern in both files for reliable signal handling.

set_variable and get_variable are top-level proxy tools (mcp__gobby__set_variable, mcp__gobby__get_variable) not invoked through call_tool wrapper, so require-schema-before-call YAML rule doesn't affect them—this rule only fires on call_tool operations. This explains why these tools bypass schema lookup requirements.

User preference: workflow filter UI should use a dropdown/select element, not chips/buttons. Chips take up too much space. The dropdown should match the height of the search box (same padding, font-size, border styles).

Rule selection uses single mechanism: agents compose via `rule_selectors: {include: ["tag:..."]}`  OR automatic `agent_scope` matching (rules declare `agent_scope: [agent_type]` to restrict usage). Reject multiple selection paths to prevent design failures. Core principle: all rules are standalone templates selected through one mechanism.
storage/pipelines.py has reset_steps_from(step) method that resets specified step AND all subsequent steps to PENDING state, clearing output, error, timestamps, and approval fields

Task closure has a multi-level gate checking for linked commits. Errors: 'no_commits_linked' (edit has no commit), 'missing_commits_for_edits' (prior session edits lack matching commits). If all attempts to close fail, delete the problematic task via mcp__gobby_delete_task to clear the gate, then close remaining tasks.

Cron jobs store their action configuration in the cron_jobs.action_config field (JSON). The developer pipeline is controlled by the 'developer_pipeline' key within this JSON (e.g., {"developer_pipeline": "gemini"}). Query and edit the database directly to modify which developer a cron job spawns.

Tests pass session_id as function arguments, but create_task() now reads from ContextVar after refactoring. Tests must set context via ContextVar instead of passing session_id as kwargs; unknown kwargs are stripped in internal.py.

POST /admin/restart endpoint added to admin.py (src/gobby/servers/routes/admin.py). Pattern: spawns a detached subprocess with inline Python script that waits for current PID to exit, then starts `python -m gobby.runner`. Uses same _process_shutdown() as /admin/shutdown. Has a nonlocal _restart_in_progress guard against double-restart. The restarter script handles PID file cleanup, port release wait, and log file redirection. No `signal` import needed at module level — only used inside the inline script string.

When calling call_tool via MCP, always use the 'arguments' parameter (not 'args') for passing inner tool arguments. The rule engine's argument unwrapping (rule_engine.py:547) only checks raw_tool_input.get('arguments'), not 'args'. Using 'args' means rule conditions like tool_input.get('commit_sha') can't see inner arguments, causing false blocks from rules like require-commit-before-close.
traces_enabled configuration in telemetry/config.py previously defaulted to False, preventing spans from being collected or stored. Default was changed to True to fix the tracing system.
Gobby's task data is stored in the file .gobby/tasks.jsonl.

Rules in src/gobby/install/shared/rules/ are templates with enabled: false by default. They get synced/installed to the database where they're actually enabled. Don't modify the install folder to enable rules — they're already installed and enabled in the DB.

Frontend test coverage baseline: 25 test files covering 212 source files in gobby/web/src (~11% coverage vs 80% target). Tests located in __tests__ subdirectories alongside source files. Would require running grep/find to rediscover.

In gobby, the required workflow sequence is: clear the memory gate (clear_memory_gate / set pending_memory_review=false) before calling close_task(). Memory gate must be cleared before task closure, not just before session stop.

Bug fix #9071: Stop hook fails to block when agent has claimed task. Root cause: dual-write desync between workflow_states and session_variables. claim_task/create_task wrote task_claimed=True to workflow_states only, but the rule engine evaluates session_variables with higher precedence. If session_variables has stale task_claimed=false from init-task-claimed at session start, it overrides the truth. Fix: mirror writes to session_variables via ctx.session_var_manager.merge_variables() in claim_task, close_task, and create_task(claim=True). Also added SessionVariableManager to RegistryContext in _context.py. Secondary fix: removed [:8] truncated UUID usage in 5 places across task tools (_lifecycle.py, _crud.py, _session.py, _expansion.py).

The call_tool function requires a 'server_name' parameter when invoking gobby tools. Omitting it causes validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]' for the server_name field. This is mandatory to successfully call any gobby-specific tools.

Git commit messages containing angle brackets (< >) in co-author attribution lines fail with shell parsing errors when passed directly to shell commands. Workaround: use file-based commit method - write commit message to file and use `git commit -F filename` to bypass shell interpretation of special characters.

Legacy agent workflow and rule definition files preserved in `deprecated/` directory (not deleted) for backward compatibility; agents (Developer, QA-reviewer, merge agents) now directly own execution steps

The mcp__gobby_call_tool function has strict parameter validation requiring 'server_name' as a mandatory field. Multiple validation errors occurred when this parameter was omitted or when using incorrect parameter names (errors: 'Invalid arguments: [Unknown parameter...]' and 'Field required' for server_name).

When deploying code fixes across multiple branches (dev, main, release), isolate version number changes to the release branch (0.2.25) only to prevent version drift between development and release tracks. Cherry-pick or merge the fix commits to all branches, but keep version increments in the release branch only.

In _process_pending_transcripts: check for missing transcript files in expired sessions after step 1. If session is expired AND transcript file is missing, mark session as processed and skip further retries to prevent orphaned sessions from deferring indefinitely.

The `arguments` parameter in `call_tool` must be valid JSON format, not URL-encoded query parameters. Passing "task_id=#10454&session=..." style parameters fails with "Invalid JSON in 'arguments' parameter" error (Turn 42).

Codex notify integration has a field name mapping bug: Codex's external notify payload uses kebab-case field names (e.g., `workspace-id`, `request-id`, `task-id`, `event-type`, `timestamp`, `trigger-type`) but gobby's `_normalize_input_data()` function in the Codex integration expects snake_case, causing 5 out of 6 fields to fail mapping. The fix requires updating the mapping function to convert kebab-case to snake_case.

When hook dispatcher blocks a tool (via `Do not stop` feedback), Claude Code silently suppresses subsequent Read/Write tool calls with `<tool_use_error>Sibling tool call errored</tool_use_error>`. Workaround: abandon the blocked operation and start fresh, or use Bash as an alternative when Read/Write are blocked.

The _personal project name is mapped to 'Personal' in App.tsx (line 86). This causes UI confusion when components use project names as labels - any toggle or display showing 'Personal' may collide with the _personal project name. The ChatInput scope toggle was redesigned to use fixed 'Personal'/'Project' labels with a search dropdown instead of showing project names directly.
Current gobby index . implementation unnecessarily requires daemon via HTTP API call; this is technical debt from MCP-to-CLI migration, not a fundamental requirement

The project migrated from in-memory to database-backed prompt storage (commit b3d4deac, task #8352). This architectural change affected test files in src/gobby/workflows/state_actions.py and required updating test expectations for database-backed storage mechanisms (task #8353).

Gobby MCP tool wrapper (mcp__gobby_*) fails with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown par[ameter]' errors during tool execution. Reliable workaround: use Bash tool with direct gobby CLI commands (e.g., `gobby tasks reopen <task_id>`, `gobby agents list`, `gobby agents kill <RUN_REF>`). The CLI works consistently while the MCP proxy layer has argument passing issues.

runner.py contains startup recovery logic for interrupted subscribers. If a subscriber's execution was interrupted, the system recovers on startup. This is critical for reliability of the wake delivery system.
nomic-embed-text-v1.5 can be packaged as part of an installation, making it more portable than NV-Embed-v2.

When calling mark_task_review_approved for status transitions that trigger workflow validation, use dispatch=true AND commit_hash parameters together. Omitting either causes validation failures ("Critical validation failed" or "skip_validation_with_commit" errors). The commit_hash must be provided alongside dispatch=true to satisfy validation constraints.

tree-sitter-language-pack language keys: C# is `csharp` (not `c_sharp`). Dart's `method_signature` node has no `name` field — query methods via `(method_signature (function_signature (identifier) @name))`. Query execution uses `QueryCursor(query).matches(node)`, not methods on the Query object.
Gobby's memory system accepts the higher token cost of raw injection in favor of lower latency.

When merging branches (e.g., 0.2.15 → dev → main), local changes to .gobby/memories.jsonl and .gobby/tasks/*.output files must be stashed before switching branches, as git checkout will fail with 'Your local changes would be overwritten by checkout' error. After merging, restore the stash on the working branch.

In gobby, the session_variables table stores variable values alongside their type (e.g., value='0', type='integer'). When bulk-updating session variables directly in SQLite, changes won't take effect until the workflow cache is explicitly cleared via the MCP tool (mcp__gobby_ with cache clear action).

Three confirmed messaging system gaps: (D5) send_message doesn't piggyback undelivered messages—web needs to fetch them separately; (D6) Web chat UI doesn't exist for non-agent users—UI layer missing entirely; (D7) No persistent queue for user→agent messages—backend/schema gap, likely needs table like agent_message but for reverse direction.

The call_tool MCP proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter as a mandatory field. Omitting it causes validation error: '2 validation errors for call_toolArguments - server_name: Field required [type=missing]'.

Gobby's [require-task-close] rule (enforced in hook_dispatcher.py) blocks workflow stop/completion until task is explicitly closed. After committing with [gobby-#NNNN] format, must call close_task() to change status from in_progress to closed before the stop operation succeeds.
_manager.py is the primary API surface of the tasks package with 35 symbols—significantly larger than all other submodules (which range from 2–9 symbols each).
Killing the tmux window sends SIGHUP to the gobby daemon but does not terminate the session. The daemon handles SIGHUP gracefully and the session persists, allowing reconnection without state loss.

YAML effect: vs effects: normalization is now DONE — all YAML templates use plural `effects:`. The singular form is only retained as a backward-compat model_validator for old DB records. No longer a won't-fix cosmetic item.
IdentityManager extracted to gobby/communications/identities.py and ThreadManager to gobby/communications/threads.py (not identity.py and thread_mapper.py as originally named in task #10954)

TemplateHashCache class in workflows/template_hashes.py implements type-aware serialization to match specific sync function requirements—this design is intentional and required for correct hash computation.

Pre-existing test failure in bundled content sync tests: `assert 21 == 19` occurs when bundled variables are added without updating the test count assertion. Pattern: when adding bundled variables to bundled content, sync test assertions that verify expected counts must be synchronized.

Gobby UI dev server auto-start: GobbyRunner.run() now spawns Vite via spawn_ui_server when ui.enabled=true and ui.mode=dev, and stops it on shutdown. Port lineup: 60887 (HTTP daemon), 60888 (WebSocket), 60889 (Vite dev). Config default is UIConfig.port=60889. The spawn/stop helpers live in gobby.cli.utils (spawn_ui_server, stop_ui_server, find_web_dir).
memories.jsonl format: all tag entries must be JSON arrays, not strings. Had 8 malformed entries with string tags that needed conversion to valid format during last cleanup.

Gobby's rules system enforces a limit of one block effect (action block) per rule. Attempting multiple block effects (e.g., multiple state transitions) in a single rule triggers a tool-failure-recovery error.

LLM model routing map (as of 2026-02-23): Haiku is used for: session title synthesis (SessionTitleConfig), memory extraction (MemoryExtractionConfig), skill description (SkillDescriptionConfig), digest generation (DigestConfig), tool summarization, task description, MCP server import, memory fact/dedup/entity extraction. Sonnet is used for: merge conflict resolution (MergeResolutionConfig), session summary, tool recommendations. Opus is used for: code review (ReviewConfig), chat (ChatConfig). All are configurable via DaemonConfig fields.

To kill a gobby agent process: First run `gobby agents list` to discover the RUN_REF (format: run-XXXXX), then use `gobby agents kill RUN_REF`. The command does NOT accept --session flags or session IDs—RUN_REF from the list output is required.
gobby project uses mcp__gobby_* endpoint pattern for MCP tool access - tools like claim_task, get_task can be discovered via mcp__gobby_ calls and are reliable even when ToolSearch hook fails.
Neo4jClient is reusable across different entity labels without modification - code entity separation can leverage Neo4j label abstraction as viable path without Neo4jClient refactoring

FastAPI test patterns: 1) Lazy isinstance checks with MagicMock need autouse fixture patching the class. 2) Test dirs named after packages (mcp/) cause import conflicts. 3) Depends-based routes use dependency_overrides, not create_X_router. 4) Generic except handlers re-wrap HTTPExceptions as 500. 5) Installer backup runs before read.

Claude model ID migration (Feb 2026): claude-sonnet-4-5 → claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-opus-4-5 → claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-20250514 → claude-sonnet-4-6. Old IDs return empty API responses causing 'Empty response from LLM' errors. Must update both source code defaults AND DB config_store values (config_store overrides Pydantic defaults). Use gobby-config get_config/set_config to check and update DB values. Key DB keys: recommend_tools.model, llm_providers.claude.models. Validation/expansion models may not be in DB if never explicitly set (they use Pydantic defaults).

Pack command help text in pack.py mentions only Neo4j Docker volume support ('optionally Docker volume data (Neo4j)') but implementation actually exports both gobby_neo4j_data and gobby_qdrant_data volumes. Help text needs update to mention Qdrant as well.

Planning agent at stage 3a IS the expansion agent (not separate concepts). Planning is expansion at higher fidelity with `affected_files` context. This unifies previously separate conceptual concerns and clarifies architectural relationships.

Memory synthesis in gobby: Memories are injected as raw content directly to agents, not pre-synthesized by a separate LLM. Attempting to use Sonnet for synthesis was rejected due to latency concerns—the system accepts the token cost of raw injection over speed, implemented as a blocking before_agent call.

User stated that compression logic is intentional in codebase and was being bypassed by agent. Conversation ended before clarification of what compression mechanism this refers to or its location. Needs investigation in next session—ask user to specify which compression feature was meant.

Canvas skill auto-injection is broken for web UI sessions.
