In rule_engine.py, tool_block_pending clearing behavior changed: previously required the same blocked tool to succeed before clearing block state; now unconditionally clears on ANY successful AFTER_TOOL execution. Changes applied to both early-return path (~line 220) and main evaluation path (~line 250).
When adding services/ to PACK_DIRS in pack.py, must explicitly exclude data directories (services/qdrant/, services/neo4j/, etc.) to prevent double-packing live Docker volume bind-mounts.

Assistant messages with only tool calls (no content) serialize with content: null via model_dump(), breaking strict Jinja templates in LMStudio. Root cause: LLM Executor message construction + MCP Tool Results to_dict() methods including nullable fields
Take proper care with every task. Don't rush or try to burn through tasks. Rely on the Gobby daemon to manage context (compaction, session persistence). Quality over speed.

A Go CLI strangler fig migration feasibility study was completed and written to docs/research/go-cli-strangler-fig.md (#8893). Key findings: ~60 thin client commands (HTTP to daemon) are trivial to port, ~30 local logic commands need real reimplementation. install/uninstall is the hardest (~1000 LOC). The CLI communicates with the daemon via DaemonClient (src/gobby/utils/daemon_client.py) using httpx to localhost:60887. Phase 2 (thin clients) and Phase 3 (local logic) can run in parallel — both only depend on the Phase 1 scaffold. The user prefers not to see arbitrary human-time estimates in plans when agents will do the work.

Daemon-loaded tools in gobby-workflows require daemon restart to appear in tool list. New tools (like pipeline and agent definition tools) won't show up in tool list retrieved via API until the daemon process is restarted—this is expected behavior, not a bug.

Query tools search_text and get_symbol have no staleness detection mechanism—results appear valid without any validity indicator when index is stale. search_symbols now has staleness detection via file hash comparison, returning {"status": "stale"} responses.

`.gobby/project.json` contains a `verification` section with four auto-detected commands: lint (`uv run ruff check src/`), format (`uv run ruff format --check src/`), type_check (`uv run mypy src/`), unit_tests (`uv run pytest tests/ -x -q`)

gobby issue #8405 fix: pipeline CRUD wrappers lacked type filtering, causing incorrect results when multiple entity types existed. Fixed by adding type filtering to the wrappers. Commit b1d67273, version 0.2.18, all 33/33 tests passing.

AgentDefinitionManager methods (delete, create, update) each manage their own database transactions using `self.db.transaction()` context manager. The `import_from_definition` method in sync.py calls `delete()` then `create()`, resulting in two separate transactions rather than a single atomic operation.

In gobby's `update_step_execution` DB method, `output_json` and `error` fields use `if value is not None` guards, meaning you cannot clear/reset them to NULL once set — passing `None` is a no-op, not a reset. Step retry/reset logic must account for this by using explicit SQL SET to NULL.

tmux `set-titles` is disabled globally in the environment (tmux 3.6a shows 'set-titles off'). This prevents automatic window title updates via escape sequences. To enable dynamic titles, need to explicitly set `set-titles on` in tmux config or per-session.

Priority conflict warning in Rules UI: `RulesTab.tsx` uses `useMemo` to compute `conflictWarning` string when current rule's event+priority combination matches an existing rule. Prop passed to `RuleEditForm`, which renders yellow warning banner below priority field using `--color-warning` design token.

The 'template' source row in Gobby's workflow_definitions table is read-only and protected from API modifications. When a rule fails with 'template protection' errors via the API, the 'installed' row must be modified directly instead.

PLANNING RULE: Never write 'Check schema during implementation' or similar deferred-lookup phrases in plans. Always look up tool schemas, API signatures, and other technical details DURING planning. Information gathered during planning prevents wrong assumptions during execution. Plans are for execution, not exploration.

The 'arguments' parameter in call_tool must be valid JSON. Passing URL-encoded or non-JSON formatted strings (e.g., 'task_id=#10454&session=...') fails with 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter' error. Arguments must be properly formatted as a JSON string.
The `de_escalate` function is referenced in multiple locations within the codebase, such as in `task_validation.py:452`, and is implemented in both the MCP tool and as an HTTP route.

The compress-bash-output rule template in settings.json has `enabled: false` but CANNOT be removed—the CLI handler checks this disabled state and auto-enables it as a fallback mechanism when needed. Attempting to delete it will break the fallback behavior.

gobby MCP tool parameters must use flattened structure at the top level, not nested within parent objects. Attempting nested parameter structures results in repeated 'Unknown parameter' validation errors.

memory-review-gate re-trigger bug: track-pending-memory-review used to fire on both file edits AND close_task, causing the gate to re-arm after already being cleared. Fixed by removing close_task from the when clause. The suggest-memory-after-close rule remains as a soft nudge (no gate re-arm). memory-capture-nudge was also updated with anti-speculation guidance: don't generalize one-time instructions, use the 5-minute rule.
LiteLLM dependency removal is deferred until after v3.2 release. Current strategy: release v3.2 with LiteLLM intact, then schedule removal work for the next development cycle as a breaking change.

Rule synchronization bug: the rule sync pipeline does not persist `agent_scope` from YAML template definitions into the `definition_json` column in the database. This causes installed rule copies to lose scope information, resulting in rules applying too broadly (e.g., `no-push-for-workers` was blocking all agents instead of only `["developer", "qa-reviewer"]`). Requires investigation in the rule sync pipeline.
For RTK-Gobby integration, adding modify_output effect to Gobby's rule engine (option 3) is most viable: RTK's pattern set is small/well-defined, making integration practical vs porting legacy code.

MCP field normalization has a specific gotcha: when parsing string tool_output values to dictionaries, existing tool_output dict values must NOT be overwritten. This behavior is enforced by test_existing_tool_output_not_overwritten in test_normalization.py—finding this constraint through testing would take 5-10 minutes of debugging.

tool_block_pending clearing logic must maintain scope awareness - only clear when the same tool that created the block completes, not when ANY tool succeeds. Regression pattern: check scope matching in block clearing conditions to prevent premature gate clearing.
`accepted_by_user` reset logic was removed from the `update_task()` function in `_crud.py` (lines 376-391) during dead code cleanup. The field no longer resets on task updates.

MCP gobby tool proxies (mcp__gobby_* tools like reopen_task, kill_agent, list_agents) consistently fail with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors and should not be used. Always use Bash CLI directly instead. Key commands: `gobby tasks reopen TASK_ID`, `gobby agents list`, `gobby agents kill RUN_REF` (obtain RUN_REF from `gobby agents list`). Use `--help` for usage info. Always prefer Bash CLI as primary path for all gobby commands including pipelines.

The gobby `call_tool` function requires a `server_name` parameter in its arguments. Calls fail with validation error 'server_name: Field required' if omitted. This is not immediately obvious from error messages about 'Unknown parameters' that appear with malformed calls.

Template YAML files with `enabled: true` cause installed copies to be auto-enabled when `install_all_templates()` runs. Template YAML should always have `enabled: false` to prevent future installs from being auto-enabled. This is inheritance behavior in src/gobby/storage/migrations.py or relevant install code.

MCP gobby tools `get_config` and `set_config` are available for programmatic config management. Both accept dotted key paths (e.g., `neo4j.enabled`, `voice.provider`) and return/accept config values directly.

ON CONFLICT clause in code index database updates content_hash but does not clear summary column when symbols are re-indexed. Query get_symbols_without_summaries only detects symbols where summary IS NULL, missing stale summaries created when symbol content changes. This pattern creates orphaned summary data.

To close a gobby task, call `mcp__gobby__close_task` (via gobby-tasks MCP server) with `commit_sha` and task number. The tool is named `close_task` and returns `{"success":true,"result":{}}` on success.

Template/installed dual-row paradigm has architectural issues: (1) get_by_name function has problematic shadow resolution logic, (2) propagation logic scattered across sync functions, (3) include_templates parameter proliferated throughout codebase. Single-paradigm simplification proposed as alternative approach.

MCP tools (reopen_task, kill_agent) fail with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors due to call_tool proxy issues. Workaround: use corresponding CLI commands instead ('gobby tasks reopen #<TASK_ID>' and 'gobby agents kill <RUN_REF>'). CLI commands execute successfully while MCP calls consistently fail.

Context window usage calculation: The context window (e.g. 200k for Claude) is an INPUT limit. Output tokens do NOT occupy it. Correct formula: context_load = input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens. Percentage = context_load / context_window. Output tokens should be shown separately in tooltips but never added to the numerator. The DB field usage_input_tokens stores the total (uncached + cache components), so when hydrating uncachedInputTokens for display, compute: total - cache_read - cache_creation.

Neo4j auth flow: `_resolve_neo4j_auth()` (line 33) generates random password via `secrets.token_urlsafe()` or reads existing config. Install stores password at `databases.neo4j.auth` in gobby config (line 136). Daemon restart (daemon.py:77-80) reads stored auth from config and re-exports as `GOBBY_NEO4J_PASSWORD` env var to docker compose.

Bmad→Gobby mapping: bmad personas map to gobby agents (preamble + optional skills/rules); bmad step workflows map to gobby agents with sequential steps. This mapping is the basis for bmad as gobby plugin candidate.

The mcp__gobby_call_tool proxy requires a mandatory 'server_name' parameter. Validation error occurs if omitted: '2 validation errors for call_toolArguments server_name Field required'. This field is not optional despite other parameters potentially working.

Test fixtures in gobby: protect_production_resources fixture patches load_config in already-imported modules (e.g., gobby.runner, gobby.mcp_proxy.stdio) to use safe_load_config with test DB paths. Must be added to test signature to ensure safe DB is used when instantiating GobbyRunner.
The .gobby/tasks.jsonl file contains approximately 6805 entries as of March 2026.

gobby project has a legacy chat UI with ~12K lines of CSS that is NOT being migrated. Chat v2 was built fresh using Tailwind CSS. The Sessions tab and Terminals tab are being deprecated in favor of the Chat page, which is now the primary UI.

In gobby's memory system, `memory_recall_relevant()` function in `memory.py` is async (line 131), but source code callers in `claude_code.py` invoke it without await, preventing memory retrieval from working during agent context injection workflows.
User preference: Always choose the proper fix over the quick and dirty fix. If it wouldn't pass a code review, don't write it.

Windows service support should be implemented by extending `src/gobby/cli/installers/service.py` with a new Windows backend alongside existing macOS/Linux paths. The existing conditional path handling in service.py provides the pattern template.
Grep block rule moved to deprecated/ directory; deprecated by inject-code-index-skill nudge

Git hook in hook_dispatcher.py enforces require-task-close rule: commits are blocked if any task remains in 'in_progress' status. Tasks must be explicitly closed via close_task() before commits can proceed.

Version bumps in gobby require updating both `/pyproject.toml` (in the `[project]` section) and `/src/gobby/__init__.py` (the `__version__` variable). The commit format follows the pattern `[gobby-#<task_id>] chore: bump version to <version>`.

Session #1760 summary: Implemented 3 commits on branch 0.2.21. (1) ab65468e [#9046]: Chat UI fixes - stable ChatInput button container (Send left, Stop right), ArtifactTextView Edit/View toggle with CodeMirror, PlanApprovalBar in ArtifactPanel, plan artifacts as type 'text'. (2) 1054cc44 [#9047]: Fixed horizontal scrollbar in chat by adding overflow-x-hidden to MessageList ScrollArea. (3) 055aeeab [#9048]: Fixed PlanApprovalBar never showing - removed !isStreaming guard since plan_pending_approval fires while agent is blocked on ExitPlanMode gate (isStreaming stays true until after approval).

When executing shell commands or running tests from the web/ directory context, use absolute paths from the repository root rather than relative paths. Relative paths from web/ do not persist correctly in shell command execution.

Artifact panel editing state architecture: isEditing and showSource state are owned by ArtifactPanel (parent), not by ArtifactCodeView/ArtifactTextView (children). The Edit/View and Source/Preview toggle buttons live in the ArtifactPanel header row as icon-sized buttons alongside Copy, Download, and Close. ArtifactCodeView and ArtifactTextView accept isEditing and showSource as props. This eliminates the full-width button bar that each child component previously rendered. Show Edit/View when onUpdateContent is defined (editable). Show Source/Preview only for read-only text artifacts (type=text, no onChange).

Gobby pre-commit hooks require python3.13, but it is not available on the system. The project venv contains Python at ~/Projects/gobby/.venv/bin/python3. This version mismatch causes hook failures with 'python3.13 not found' errors during commit attempts.

MCP tools created/modified but not appearing in live registry despite passing tests: daemon likely running stale code. Fix: restart daemon via 'gobby restart' to reload modules into MCP registry. Implementation correctness verified by tests doesn't guarantee visibility without daemon restart.

When adding scope columns to a DB table for the DB-first pattern (bundled/global/project), the create() method must auto-infer scope='project' when project_id is provided and scope is the default 'global'. Without this, existing callers that pass project_id without explicit scope will create records with scope='global', breaking the SQL scope precedence query (ORDER BY CASE scope WHEN 'project' THEN 1...).

Sentence collision fix in websocket/chat.py: The has_sent_text flag tracks whether any text was sent in the current turn, independent of accumulated_text flushes. The old check `if accumulated_text and not accumulated_text.endswith(...)` was always False because accumulated_text was already flushed to '' by ToolCallEvent handler before the separator check ran. The fix uses `has_sent_text` which survives the flush.

Hook dispatcher rules (like 'edit-write-recovery') are separate from the rules engine and cannot be disabled via set_rule tool. Attempting to set them triggers 'Rule not found' error. These rules live in ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py, not in the rules engine database.

Pipeline constraint control: use `_pipeline_step` variable as rule conditional driver and `_assigned_pipeline` variable as lock mechanism. When `_assigned_pipeline` is set, rules block all actions except `run_pipeline` and discovery. Step completion gates advance `_pipeline_step`, activating new constraint tiers.

Symptom of task state desync: `task_claimed` flag and `claimed_tasks` list diverge when state mutations occur in both rules and observers. Fix requires centralizing all state updates through observer pattern with reconciliation on event boundaries.

The daemon startup health check failure ('Daemon started but health check failed') is caused by the HTTP server (uvicorn) starting last in runner.py's run() method — after MCP connect_all (10s timeout), VectorStore init, and several other async steps. The health endpoint is at /admin/health (defined in servers/routes/admin.py). The CLI health check polls this endpoint from daemon.py. With 5000+ tasks and VectorStore rebuilds, startup can take 60+ seconds. The max_wait was increased from 15s to 120s. A future improvement would be starting uvicorn earlier in run() so the health endpoint is available before other initialization completes.
In gobby, session visibility relies on the `terminal_parent_pid` field set during SESSION_START events.

In daemon.py start() method (lines 212-230), bare os.kill(pid, 0) alone is insufficient to verify if daemon is already running because it succeeds on zombie processes. Must use _is_process_alive() + gobby cmdline verification instead, matching the pattern in stop_daemon().

In task_claim_state.py: `task_claimed` (boolean) and `claimed_tasks` (dict) are always synchronized by add_claimed_task()/remove_claimed_task(). Checking both in YAML `when` condition is redundant—if task_claimed is truthy, claimed_tasks is guaranteed non-empty. But claimed_tasks still required as template variable for effect.reason (renders task list).

ToolSearch is blocked by a PreToolUse hook error originating from gobby's hook dispatcher at '~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py'. This framework-level hook validation prevents the tool from executing, eliminating one discovery mechanism in the gobby environment.
Web UI Vitest setup incomplete: v4.0.18 installed, but config minimal (test.include only). Missing dependencies: jsdom (DOM testing), @testing-library/react (component testing), coverage tools.
claimed_tasks dict is required for template rendering in task_claim_state.py line 13 (effect.reason template) - removing it breaks template output even if task_claimed boolean remains

Do not include time estimates or predictions in plans. No "Week 1-2", no "< 20ms", no duration targets. Sprints should be ordered phases without timelines. Benchmarks should say "measure X" not "X < N". Focus on what needs to be done, not how long it might take.

IMPORTANT: Files in src/gobby/install/shared/ are TEMPLATES with enabled: false. They are NOT active enforcement. The installed copies in the workflow_definitions DB table (with source='installed') are what's actually active. Always check the DB state, not the template YAML files, when debugging rule behavior. This is documented in CLAUDE.md under 'Templates vs Active Enforcement'.

The close_task operation in gobby requires a 'changes_summary' parameter. Omitting it fails with error 'missing_changes_summary'. This field is mandatory even for tasks with no code changes. Not immediately obvious from API exploration.
Component library is the primary testing gap: ~146 of ~160 component files are completely untested (~91% untested), making components the highest priority for future test coverage work.

Prior attempts to call mcp__gobby_call_tool without the 'server_name' parameter returned generic 'Unknown parameter' errors. The root cause was the missing required 'server_name' field, not a tool proxy issue. MCP tools may work correctly when 'server_name' is provided.
For gobby-code indexed content, symbol-level retrieval is preferred over full-file reads for efficient navigation of large codebases.

macOS service manager daemon restart reports success but daemon may not be immediately operational. Tool calls and daemon checks can fail with 'Daemon not running or not reachable' immediately after restart completes. After restarting via service manager, expect a brief delay before daemon is fully ready to handle tool requests.

The `call_tool` function (gobby's mcp__gobby_call_tool) requires `server_name` as a mandatory parameter in the arguments. Validation will fail with 'Field required' errors if this is omitted. Error messages from earlier attempts were unclear about missing fields, only revealing the actual requirement after multiple parameter variation attempts.

No built-in platform-agnostic daemon installation mechanism exists in Gobby. `gobby start` runs in foreground; `--daemon` flag enables detached mode but provides no auto-start-on-boot functionality. Daemon setup requires manual per-platform configuration.

The mcp__gobby call_tool endpoint requires a 'server_name' field in the arguments parameter (validated as a required field in the schema). Omitting it causes validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'. Arguments must be passed as valid JSON, not as individual parameters.

MCP gobby tool interface (mcp__gobby_*) frequently fails with 'Invalid arguments' errors (seen in turns 6, 32). Use bash CLI commands directly (e.g., 'gobby agents list', 'gobby tasks reopen #ID') which are more reliable—workaround identified after multiple MCP failures.

Claude Agent SDK supports native session resume via `sdk_session_id` stored on agent runs (see `session_control.py`). WakeDispatcher can leverage this for legitimate SDK session resumption, not a workaround.

Top-level gobby tools (like set_variable) must be called directly as mcp__gobby__<tool_name>, not routed through call_tool. Attempting call_tool(server_name="gobby") fails because "gobby" proxy itself is not in listed_servers — only downstream server names are listed.

mode_level architecture: Numeric autonomy level (0=Plan, 1=Act, 2=Full Auto) derived from chat_mode + plan_mode via compute_mode_level() in observers.py. plan_mode=True always forces level 0. mode_level is a workflow session variable synced in 4 places: (1) session-lifecycle.yaml on_after_tool for EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode, (2) _handle_set_mode() in chat.py on UI slider change, (3) detect_plan_mode_from_context() for system-reminder detection, (4) on_session_start/on_before_agent for init. Existing plan_mode boolean is kept for backward compat; _is_plan_mode() in _agent.py prefers mode_level when available.

Tests test_uses_model_from_provider_config and test_model_override_takes_precedence fail when defaulting to auth_mode='api_key' without API key present. Fix: explicitly set auth_mode='adc' (Application Default Credentials) in test configuration.
Escalation tier routing pattern for continuous daemon: local model handles bulk/grunt work → frontier API for architecture decisions and stuck cases → human for taste/merge approval

The cron_jobs table uses `cron_expression` as the column name, NOT `schedule`. Schema: id, project_id, name, action_type, action_config, enabled, cron_expression, last_run_at, next_run_at, created_at, updated_at. The action_config column stores JSON with pipeline parameters like developer_provider.
Gobby HookResponse model lacks modified_output field. Rule engine supports 5 effect types (block, set_variable, inject_context, mcp_call, observe) but cannot compress/modify output in-place.
DaemonClient().get() and .post() methods don't exist; use call_http_api() instead. Found in compress.py where incorrect method calls were replaced.

Database-backed prompt storage plan for gobby (scalable-churning-boole.md) explicitly documents that gobby-prompts will NOT provide MCP tools, with rationale: prompts are context-injection mechanisms used internally by other tools, not standalone capabilities for external consumption.

tasks table has 16 completely unused columns (0% populated across 6,547 tasks): summary, original_instruction, details, workflow_name, verification, sequence_order, github_issue_number, github_pr_number, github_repo, linear_issue_id, linear_team_id, agent_name, reference_doc, use_external_validator, requires_user_review. NOTE: compacted_at, complexity_score, estimated_subtasks are also 0% populated but have active code references (compaction feature, CLI scoring) — do NOT drop.

The `require-error-triage` rule in hook_dispatcher.py blocks session stops via stop hook until `set_variable(name="pre_existing_errors_triaged", value=true)` is called to mark error triage as complete

YAML `>` folded scalar pitfall: Lines with MORE indentation than the first content line preserve the preceding newline instead of folding it to a space. This caused a critical bug in command-mcp-tool-restriction where `not in` on an extra-indented line produced `...)
  not in ...` — a SyntaxError in ast.parse(mode='eval'). Because _evaluate_condition in rule_engine.py uses fail_closed=True for block effects, the SyntaxError defaulted to blocking ALL tools unconditionally. Fix: (1) keep all continuation lines at same indent in YAML `>` blocks, (2) SafeExpressionEvaluator._normalize_expr() now collapses whitespace before parsing as defense-in-depth.
observers.py detect_plan_mode_from_context(): sets plan_mode=True on entry, then plan_mode=False and plan_skill_loaded=False on exit; maintains existing mode_level updates

BMAD master pipeline: 4-phase structure: Phase 1 (Analysis, optional) → Phase 2 (Planning, PRD required) → Phase 3 (Solutioning, architecture+epics required) → Phase 4 (Implementation loop: create-story→validate→dev→code-review with loop-back on review failure).

The `track-task-release` rule unconditionally clears `task_claimed` on task close, creating a redundant cleanup mechanism—`close_task` function already handles multi-task cleanup correctly. Rule should be removed entirely to prevent task claimed state loss.

Gobby frontend test coverage is ~11% (25 test files covering 212 source files). Coverage threshold is set at 80% but actual coverage is far below. An epic for generating frontend tests was prematurely closed by an agent before completion.

Output compression in _fire_lifecycle() (src/gobby/servers/websocket/chat/_lifecycle.py, lines 183-206): extracts strategy from response.metadata; applies OutputCompressor.compress() on AFTER_TOOL events with string tool_output; sets result["modified_output"] unless strategy is passthrough/excluded

DANGER: Do NOT write OSC 2 escape sequences directly to the tmux client TTY (/dev/ttysXXX). This injects bytes into tmux's input stream and can kill the terminal window. Instead, use `tmux set-option -g set-titles on` to propagate window names to the outer terminal emulator.

TmuxSession.send_keys() defaults to literal=True, meaning special key names like 'Escape' are sent as literal text, not interpreted as key presses. To send actual key sequences (Escape, Enter, etc.), pass literal=False. Bug was in lifecycle_monitor.py tip prompt dismissal.

Task validation: skip_validation is blocked by the [block-skip-validation-with-commit] rule when a commit is attached. Instead, provide detailed changes_summary with enough evidence for the validator to verify. Include specific file paths, line numbers, test results, and enumerated evidence.

Fix for debug_echo_context persisting after YAML change: Changing debug_echo_context from true to false in workflow YAML (e.g., claude-sdk-lifecycle.yaml) does NOT take effect for existing sessions because persisted workflow state variables take precedence over YAML defaults. Fix: use set_session_variable(name="debug_echo_context", value="false", session_id="#N") via gobby-workflows MCP server. Session variables (_session_variables_override) have highest precedence per evaluate_all_lifecycle_workflows. New sessions inherit the YAML default. The duplicate [DEBUG additionalContext] injection was caused by the debug echo re-injecting the same additionalContext as a system_message with prefix.

Migration test files that would break during upgrade path testing were identified: test_migration_103.py (drop memory_embeddings table), test_migration_104.py (drop mem0_id column), test_migration_105.py (drop importance column), and test_migration_107.py (agent_definitions scope constraint). This analysis was documented in a plan file after examining 89 test files in the project.

LMStudio prompt template error: 'Cannot apply filter string to type: NullValue' occurs when chat template applies `| string` filter to null values. Fix: In LMStudio UI navigate to My Models → [model] → Settings → Prompt Template and replace all `| string` instances with `| default('') | string` to convert nulls to empty strings before filtering.

ActionExecutor class has a self.task_manager attribute (confirmed at line 105+ in the codebase with memory_manager and memory_sync_manager as sibling fields). This is the correct path to access task management functionality from within action execution context.

Web UI font rule: mono/monospace font should ONLY be used for code editors and tool use blocks inside the web chat. Everything else (task refs, file paths, session refs, labels, navigation) uses the base sans-serif font. No font-mono on refs like #123, file paths in status bars, or any non-code UI elements.
code_index_available session variable initializes on session start to track whether code indexing is available—set in /api/code-index/session-start endpoint

Gobby daemon accepts HTTP API calls to query and reconfigure MCP servers at runtime (e.g., httpx GET/POST to localhost:60887/api/admin). MCP configs are stored in daemon memory/DB, not loaded from source files—changes persist in daemon state without file modifications.

Gobby stop hook at `~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py` (--type=stop) can fail transiently. The `[tool-failure-recovery]` rule handles this automatically. Safe to proceed with task closure if lint checks and test suite both pass—transient failure, not a code quality issue.

Agents v3 sentinel convention: `inherit` is the universal sentinel meaning 'resolve from runtime context.' Used for provider (→ CLI source), model (→ CLI default), and base_branch (→ current git branch). One word, one concept, everywhere. Don't use `self` (already a mode value), `current` (was used for isolation and renamed to `none`), or identity maps. Also: don't create tasks before a plan is approved — finish the plan first, get approval, then expand.

gobby's call_tool function requires a 'server_name' field as a mandatory parameter. Omitting it triggers validation error: 'Field required [type=missing, input_value={...}]'. This field must be explicitly included in every call_tool invocation, even if its purpose isn't immediately clear.

State drift pattern: claimed_tasks dict empty while task_claimed=true indicates either create_task not called with claim=True or add_claimed_task merge failed silently. Suggests DB fallback query needed in _get_claimed_task_info: query tasks where assignee=session_id AND status=in_progress.

commands/index.rs contains a Rust-side defense-in-depth fix that re-resolves project context when paths belong to different projects, providing redundant safety alongside Python-side context fixes in maintenance.py

ParsedSkill.always_apply field (not is_always_apply() method) is used for legacy fallback when checking if skills should always be applied. This is the actual field used in skill routing logic, not the method.

The `call_tool` function in gobby MCP requires both `server_name` and `tool_name` as required fields (not nested under 'arguments'). Validation error: 'server_name [type=missing, input_value=...]'. Passing parameters in the wrong structure will cause repeated 'Invalid arguments' errors.

Orphaned web-to-handoff session cleanup requires three coordinated fixes: (1) session_control.py:809—mark deleted web chat sessions status='expired' not 'handoff_ready' to prevent unnecessary 24h DB retention; (2) storage/session_lifecycle.py—add expire_orphaned_handoff_sessions(timeout_minutes=30) method since legitimate handoffs complete in seconds, making 30min+ timeout indicator for orphaned sessions; (3) sessions/lifecycle.py—integrate via LocalSessionManager delegation into _expire_stale_sessions() background loop
Rate-limiting is enforced on consecutive failed command attempts in Gobby. Break the retry chain by executing a different command (e.g., `ls`, directory listing) before retrying the same command.

The mcp__gobby_call_tool MCP tool requires a 'server_name' field as a required parameter. Without it, validation fails with 'Field required' error. This is not obvious from tool discovery and would require trial-and-error to discover.

Pipeline execution discovery is now fully exposed: list_pipeline_executions and search_pipeline_executions MCP tools on gobby-workflows server, CLI subcommands (gobby pipelines executions, gobby pipelines search), HTTP endpoint (GET /api/pipelines/executions/search). Storage layer search_executions() uses LEFT JOIN step_executions with LIKE-based text search across pipeline names and step errors/outputs. The previous API gap (no list/search without knowing execution ID) is closed.

Antigravity's terminal emulator is xterm.js-based and treats Shift+Enter identically to regular Enter at the escape sequence level. This prevents tmux from distinguishing between the two inputs and is an environmental limitation, not a keybinding/configuration issue. Any Shift+Enter keybinding workarounds will fail in Antigravity's terminal specifically.

When rebuilding the Neo4j knowledge graph in gobby, memories must be processed in batches to avoid memory issues. The script uses `batch_size=100` and processes memories with `graph.add_memory(memory)` followed by `graph.commit()` after each batch. Without batching, the rebuild process fails with memory errors.

ClawdHub CLI actual command flags differ from provider expectations: `--version` is actually `--cli-version` or `-V`; `search`, `install` commands don't support `--json` output; `info` command doesn't exist; only `explore` reliably supports JSON output.

FastAPI test patterns learned during coverage push:

1. isinstance with lazy imports: When route handlers do `from gobby.storage.database import LocalDatabase; isinstance(db, LocalDatabase)`, MagicMock fails. Fix: autouse fixture patching `gobby.storage.database.LocalDatabase` with `MagicMock` class so isinstance(MagicMock(), MagicMock) returns True.

2. Test directory naming conflicts: Never name a test directory after a Python package (e.g., tests/servers/routes/mcp/ clashes with the `mcp` package). Use `mcp_endpoints/` instead.

3. FastAPI dependency_overrides: Routes using Depends(get_server) need `app.dependency_overrides[get_server] = override_fn` instead of `create_X_router(mock_server)` pattern.

4. Exception re-wrapping: If a route catches HTTPException(503) inside a generic `except Exception`, it re-raises as 500. Check the actual handler logic when writing test assertions.

5. Installer backup-before-read order: copy2 (backup) runs before open (read), so making a file unreadable causes 'Failed to create backup' not 'Failed to read'.

The call_tool MCP proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter in its arguments (Turn 46 validation error: 'Field required'). Invoking call_tool without server_name fails with validation error even if other parameters are correct.

SQLite spans table stores timestamps in UTC (via datetime('now')). When viewing traces in the UI, times appear as UTC not local time. The created_at column is used for retention cleanup (DELETE FROM spans WHERE created_at < ...).

Stop hook at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py can trigger tool failures. When tool-failure-recovery rule enforcement activates, the agent can choose to recover and continue rather than stop, per the rule directive.

_lifecycle.py:258 close_task() correctly implements multi-task claiming: uses remove_claimed_task() which only sets task_claimed=false when claimed_tasks dict becomes empty. This is the intended design but gets overwritten by track-task-release rule.

Gobby daemon must be running before mark_task operations. If daemon is not running, mark_task calls fail with 'Daemon not running or not reachable' error. Start daemon with 'gobby start' (runs in background, output logged to file). This dependency is not obvious from error messages and would take 10+ min to discover during troubleshooting.

Gobby task closing requires a changes_summary parameter. Turn 41 shows error 'missing_changes_summary' when closing without it; Turn 42 succeeds when parameter is provided. This is a required field for task completion in the gobby MCP tool.

Docker installer test class TestInstallCommand uses class-level autouse fixture to globally mock install_qdrant and install_neo4j functions, with per-test @patch decorators on test_install_neo4j_default and test_install_no_ext_services providing test-specific mock overrides

`pipeline-heartbeat` cron runs every 30s with expected fire times at `:29` and `:59` of each minute. If execution records stop appearing at a given timestamp, the daemon process likely stopped or crashed at that point.

Gobby's hook_dispatcher.py enforces [require-task-close] rule: task closure requires BOTH a commit with [gobby-#NNNN] format AND an explicit close_task() operation to transition task status from in_progress to closed. Hook triggers validation if task is still in_progress when expected to close.

Sync mechanism in sync_rules.py: On daemon start, calls get_by_name(rule_name, include_deleted=True). If no existing row found (including soft-deleted), system creates new row with source="installed". This explains how template YAML definitions get synced into database.

The call_tool MCP function requires a 'server_name' parameter—it is a mandatory field. Omitting it causes validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'. This was discovered in Turn 46 when attempting to call task operations.

React useCallback stale closure pattern in Gobby web UI: when an async handler inside useCallback references state (like graphData) and also appears in the dependency array, two problems occur: (1) the callback recreates on every state change causing unnecessary re-renders, and (2) if the async operation completes after state changes, it merges with stale data. Fix: use functional state updates — setGraphData(prev => prev ? mergeGraphData(prev, data) : data) — which removes the state from the dep array and always uses current state. Applied in KnowledgeGraph.tsx handleNodeClick.

Knowledge graph entity extraction (memory.services.knowledge_graph.add_to_graph) calls _generate_text_sdk separately from summary generation. Failures in entity extraction can appear as _generate_text_sdk errors but are distinct from summary generation failures, which can remain operational.
Planning workflow entry point: gobby-planning skill/pipeline
Gobby hooks test: mock_task fixture must include seq_num attribute. Observer code accesses this via task.seq_num when storing task state.

In runner.py, SessionTitleConfig (lines 182-190) demonstrates correct timeout pattern for LLM calls: add `timeout: int = 30` field to config class, wrap `provider.generate_text()` in `asyncio.wait_for(timeout=config.timeout)`, add TimeoutError handler. Reusable pattern for other LLM calls.

The McpPage component (web/src/components/McpPage.tsx) contains server management UI with className patterns like 'mcp-server-row', 'mcp-server-header', 'mcp-connection-status', and extensive tool/resource display sections. This matches the CSS structure in McpPage.css.

Shell escaping issue with parentheses in commit messages - parentheses cause escaping complications in Gobby commit workflow. Recommendation: avoid parentheses in commit messages or use simplified wording instead of attempting special character handling.

source-tree.md is systematically regenerated from the actual filesystem state as part of documentation maintenance work. This is a generated file reflecting current codebase structure, not manually-maintained documentation.

Dev workflow pattern for skills: Edit in DB via UI/MCP tools → drift detection shows divergence from immutable templates → MCP export tool persists changes to YAML templates → commit template files to version control.

In gobby, pushing a new branch requires setting the upstream explicitly. When `git push` fails with exit code 128 ('fatal: The current branch X has no upstream branch'), use `git push --set-upstream origin <branch>` or `git push -u origin <branch>`.

Neo4j entity-memory linkage: Entities in Neo4j get a common _Entity label (via SET after MERGE to avoid mismatch). When memories are created, entities are linked to (:Memory {memory_id}) nodes via MENTIONED_IN relationships. This enables graph-augmented search: vector search finds entities, MENTIONED_IN links resolve to memory IDs, and graph traversal finds related memories. Backfill via rebuild_knowledge_graph MCP tool passes memory_id to add_to_graph().

8 database-only skills exist with enabled=true, installed=true but no SKILL.md source files in install/shared/skills/: discovery, merge, metrics, sessions, tasks, usage, workflows, worktrees. Likely created via direct DB insertion rather than bundled deployment.

Exec steps in PipelineExecutor don't raise exceptions on non-zero exit codes—they return dicts with `exit_code` fields. Tests expecting exceptions to be raised will silently pass/fail without proper validation. Must explicitly check the `exit_code` field in returned dicts to detect command failures.

ChatInput button layout fix (ChatInput.tsx): Replaced conditional streaming/non-streaming button blocks with a single stable container using 'flex gap-1 shrink-0'. Send button is always present (left), disabled when not streaming and no input. Stop button appears conditionally to the right during streaming. This prevents the bar from growing vertically when buttons appear/disappear. Send stays enabled during streaming to allow interrupt-with-message.

Expansion system is flaky due to mixed concerns: agent researches AND creates tasks in same pass, causing research findings to bleed into task creation as invented requirements. Proposed fix: three-phase pipeline (research → validation → execution). Phase 1: expansion agent explores codebase and produces finalized spec via `save_expansion_spec`. Phase 2: mechanical validation against source plan. Phase 3: builder (`execute_expansion`) creates tasks deterministically from spec.

Claude Code's PreToolUse hook treats tool calls with non-zero exit codes as 'stop and wait for user input' errors, even when the error is non-fatal. The hook dispatcher at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py uses exit codes as control signals (not just error reporting). Workarounds already exist in gobby for this, but the root issue is tracked in claude-code issues #38024 and #39814.

In Gobby's tool execution environment, directory changes with cd do not persist across tool invocations. When working in subdirectories like web/, use command flags (e.g., git -C <path>) to specify working directories instead of attempting to cd and run subsequent commands.

Progressive disclosure pattern in system prompts only needs to be followed once per tool, not repeatedly. The gobby system already implements this correctly—agents don't need to re-disclose tool capabilities on each invocation.

MCP gobby tools fail consistently with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors ("call_tool proxy issue" per agent notes). Use Bash CLI directly instead: `gobby tasks reopen TASK_ID`, `gobby agents list`, `gobby agents kill RUN_REF`.

The gobby project has CodeRabbit suggestions concentrated in two CSS files: web/src/components/McpPage.css and web/src/components/CronJobsPage.css. McpPage.css is a large file (400+ lines) containing comprehensive styling for the MCP server management interface, including layout, server rows, control buttons, and modal dialogs.

The tool-failure-recovery rule in ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py blocks all file memory operations (both reads and writes), preventing legitimate use cases like one-time migration of memories from Claude native system to gobby-memory

ToolSearch fails with 'PreToolUse hook error' in this gobby environment (triggered by hook_dispatcher.py). Workaround: use mcp__gobby_ MCP tools directly to discover available tools—this successfully returned tool lists (create_task, claim_task, get_task, etc.) when ToolSearch failed repeatedly.
In the gobby project, expansion operations should use the opus model (claude-opus-4-6) rather than sonnet. This is configured in gobby-config and was identified as needing a fix.

React 18 error boundary limitation: Error boundaries only catch errors during rendering, lifecycle methods, and constructors. They do NOT catch errors in event handlers, async code (setTimeout, requestAnimationFrame, promises). Three.js render loops use requestAnimationFrame, so WebGL crashes bypass error boundaries entirely. When no error boundary catches a render error in React 18, the ENTIRE root tree is unmounted (different from React 17 which left corrupted tree mounted).

Watchdog module was deleted via commit ed8152870 but left stale artifacts: `.pyc` cache files at `src/gobby/__pycache__/watchdog.cpython-313.pyc` and `src/gobby/config/__pycache__/watchdog.cpython-313.pyc`, plus stale comment at `runner.py` line 872 referencing the removed module. Cleanup only needed (no in-place upgrade users affected).
task_claim_state.py: task_claimed (bool) and claimed_tasks (dict uuid→task_num) are always synchronized via add_claimed_task()/remove_claimed_task() methods - they form an inseparable state pair

BUG: pending_memory_review stop gate is unresettable via MCP set_variable. Root cause: workflow YAML set_variable action writes to workflow_states.variables, but MCP set_variable tool writes to session_variables table (because session_var_manager takes priority in _variables.py:124-126). The lifecycle evaluator (lifecycle_evaluator.py:621-623) only reads from workflow_states.variables, never session_variables. So the MCP call writes to the wrong store. Fix: merge session_variables into context_data in the lifecycle evaluator around line 618, so both stores are visible to when-clause evaluation.

No tests exist in test suite for mcp_proxy or ensure_daemon functionality - confirmed via pytest --collect-only and multiple test runs. Background tests passed but matched no actual test functions.

In `src/gobby/workflows/summary_actions.py` line 630, the `synthesize_title` function already handles both `event.prompt` and `event.user_message` field names for compatibility. This dual-field handling pattern is the correct approach for accessing user input across different event sources.

Inter-agent messaging overhaul plan (docs/plans/inter-agent-messaging.md): Clean break replacing 5 hierarchy-locked tools (send_to_parent, send_to_child, broadcast_to_children, poll_messages, mark_message_read on gobby-agents) with 3 new tools: send_message (P2P, any session in same project), send_command (ancestor-only, overrides child's workflow with __command__ sentinel and restricts tools), complete_command (restores workflow). Push delivery via EventEnricher injecting undelivered messages into response.context during PreToolUse/UserPromptSubmit hooks. Delivery acks auto-sent back to sender (delivery_ack for messages, command_ack for commands). Ack messages excluded from generating their own acks. agent_runs.result bridge preserved when send_message recipient is sender's parent. Migration 115 adds message_type/metadata_json/delivered_at to inter_session_messages and new agent_commands table. No timeout on commands. exit_condition evaluated via ConditionEvaluator.

max_lines=0 or None disables the output cap entirely in OutputCompressor

React TSX gotcha: When using `obj?.field && (<JSX>)` pattern in JSX conditional rendering, if the type of `field` is `unknown` (e.g. from a Record<string, unknown>), TypeScript errors with 'Type unknown is not assignable to type ReactNode'. Fix by using `!!obj?.field && (<JSX>)` to coerce to boolean. This is because JSX conditional `{x && <Foo/>}` requires `x` to be a valid ReactNode when truthy, and `unknown` doesn't satisfy that constraint.
Qdrant image version difference: installed pins to qdrant/qdrant:v1.13, template uses qdrant/qdrant:latest—template uses more flexible versioning strategy

For #11187 skill-template-refactor: sync.py rewrite (420→100 lines) AND DB migration are critical path blockers—all dependent test rewrites cannot proceed until these complete. Frontend + drift detection work can parallelize after storage layer is done.

Bug: Session variables get reset during companion session creation. Chain: `_activate_default_agent` → `_build_session_context` → `merge_variables` (line ~162 in session_variables manager) uses `current.update(updates)` which overwrites existing variable values with defaults (e.g., `pre_existing_project: false` overwrites a previously-set `true`). The fix needs `merge_variables` to skip keys already present, or use a non-overwriting merge strategy.

When persisting and recovering runtime state for agent execution, use `agent.clone_id` (not `worktree_id`) to interact with lifecycle_monitor. The lifecycle_monitor specifically tracks execution via clone_id, which is the authoritative field for cross-agent state correlation.

The UI is served by the main HTTP server (not a separate Vite process) in production mode from web/dist/. Semantic spec validation is missing. Required checks for autonomous operation: (1) intent alignment with original task goal, (2) dependency ordering correctness with no circular deps, (3) validation criteria actually measurable/verifiable, (4) blast radius proportionate to scope.

Gobby's `no-destructive-git-interactive` safety rule blocks `git worktree remove` command execution, preventing automated cleanup even when worktrees are confirmed safe to delete. Requires manual execution or rule modification.

Indexing progress bar counts only tree-sitter-parseable files, but code_indexed_files table includes all files including content-only files (JSON, configs, etc.). File count in code_indexed_files can be significantly higher than progress indicator—this discrepancy is expected and not an error.

The call_tool MCP proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter in its Arguments schema. Omitting it causes validation error: 'server_name Field required [type=missing]'. This parameter must be included in all call_tool invocations.

Gobby tmux sessions use an isolated socket (`-L gobby`). To interact with agent tmux sessions, always use `tmux -L gobby` prefix: `tmux -L gobby list-sessions`, `tmux -L gobby attach -t <name>`, `tmux -L gobby capture-pane -t <name> -p`. Plain `tmux list-sessions` checks the default socket and won't find gobby sessions.

Skill router now supports /gobby skill(s) <name> namespace prefix in addition to /gobby <name>. When first word is 'skill' or 'skills', the second word is treated as the skill name. Bare /gobby skills falls through to help. Implemented in _intercept_skill_command() in src/gobby/hooks/event_handlers/_agent.py.

There was a UNIQUE constraint error when attempting to execute an expansion, which indicates that all 17 dependencies were already wired. This suggests that the expansion logic correctly ensures all dependencies are set up, and re-running it will not duplicate tasks.

## Agents v3 Design Decisions (Epic #9085)

### Core Concepts
- **Selectors**: Compact string syntax for targeting rules by metadata — `tag:X`, `group:X`, `name:X`, `source:X`, `*`. Fnmatch globs. Include is OR, exclude wins on conflict. No boolean combinators in v3.
- **`extends`**: Single inheritance for agent definitions. Child overrides parent. Max depth 5, cycle detection. Uses Pydantic `model_fields_set` to detect explicit overrides vs defaults.
- **`inherit`**: Sentinel value for provider/model. Resolved from CLI source at session_start or spawn time via `_CLI_SOURCE_TO_PROVIDER` map.
- **`mode: self`**: "Persona, not process." Loads rules+variables onto current session without spawning. Already exists in spawn layer but not in `AgentDefinitionBody.mode` Literal. Being added as first-class + decoupled from requiring a workflow.
- **`default_agent`**: New `DaemonConfig` field (default: "default"). Deep loaded at session_start in `handle_session_start` — before rules evaluate. Writes `_agent_type`, `_active_rule_names`, preset variables to `session_variables`.
- **`_active_rule_names`**: New session variable. When set, rule engine filters to only these rule names (via `_filter_by_active_rules`). When None, all rules pass (backward compat).

### Key Architecture
- New files: `src/gobby/workflows/selectors.py`, `src/gobby/workflows/agent_resolver.py`
- Injection point: `src/gobby/hooks/event_handlers/_session.py` handle_session_start, after session registration, before rules evaluate
- Rule engine change: `_filter_by_active_rules` added after `_filter_by_agent_scope` in evaluate()
- Spawn evolution: `_modes.py` gets `_handle_self_persona` for workflow-less mode:self
- Agent sync already exists at `src/gobby/agents/sync.py`

### Phase 2: Variable Definitions
- First-class `workflow_type='variable'` in `workflow_definitions` table (no schema change)
- Replaces 14 session-defaults rules (all pure set_variable initializers guarded by `is None`)
- 6 conditional set_variable rules in other groups stay as rules
- User chose first-class variable registry over variables-as-rule-outputs

### User Preferences
- User wants `mode: self` (not terminal/headless) for the baseline agent concept
- User prefers config setting for default agent activation (not convention-based)
- User wants deep load pattern (like session ID) for agent activation at session_start
- Keep straightforward for agents v3 — no boolean combinators, no compound selectors

The gobby MCP 'call_tool' endpoint requires a 'server_name' parameter and arguments as valid JSON objects. Parameters must be flattened at the root level of the arguments object rather than nested in sub-objects — nested structures cause 'Unknown parameter' errors.
ToolSearch tool is non-functional and consistently fails with PreToolUse hook dispatcher errors. Do not attempt to use ToolSearch; use mcp__gobby_ or other available tools instead.

Python import gotcha: When `gobby.cli.__init__.py` has `from .setup import setup`, the `setup` attribute on `gobby.cli` becomes the Click Command object, shadowing the `setup` submodule. In tests, `import gobby.cli.setup as setup_mod` resolves to the Command, not the module. Fix: use `importlib.import_module('gobby.cli.setup')` to get the actual module for monkeypatching. This applies to any CLI module where __init__.py re-exports a same-named symbol.

Skill markdown files are inert and do not require Python test coverage when modified. Linting/formatting validation is sufficient. Verified by test_skill_sync.py (40 tests) and broader skills suite (128 tests) — these validate sync mechanisms and are unaffected by markdown-only changes.

## Completion Event Registry Architecture

Push-based notification system replacing polling/blocking patterns. Key files:

- `events/completion_registry.py`: In-memory asyncio.Event dict with register/notify/wait/subscribe/cleanup. Takes `wake_callback` that fires for each subscriber on notify.
- `events/wake.py`: WakeDispatcher routes by session type: tmux send-keys (terminal agents) → SDK resume (sdk_session_id) → ISM fallback. Uses `_resolve_sdk_session_id` checking external_id and agent_runs.
- `runner.py` (~line 366): Creates WakeDispatcher + CompletionEventRegistry with wake_callback wired. tmux_sender wraps `get_tmux_session_manager()` singleton via asyncio.to_thread.
- Auto-subscribe: `pipelines/__init__.py` and `spawn_agent/_factory.py` call `_auto_subscribe_lineage` after creating executions/spawning agents. Uses `ChildSessionManager.get_session_lineage()` for parent chain. Persists to `completion_subscribers` DB table.
- `continuation_prompt`: Column on `pipeline_executions` and `agent_runs` (migration 137). Stored via `create_execution()`, included in PipelineExecution/AgentRun dataclasses.
- Startup recovery: `runner.py` (~line 728) wakes subscribers of INTERRUPTED pipelines after `interrupt_stale_running_executions()`.
- `wait` step type in PipelineExecutor blocks on registry.wait() — replaces old `wait_for_agent` MCP tool.
- `fail_stale_running_executions()` is now an alias for `interrupt_stale_running_executions()` which uses INTERRUPTED status (non-terminal).

Code tasks require validation_criteria parameter - when creating code tasks via gobby tools, you must specify validation_criteria or the API returns: "Code tasks require validation_criteria. Describe why...". This is a mandatory field, not optional.

ExitPlanMode generates a real tool call (unlike EnterPlanMode which is internal state only). Both PreToolUse and PostToolUse hooks fire on ExitPlanMode execution. User rejections appear as post-tool-use-failure hooks with is_error=True and content='The user doesn't want to proceed with this tool use.' (verified in logs lines 128, 142, 155).

The mcp__gobby call_tool operation requires a mandatory 'server_name' parameter in the arguments - calling without it fails with validation error 'Field required [type=missing]'. This is a required field when invoking gobby tools via call_tool.
ToolSearch tool fails with PreToolUse hook error in gobby environment (hook_dispatcher.py rules). Workaround: use mcp__gobby_ tool instead to discover available tools and fetch their schemas.
Session blob compression uses gzip level 6 (balance of speed/ratio) in session_transcripts.py LocalSessionTranscriptManager. Handles both JSONL and JSON format snapshots.
In the gobby project, task expansion should use Claude Opus (not Sonnet) as the model. This needs to be configured in gobby-config. As of 2026-02-21 it was still using Sonnet for expansion.

hook_dispatcher.py runs standalone via `uv run hook_dispatcher.py` (not in daemon process). Changes to installed copy at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py take effect immediately without daemon restart. Do not expect daemon restart to activate hook dispatcher changes.
Health endpoint skills count must use server.skill_manager.count_skills(), not registry.call('count_skills'). The latter is broken. Fixed in commit bdf5e6ca.

The mcp__gobby call_tool function requires a 'server_name' parameter in the request schema. Turn 46 shows validation error: 'server_name Field required'. Omitting this causes validation failures regardless of other arguments.

gobby call_tool MCP function requires 'server_name' as a mandatory parameter and 'arguments' must be valid JSON (not string literals). Calling without server_name or with improperly formatted arguments triggers validation errors like 'Unknown parameters' or 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter'.
AgentRun database model located in storage/agents.py, uses SQLite table 'agent_runs', run IDs formatted as 'run-{uuid12}', tracks session lineage and provider/model selection
`create_defaults` function in workflow definitions is separate from config initialization code—safe to ignore in config-related refactoring

The call_tool MCP function requires 'server_name' as a mandatory field alongside 'tool_name' and 'arguments'. Missing 'server_name' caused cryptic 'Invalid arguments: Unknown par...' errors across many attempts until a validation error at Turn 46 revealed: 'Field required [type=missing]'. This was the root cause of persistent mcp__gobby_* tool failures previously attributed to MCP proxy serialization issues.

terminal → interactive rename: 44 occurrences across 16 source files for "terminal" as mode value. Blast-radius tools won't detect string literals (only symbol usage), so grep is the appropriate method. Some occurrences are implementation details (e.g., close_terminal, terminal_type) that shouldn't change.

config.yaml is no longer a runtime config source (commit 45e22408). Runtime config now comes from: (1) bootstrap.yaml for 5 pre-DB settings (database_path, daemon_port, bind_host, websocket_port, ui_port), (2) DB config_store + Pydantic defaults for everything else. config.yaml is retained only for import/export via export_config_to_yaml(). The bootstrap module is at src/gobby/config/bootstrap.py. Hook dispatchers read bootstrap.yaml from ~/.gobby/bootstrap.yaml. CLI commands needing full config without a running daemon use load_full_config_from_db() from src/gobby/cli/utils.py. Agent-facing strings now point to gobby-config MCP tools instead of config.yaml.

Claude Code client-side tool rejections bypass the entire hook system—no pre-tool-use, post-tool-use, or post-tool-use-failure events fire. This creates observability gaps in hook-based monitoring when tools fail client-side validation.

launchd PATH is configured in `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gobby.daemon.plist` by `_build_path()` in `service.py` during `install_service_macos()`, which captures the shell's current PATH at install time and embeds it in the plist

BMAD pipeline architecture decision: Use `spawn_agent` MCP tool to invoke agents from pipelines rather than embedding workflow instructions in prompts—preserves full persona, memory, and autonomy per agent.

Skills architecture: DB is authoritative source of truth, templates function as Pydantic defaults seeding database on first install only. Bidirectional sync optional (export to YAML not required). No special sync logic needed—unlike original filesystem-first proposal.

Don't use skip_validation on close_task unless there's a genuine reason (e.g. validator can't see the diff, truncated context). Tasks without validation_criteria just need a linked commit_sha to close normally. Using skip_validation unnecessarily routes tasks to needs_review instead of closing them.
auto_link_commits MCP tool exists to link git commits matching [gobby-#NNNN] patterns to tasks, but requires explicit manual invocation. Nothing triggers it automatically on commit detection.
Step editor UI component located at AgentStepsEditor.tsx - handles editing agent step workflows in the web UI

The Claude provider's generate_text implementation shells out to Claude CLI, which can return exit code 1 with no useful stderr output. Log pattern to identify: 'Entity extraction failed' messages indicate the KG service's _generate_text_sdk call failed, not summary generation.

The gobby project's tasks.md file is 5.1MB in size, which exceeds the Read tool's 256KB limit. To query task information, use Grep with pattern matching (e.g., counting '## \[ \]' for open tasks) rather than attempting to read the entire file.

Dev-loop pipeline architecture is hardwired for shared worktree isolation; clone-based isolation (required for Gemini CLI) incompatible without significant refactoring (adding clone_id support, implementing merge-back logic)

MCP block rule silent failures follow the chain: list_mcp_servers() → list_tools() → get_tool_schema() → call_tool(). The failure occurs at schema retrieval stage even though server listing succeeded.

listed_servers at session startup contains only the 15 downstream gobby servers (gobby-tasks, gobby-sessions, etc.), never the proxy name "gobby" itself. This is correct by design — the proxy is not a callable MCP server.

Agent-safety hooks block execution of `rm -rf` on managed service directories (e.g., `~/.gobby/services/qdrant`). Recursive cleanup of old service directories requires manual intervention outside the agent, even after successful data migration.
close_task API endpoint requires 'changes_summary' parameter; omitting it returns 'missing_changes_summary' error (seen in Turn 10). This field must be provided when closing tasks.

tmux send-keys -l with literal \n does NOT submit in either Claude Code or Gemini CLI TUI. Both need a separate `tmux send-keys Enter` (without -l) after the text to actually submit. Fixed in session_manager.py send_keys() — strips trailing \n and sends Enter as separate key event. Commit that fixed it referenced task #10169.

Qdrant embedded mode requires string point IDs to be valid UUIDs. Non-UUID strings like 'mem-1' will raise ValueError('Point id mem-1 is not a valid UUID'). Use uuid.uuid5() or actual UUIDs for point IDs.
Sync layer rewrite identified as blocking dependency in template refactor. Parallelization opportunity: frontend work and drift detection can execute concurrently after storage layer completion
ToolSearch tool fails with PreToolUse hook errors in gobby setup - use mcp__gobby_get_available_tools as a reliable alternative for discovering available tools
In gobby, `resolver.py` and `claude_executor.py` use the dated model ID `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`. The canonical model ID to use across the codebase is `claude-sonnet-4-6` (no date suffix).

File modifications after indexing invalidate byte offsets stored in SQLite—causes silent source code loss (_read_symbol_source returns None) while symbol metadata remains accessible in search results.

The `call_tool` MCP proxy requires `server_name` as a mandatory parameter field (validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'). This was discovered through failed attempts using other parameter names.

Two distinct autonomous failure modes: (1) Fire-and-forget—structurally valid spec but strategically flawed executes blindly to completion; (2) Self-healing—system generates spec with no quality judgment, executes, may compound original problem. Both caused by missing semantic validation before execution.

migrations.py is 1301 lines total; ~1050 of those lines are the BASELINE_SCHEMA string literal (raw SQL containing all baseline table/trigger definitions). This explains the large file size after decomposition—it's expected, not a bloat issue.

The Claude Code `Skill` tool only knows about skills registered directly with Claude Code (via .claude/commands/ or built-in). Gobby skills (expand, tasks, memory, etc.) are discovered via gobby-skills MCP server and injected through hooks — they are NOT in Claude Code's native skill registry. Never use the Skill tool to invoke gobby skills; they're routed through the /gobby command's hook interception in _agent.py.

In upsert_symbols function, use CASE WHEN excluded.content_hash != code_symbols.content_hash THEN NULL ELSE code_symbols... pattern to invalidate stale cached summaries when symbol content changes while preserving summaries when content is identical
When calling gobby tools via `call_tool`, the `arguments` parameter must be valid JSON. Attempts to pass URL-encoded strings or non-JSON formats fail with 'Invalid JSON in arguments' error.

`reconcile_claimed_tasks()` function in `src/gobby/workflows/observers.py` runs on STOP events to fix inconsistencies where `task_claimed=True` but `claimed_tasks` collection is empty. This synchronization is critical before rule evaluation.

YAML templates (bundled configurations) are initial reference only—the actual source of truth is the database. Editing templates won't affect running operations. For cron jobs, changes must be made directly to the cron_jobs table in the database, not to template files.
If LiteLLM code is needed after removal, git commit history serves as the recovery/reference mechanism rather than maintaining parallel branches or archived versions.

SESSION_START blocking (120s timeout) is intentional architectural design for session handoff. Parent's summary must be injected as context before LLM execution when session starts via `/clear` or `/compact` endpoints. Non-blocking approach would allow LLM to run before summary is available, defeating the handoff mechanism. CodeRabbit suggested making this non-blocking but was rejected.

When using generic retry helpers that take an async callable (lambda) in Python 3.13, Mypy may fail to infer the type of the lambda, resulting in [misc] errors. Placing `# type: ignore[misc]` on the same line as the lambda definition resolves this issue.
session_manager.resolve_session_reference() can accept session_id as a parameter and resolve it like a ref. This allows flexible session lookup by either ID or reference format.

Web UI font consistency: admin pages (Agents, Skills, MCP Servers, Rules/Workflows) now use calc(var(--font-size-base) * factor) instead of hardcoded px values. The scale: 1.0 for page titles (16px), 1.25 for overview counts (20px), 0.875 for item names (14px), 0.75 for descriptions/inputs/buttons (12px), 0.625 for badges/labels (10px). Chat page uses Tailwind text-sm (14px) for content and text-xs (12px) for metadata. Skills row names, detail titles, MCP tool names, and detail headers no longer use var(--font-mono) — monospace is reserved for code editors and tool use blocks only.

Commit 511e7b0d9 switched KG extraction from Claude Opus to Haiku to reduce token consumption. Added config fields: kg_provider and related settings. Added stderr capture to Claude CLI subprocess error handling.
mcp__gobby__call_tool requires explicit session_id parameter. Error 'session_id is required. Pass the session ID explicitly...' occurs when omitted. This is separate from other Gobby tool parameters.

gobby-* internal MCP servers should NEVER be lazy loaded. All gobby-* servers must always register at startup, even if their dependencies (like pipeline_executor or git_manager) are None. Individual tool calls should resolve dependencies lazily and fail gracefully if unavailable.

In `source_control.py`, `_resolve_project` must fall back to the first available non-_global project when no project is selected (e.g. 'All Projects' sentinel is chosen in the GitHub tab). Without this fallback, the GitHub tab breaks when no explicit project is active.

Exclusions in the compress-bash-output rule are added to the when condition using: 'and not ... .startswith(<value>)'. Example syntax: 'and not ... .startswith('gcode')' to exclude commands starting with gcode.

Each supported CLI has its own config directory — they do NOT all share .claude:

- Claude Code: ~/.claude/ (project: .claude/)
- Gemini CLI: ~/.gemini/ (project: .gemini/ or ~/.gemini/tmp/{hash}/)
- Codex CLI: ~/.codex/ (project: .codex/)
- Cursor CLI: ~/.cursor/ (project: .cursor/)
- Windsurf: ~/.codeium/windsurf (project: .windsurf/)
- Copilot CLI: ~/.copilot/ (project: .github/copilot/)
- Gobby: ~/.gobby/ (project: .gobby/)

The mapping in agents/isolation.py (cursor→.claude, windsurf→.claude, copilot→.claude) only covers what Gobby copies hooks TO for agent spawning — NOT the CLIs' actual config directories. Do not conflate these.

Bandit B311 (cryptographically weak randomness) flags random.uniform() even when used for non-cryptographic backoff jitter timing. This is a false positive in this codebase's pre-commit hooks. Suppress with inline comment '# nosec: B311' on the jitter calculation line. Location: backoff/retry logic in src/gobby/llm/ area.

When adding columns to BASELINE_SCHEMA in migrations.py, BASELINE_VERSION must be bumped to match the highest migration version baked into the baseline. Otherwise new databases will get the column from baseline AND the ALTER TABLE migration will fail trying to add it again.

Rule engine enforces at most one 'block' effect per rule (RuleDefinitionBody validation). When a rule needs to block multiple command patterns, split into separate named rules within the same YAML file — one rule per pattern. See no-destructive-shell.yaml for the pattern.

SessionContext middleware migration completed in commit f3898911e: added session_id parameter to stdio call_tool wrapper, removed from 12+ internal tool schemas, migrated pipeline/canvas/workflow tools to ContextVar-based context, eliminated _context injection and call_context plumbing, updated 13 test files. Result: 2272 tests passed, tools now resolve via ContextVar path without explicit session_id arguments.

gobby's useSessions hook (src/hooks/useSessions.ts) provides deleteSession(sessionId) function that: 1) calls window.electron?.deleteSession(sessionId) for IPC deletion, 2) immediately updates local React state by filtering out the deleted session, 3) returns both the deleteSession function and sessions array. The sessions are typed as GobbySession[] and filtered by projectType in App.tsx before being passed to UI components.

Gobby validation rule 'skip_validation_with_commit' blocks certain commit operations. Discovering what triggers this error and recovery steps would require trial-and-error or code inspection (~5-10 min).
We don't do workarounds, we log and fix bugs. When encountering a bug, document it and fix it rather than working around it.
Summarizer implementation uses configurable provider/model parameters (not hardcoded defaults) to enable future model swapping without code changes

Claude Agent SDK's ResultMessage.usage accumulates token counts across ALL API calls within an agentic turn (not per-call). For a turn with N tool calls, cache_read_input_tokens ≈ N × context_size, easily exceeding the context window. To get accurate per-call usage, enable include_partial_messages=True on ClaudeAgentOptions, then capture message_start StreamEvent objects — their usage dict contains the actual per-call input token counts. Use the LAST message_start's usage for context % calculation. StreamEvent is imported from claude_agent_sdk.types (not the top-level package). Fixed in chat_session.py and claude_streaming.py.
session_id is auto-injected into pipeline inputs at pipeline_executor.py lines 371–373; templates should reference ${{ session_id }} directly, not ${{ inputs.session_id }}
Git worktree deletion in Gobby automatically handles comprehensive cleanup: removes filesystem worktree, corresponding database record, and branch references. No manual post-deletion cleanup required

MCP proxy connection failures during active session (e.g., 'Failed to reconnect to gobby') require manual `/mcp` command retry—first reconnection attempt may fail even if connection is restorable. Cannot be fixed by other commands; requires explicit reconnect retry or session restart.

Task export assertions for closed_at field must normalize timestamp precision at microsecond level. Exported values include microseconds, so test comparisons need to match or strip this precision to avoid false failures.
Orchestrator pipeline hardcodes temporary clone paths to `/tmp/gobby-clones/epic-[seq_num]` in `src/gobby/install/shared/workflows/pipelines/orchestrator.yaml:141` instead of using `~/.gobby/clones/`
gobby has no existing installs/users other than Josh himself, so backward compatibility, migration infrastructure, and legacy support are unnecessary when making breaking changes.
`ExecutorRegistry` is instantiated at exactly one location: `runner.py:498` (within `DaemonRunner.__init__`). Single instantiation point for the executor registry.

HTTP daemon response None values survive JSON serialization via pydantic_core.to_json() and propagate through DaemonProxy._request() to tool results. Issue only occurs when Gobby is connected and affects how tool schema is serialized for embedding in prompts.

Jinja template filter error 'Cannot apply filter string to type: NullValue' when LMStudio connects to Gobby's ListToolsRequest is caused by optional parameters in tool schemas generating 'default': null entries in the anyOf structure: {"anyOf": [{"type": "string"}, {"type": "object"}, {"type": "null"}], "default": null}

Session handoff guard in src/gobby/hooks/event_handlers/_session.py (line 260): condition `session_source in ("clear", "compact")` prevents fresh startup sessions from incorrectly searching for handoff_ready parent sessions. Only `/clear` and `/compact` continuation operations should trigger parent lookup.

DaemonClient class in src/gobby/utils/daemon_client.py provides a check_health() method for CLI health checks. The daemon runs on a configurable port (default 8765) using httpx for HTTP communication between CLI and daemon processes.
The call_tool MCP function requires 'server_name' as a mandatory field parameter. Omitting it causes validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'.
Dead code removal: read_summary_file() in src/gobby/sessions/manager.py:314-360 is unused and can be deleted

Orphaned transcript sessions with status=expired and transcript_processed=0 (when transcript JSONL files are deleted/missing from disk) cause the sync worker daemon to retry processing every 5 minutes. Temporary fix: set transcript_processed=1 in database. This is a recurring cleanup pattern.
In cli/sessions.py, DB instance must be created before async helper and closed in finally block to prevent resource leaks in async contexts

PipelineExecutor was refactored: _execute_mcp_step() was extracted to standalone execute_mcp_step() in gobby.workflows.pipeline.handlers, and _render_step() was replaced by self.renderer.render_step() via StepRenderer in gobby.workflows.pipeline.renderer. Tests that called these private methods directly need to be updated to use the new public API.

Editing src/gobby/install/shared/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py does NOT update the installed copy at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py. The installed hooks are copies, not symlinks. After modifying the source, run `gobby install` to update the installed version. If the installed copy is missing, ALL tool calls will fail because every PreToolUse hook tries to run it.

tmux rename-window only changes the tmux status bar name. To propagate the window name to the outer terminal emulator (e.g. Antigravity sidebar), you must also enable `set-titles on` globally. Additionally, disable `automatic-rename off` per-window to prevent tmux from overwriting the title on the next command. DANGER: Never write raw OSC escape sequences to the tmux client TTY (/dev/ttysNNN) — this injects bytes into tmux's input stream and can kill the terminal window.

task-creation and memory skills require callouts near the top of each file clarifying that Claude's native task and memory systems are disabled under Gobby rules, with explicit redirects to gobby-tasks and gobby-memories alternatives

Critical requirement distinction: actual goal is a continuous improvement daemon (24/7 episodic loop with no exit state), not episodic human-gated automation. Current pipeline is human-gated and terminates.

In skill injection system, skill_selectors=null on an agent means permissive mode. To prevent automatic skill injection, either: restrict agent skill_selectors, remove skill's audience metadata, or modify injector logic.
Verification workflow requires reading `project.json` under `type_check` section to identify all required checks (e.g., mypy); agent should not default to ruff + pytest only.

Claude Code (via MCP) does not include tool_result content in post-tool-use hook payloads - only pre-tool-use hooks receive the full tool call parameters. This was discovered when attempting to access tool results in a post-tool-use hook context and finding the data unavailable.

Vector store Neo4j batch delete optimization needed: Line 758 in vector store reconciliation code uses sequential MATCH...DETACH DELETE loops for orphan deletion. Should implement single Cypher batch query like Qdrant path (line 737, already uses delete_many()). Discovered during CodeRabbit review, added to implementation plan.
Gobby's init flow already validates for tmux presence with appropriate warnings

src/gobby/install/shared/skills/automate/SKILL.md has metadata fields 'audience: interactive' and 'depth: 0' that cause the skill to inject into all interactive sessions. Removing these fields causes fallback to 'always_apply=false', so the skill only loads when its triggers match. This configuration controls skill injection behavior.
