The bash compression rule 'compress-bash-output.yaml' uses 'gobby compress -- <command>'; if gobby is outdated globally, compressor fixes won't apply. Fix: run 'uv tool install --force --from . gobby' to update global installation. The --force flag overrides the stale version; --from . builds from current project directory.

Task status semantics: needs_review = waiting for QA agent to review. escalated = bumped to a human. closed = only terminal 'complete' state. is_task_complete() should ONLY check status==closed. requires_user_review column was dropped in migration v151 and is NOT on the Task model.

Tool approval buttons in web chat were completely non-functional because ToolApprovalCard was wired to onRespond (which sends ask_user_response WS message) instead of the dedicated respondToApproval (which sends tool_approval_response). The respondToApproval function existed in useChat.ts but was never threaded through the component hierarchy. Fixed by adding onRespondToApproval prop through ChatState → MessageList → MessageItem → ToolCallCards → ToolCallItem → ToolApprovalCard. The backend handler _handle_tool_approval_response already existed in chat.py. Commit 5bb68ea8.

Don't use wait_for_completion=True on orchestrator workflows — this is a gotcha documented in SKILL.md for test-battery. The orchestrator pipe requires special handling and will hang/behave unexpectedly with standard completion waiting patterns.

Gemini auto-linting scope creep: when fixing linting issues in 6 dirty files, modified 40 files total (23 src, 17 test). Changes include isinstance((A,B)) → isinstance(A|B) Python 3.10+ modernization, mypy cast() additions for getattr() return types, ruff line-length reformatting, import reorganization. This cascading behavior happens despite explicit scoping. Prevent by: (1) configure linter to warn only on target files, (2) review full diff before commit, or (3) avoid auto-fixing.

SkillHub provider was removed and replaced with SkillsMP provider targeting REST API at skillsmp.com/api/v1. Default hub list is now: anthropic-skills, claude-plugins, clawdhub, skillsmp. HubConfig.type Literal changed from 'skillhub' to 'skillsmp'.

In gobby MCP setup, ToolSearch fails with pre-tool-use hook errors from ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py. Workaround: Use mcp__gobby_* prefixed MCP tool calls for tool discovery instead—these succeed where ToolSearch fails (seen in Turns 6, 9, 14, 17).

_process_session_transcript function needs graceful handling for missing JSONL files on expired sessions. Currently retries indefinitely instead of marking as processed when transcript unavailable and session expired. See Task #11184 for implementation.

The current skill system uses sync_bundled_skills() function to automatically propagate bundled skill updates to both template rows AND installed copies in the database. Rules and Workflows lack this propagation mechanism—they only detect drift. Removing DB template rows from skills would eliminate this propagation capability entirely, forcing a design decision between auto-update support or drift-as-design.

Conductor technical gaps identified but not yet prioritized: error handling strategy for failed ticks, event replay mechanics for recovery, and subscription cleanup on conductor disconnect. Committed to roadmap addition.

Click default subcommand pattern: Override parse_args on a custom Group subclass to inject a default subcommand name when the first arg isn't a known subcommand or --help. Use hidden=True on the default subcommand so it doesn't clutter --help. Do NOT use protected_args (deprecated in Click 9). See _IndexGroup in src/gobby/cli/code_index.py.
canvas_present tool requires explicit file_path parameter; omitting it returns 'Invalid arguments: [Unknown parameter]' error.

Two dev mode detection functions:

1. `is_dev_mode(project_path)` in `src/gobby/utils/dev.py` — checks if the project IS the gobby source repo by looking for `src/gobby/install/shared/` dir. Use this when gating behavior based on whether the current project is gobby itself.

2. `_is_dev_mode()` in `src/gobby/cli/installers/service.py` — checks if running from a dev install (sys.executable in gobby project dir or CWD is gobby project with .venv). Used by the service installer to decide install strategy.

TaskValidationConfig has a stale model default that causes validation errors. The fix is a one-line change to update the default model reference in TaskValidationConfig (commit b4d4ff3f). This affects task validation across the system.
A pre-tool-use hook error in the file path '~/Projects/gobby/.claude/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py' caused a tool to be blocked, and feedback suggests not stopping despite the error.
TaskAffectedFileManager implemented in src/gobby/storage/task_affected_files.py with 6 methods: set_files(), get_files(), add_file(), remove_file(), find_overlapping_tasks(), get_tasks_for_file()

SDK client initialization was extracted from session-lifecycle.yaml into claude-sdk-lifecycle.yaml (#8756). The new workflow (priority 11, sources: claude_sdk, claude_sdk_web_chat) handles on_before_agent init (plan_mode, mode_level, baseline files, memory/task sync, session context injection, skills, task context, pre-existing error policy) with its own _sdk_initialized guard variable. The _session_initialized variable was removed from session-lifecycle.yaml entirely. on_pre_compact in claude-sdk-lifecycle resets _sdk_initialized so re-init fires after context loss.

All installer uninstall functions must respect GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR env var for test isolation. Pattern: `Path(os.environ.get('GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR', str(Path.home() / '.gobby' / 'hooks')))`. Tests using --all flag must mock ALL uninstallers (claude, gemini, codex, copilot, cursor, windsurf), not just the ones being asserted on, because --all auto-detects installed CLIs on the developer's machine and will call any unmocked uninstaller for real. Fixed in windsurf.py:199 and test_cli_install.py (commit 8ae3fee7).

Web chat agent (default-web-chat) has BOTH built-in Claude Code tools (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Grep, Glob, etc.) AND MCP tools via the Gobby proxy. The `allowed_tools=["mcp__gobby__*"]` in ClaudeAgentOptions only controls which MCP tools are available — built-in SDK tools are always present regardless of allowed_tools.

Gobby's pre-push hooks validate the entire codebase, not just changed files. Push operations (including releases) may encounter validation failures in unrelated files and must be resolved locally before completing—this is expected behavior.

The mcp__gobby_ call_tool function requires a 'server_name' parameter in its JSON arguments. Omitting this field produces a validation error: 'Field required [type=missing, input_value=...]'. This parameter is mandatory for the tool to execute successfully.

Terminal mode spawn has separate session ID generation: prepare_terminal_spawn() creates its own child session ID, making any pre-generated UUID incorrect. This is a distinct code path from non-terminal spawning in the expand-task pipeline.

The mcp__gobby_call_tool proxy tool requires a 'server_name' parameter as a mandatory field (validation error: 'server_name Field required' in Turn 46). This is not obvious from initial tool discovery and would impact any future tool invocations through the proxy.

src/gobby/worktrees/git.py:has_unpushed_commits() (lines 783-797) safely handles branches with missing remote tracking branches: uses `git rev-parse --verify origin/{branch}` to check existence; if remote doesn't exist (returncode != 0), falls back to treating all local commits (via `git rev-list --count`) as unpushed. Lines 799+ only calculate diff when remote exists.

The mcp__gobby_call_tool endpoint has a required 'server_name' field in its Arguments validation schema. Omitting this field causes validation failure: 'Field required [type=missing]'. This was discovered after multiple failed attempts with different argument formats.

Gobby has a bind_host configuration (defaults to localhost) that can be set to 0.0.0.0 for Tailscale remote access. Currently, detection/automation of Tailscale presence is NOT implemented—it's purely manual configuration via bind_host setting in src/gobby/config/app.py.

gcode Rust CLI extracted to standalone repo: github.com/GobbyAI/gobby-code (repo name gobby-code, binary name gcode). Follows gsqz precedent. Package name in Cargo.toml is gobby-code with [[bin]] name = gcode. Cargo feature gate: embeddings (default on) wraps llama-cpp-2 behind #[cfg(feature)] in search/semantic.rs. Build without: cargo build --no-default-features. CI workflow at .github/workflows/build.yml with 6-target matrix (macOS with embeddings, Linux/Windows without). Release publishing on v* tags via softprops/action-gh-release. 31 tests pass both with and without embeddings feature.

The grep blocking rule `intercept-grep-with-code-index` (type: block, priority: 50, file: src/gobby/install/shared/workflows/rules/code-index/block-grep-indexed-files.yaml) and nudge rule `inject-code-index-skill` (priority: 50) conflict at identical priority levels. The block rule wins execution, preventing skill injection and causing hard blocks instead of nudges. Fix requires demoting block rule or reprioritizing nudge rule.

When mocking PromptLoader for tests that use validation or other template-rendering code, the render mock must pass through context variables (not return a static string). Use `side_effect=lambda path, ctx: '\n'.join(str(v) for v in ctx.values() if v)` so tests that assert specific content appears in the rendered prompt still pass. A static mock like `render.return_value='fixed string'` will break tests that check for file_context, criteria_text, etc.
Expansion templates (expander agent, expand-task pipeline) appear not yet installed in codebase - verify availability before attempting expansion system test execution

On macOS, chmod 444 (read-only) does NOT prevent file deletion — Unix file deletion is governed by the parent directory's write permission, not the file's. To make a file truly undeletable on macOS, use `chflags uchg <file>` which sets the immutable flag. Undo with `chflags nouchg <file>`. Useful for protecting critical files during debugging.

In _factory.py, both get_project_context and resolve_session_ref imports are actively used at lines 207 and 565, requiring dual imports to maintain backward compatibility during SessionContext middleware migration

When calling gobby tools via `call_tool`, arguments must be passed as valid JSON in the 'arguments' parameter, not as query strings (e.g., task_id=#10454&sess...). Invalid JSON format causes error: 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter'

Arguments passed to gobby tools must be valid JSON, not URL-encoded strings. Turn 42 shows error 'Invalid JSON in 'arguments' parameter: task_id=#10454&sess...' when attempting to pass arguments as URL-encoded data.

## weather-kamikaze Pipeline Test Results (Session #209)

### FIXED: UNIQUE constraint on agent_runs
The double-insert bug from session #196 is resolved. Root cause was `spawn_agent.py` pre-creating an agent_runs row AND `spawn.py:prepare_terminal_spawn()` creating the same row. Fix: removed pre-create from spawn_agent.py (line 572 comment), made `LocalAgentRunManager.create()` use `INSERT OR REPLACE`.

### FIXED: Daemon not loading edited code
`gobby restart` targeted a stale PID file (58772) while the real daemon (PID 58771) kept running with pre-edit code. The restart command needs to find the actual process listening on :60887, not just trust the PID file. Workaround: manually `kill <pid>` the process found via `lsof -i :60887`.

### Pipeline completed successfully
All 4 steps passed: spawn_weather_agent → wait_for_weather (90s) → get_result → kill_agent. Agent searched web, fetched NWS/WU/AccuWeather, sent results via send_to_parent, self-terminated.

### Remaining Issues
1. **Agent status = 'cancelled' not 'success'**: Agent completed its work and called send_to_parent, but DB shows status=cancelled. The kill_agent step found it 'already exited'. Status tracking doesn't distinguish clean exit from cancellation.
2. **Ghost agents in UI**: Two stale agents show as 'active' in chat UI. One is `web-a73ffa10` tmux session from Feb 18 (blank terminal). The RunningAgentRegistry or UI isn't cleaning up dead agents.
3. **Agent self-kill hook error**: Agent tried `kill_agent` on itself but got hook error 'Schema required before calling call_tool'. Had to call get_tool_schema first, then retry — second attempt got 'MCP error -32000: Connection closed' (because the kill succeeded mid-call).
4. **get_result returns result=null**: Despite agent calling send_to_parent with weather data, `get_result` step shows `result: null`. The send_to_parent data isn't being captured/stored where get_result looks for it.

The gobby MCP hook dispatcher at `~/Projects/gobby/.claude/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py` throws PreToolUse hook errors for `mcp__gobby__call_tool` and `mcp__gobby__get_tool_schema` tools, but these errors are non-fatal — the tools still execute successfully afterward. Retry with the direct tool call after the hook error.
toggle_rule and list_rules tools are located on the gobby-workflows server, not gobby-rules. Use gobby-workflows daemon for rule management operations.
transcript_path is captured from SDK lifecycle hook in src/gobby/servers/chat_session.py (_jsonl_path_captured field tracks state) to enable archival integration without manual intervention

Mobile responsive CSS added to WorkflowsPage.css and ConfigurationPage.css using @media (max-width: 768px) breakpoint, consistent with DashboardPage, McpPage, CronJobsPage. Patterns: flex-wrap for filter rows, single-column grid, horizontal scroll for filter chips, full-width modals. The 'gobby' tag is injected at sync time (not in YAML source files) in workflows/sync.py and agents/sync.py via _ensure_gobby_tag_on_installed() helper. The 'Hide Built-in' toggle in WorkflowsPage filters items by this tag across all 3 tabs.
In Gobby, the chat page's activity panel should behave as a full-screen takeover on mobile view, include a close button, similar to how the session page handles its artifact panel.

Line 541 in HTTP dispatch passes arguments directly to tool_proxy.call_tool(); existing kwarg stripping logic in internal.py already handles unknown parameters. Removing session_id from dispatch args leverages this existing mechanism.

_completion_registry is already wired in register_pipeline_tools() within gobby-workflows, enabling new pipeline tools to access it directly via completion_registry.wait(). This pattern eliminates setup work for future registry integrations.

The `update_memory()` route in `gobby/memory/routes.py` (line 235) returns the updated Memory object directly, while `memory_recall_relevant()` wraps its return value with `memory.to_dict()` (line 90). This inconsistency in API response formats exists across memory-related endpoints.

The stop hook in `~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py` enforces a `require-error-triage` rule that blocks stop actions. Requirements: (1) run lint and tests on all modified files, (2) verify failures are unrelated to user changes, (3) use `git diff --name-only` to identify uncommitted changes, (4) confirm failures exist independently of touched code. Stop is blocked until all steps are satisfied.

`src/gobby/sessions/summary.py` is dead code—contains only backward-compatible aliases (e.g., `TranscriptProcessor`) with zero production code imports. Only imported by test file `tests/sessions/test_summary.py`. Safe candidate for removal.

In Gobby, `set_variable` and `get_variable` are top-level proxy tools, NOT namespaced under MCP servers. Calling `mcp__gobby_set_variable` will fail with 'Tool not found on server'. Use them directly as `set_variable`/`get_variable`.

When removing a field across the full Gobby stack, the layers to check are: storage/migrations.py (baseline schema + migration), storage/memories.py (dataclass + CRUD), memory/protocol.py (MemoryRecord + MemoryQuery + MemoryBackendProtocol), memory/backends/ (storage_adapter + null), memory/manager.py, memory/components/ + memory/ingestion/, memory/services/, mcp_proxy/tools/, cli/, servers/routes/, workflows/*_actions.py, sync/, utils/status.py, install/shared/workflows/*.yaml, install/shared/prompts/, install/shared/skills/, storage/schema_dump.sql, and ~29 test files. Using parallel Task agents (4 groups) to edit test files is effective.

Web frontend is in `web/` directory. Build command is `tsc && vite build`. TypeScript checking via `npx tsc --noEmit` must be run from the `web/` directory. The `tsc` command at project root returns 'This is not the tsc command you are looking for'.

When closing a task in gobby, if there are uncommitted changes, the close_task tool will fail with a hook error requiring commit_sha. The workflow is: 1) commit changes with task number in branch/message, 2) pass the commit SHA to close_task. The git command flag order matters: `git show <sha> --stat` works, but `git show --stat <sha>` fails with 'fatal: option must come before non-option arguments'.

Safe evaluator expression parser bug: single-quoted dict keys inside double-quoted Jinja templates misinterpreted as Python set literals. Example: {{ variables.get('error_triage_blocks', 0) + 1 }} fails parsing. Root cause: quote escaping confusion. Currently active post-restart in Task-4120.
Researcher agent for expansion work is spawned as separate process to parallelize with main agent workflow - pattern for delegating expansion task generation/requirement breakdown

session_messages table has tool_use_id column (migration v140). ParsedMessage.tool_use_id is persisted through _persist_tool_call in _messaging.py. Frontend ApiMessage and SessionMessage interfaces include tool_use_id. mapApiMessages and session_message WS handler use ID-based matching with positional fallback for old data.
The close_task tool requires a commit_summary parameter; omitting it results in a 'missing_changes_summary' error. This is a required field for successfully closing tasks in gobby.

When using unittest.mock.patch() with string paths like 'gobby.cli.installers.claude.install_claude', the patch fails if gobby.cli hasn't been imported yet (module 'gobby' has no attribute 'cli'). Fix: use patch.object() with direct module imports instead (e.g., from gobby.cli.installers import claude as claude_mod; patch.object(claude_mod, 'install_claude')). This is a general Python gotcha with lazy/nested imports and mock.patch string paths.

The gobby project uses a custom hook system that blocks Edit tool calls and requires using the `mcp__gobby_start_task` tool before making file modifications. When hooks block a tool, a 'Stop hook feedback' message instructs to retry after starting a task. Tasks must be explicitly closed with `close_task` after completion or the stop hook will prevent session termination.

Task compaction design constraint: preserve task_validation_history rows and closed_reason field when compacting closed tasks. Only remove null/empty fields and references to dead columns. Known issue: compaction logic still references summary column (dropped in v151) causing potential failures—code needs updating to skip dead column references.
Daemon restart required after skill code changes to reload skill definitions. Use 'uv run gobby restart' to pick up newly added or modified skills (session discovered 32 skills loaded after restart).

To safely condition after_tool rules on ExitPlanMode user rejections: check event.data.get('tool_name') == 'ExitPlanMode' combined with error state. The is_error flag distinguishes user rejections from actual tool failures.

SESSION_START 120s blocking wait is intentional design, not a bug: parent session summary must be injected as context before LLM execution during session handoff. 120s is worst-case timeout; typical waits are short because BEFORE_AGENT hook triggers parent summary generation earlier. CodeRabbit suggested making this non-blocking but was rejected.

Gobby enforces a 'require-task-close' hook that prevents session completion if tasks remain open. The hook validates task status before stopping and blocks the stop hook if any tasks are not properly closed (likely need status in ['closed', 'escalated']).

When closing a task that involves only pipeline/workflow definition changes (no code file modifications), the regular `close_task` tool will fail with a hook-level 'tool-failure-recovery' enforcement. Use `close_task_without_code_changes` instead in this scenario. The `close_task` tool appears designed to require either actual code changes or explicit use of the alternative tool.

Rust-side defensive fix exists in indexer.rs:78-94 that prevents worst outcomes of missing --project parameter, but the Python caller (maintenance.py) still requires remediation to fully resolve the bug.

Task closure workflow is entirely manual and agent-driven. The [gobby-#NNNN] commit naming convention is documentary only. Tasks require explicit close_task() calls with commit SHAs to mark closed_at and link commits. No automatic commit detection or auto-closure mechanism exists.
install_all_templates() is still invoked at startup despite skill template refactor planning; refactor task #11187 documented in docs/plans/skill-template-refactor.md but implementation pending

Pipeline condition evaluation is fail-closed: When should_run_step condition evaluation fails (ValueError), the step is SKIPPED (return False), not run. This prevents broken conditions from silently executing steps. strict_conditions=True raises instead of skipping.

react-force-graph-3d uses TrackballControls (not OrbitControls). TrackballControls lacks autoRotate support. To implement idle rotation, use manual camera orbit via requestAnimationFrame + fg.cameraPosition() API. Also: three-spritetext sets .scale to text dimensions (e.g. 9,3,0) during construction. Never overwrite sprite scale with absolute values — always capture original scale first and multiply by the desired factor.

`rule_definitions` is now on BOTH `WorkflowDefinition` AND `AgentDefinitionBody` (added in 2c14cdfc). Agent inline rule_definitions are loaded by WorkflowHookHandler._load_agent_rule_definitions() and injected as extra_rules into RuleEngine.evaluate(). The `imports` field remains on WorkflowDefinition only.

When gobby task management tools fail with tool-failure-recovery hook errors after markdown-only file changes, call skip_python_lint_check tool (returns true/false for skip status) before retrying the task operation. This bypasses Python linting checks that shouldn't apply to non-code modifications.

Neo4j status shows 'Not responding' in `gobby status` when `config.memory.neo4j_url` is None (the Pydantic default). The status check chain: daemon.py passes `config.memory.neo4j_url` → `get_neo4j_status()` → `is_neo4j_healthy(url)` which returns False immediately if url is None. The Docker container can be healthy while status reports otherwise. Fix: set neo4j_url Pydantic default to 'http://localhost:8474' (matching docker-compose.neo4j.yml port mapping 8474:7474). Also optimized get_neo4j_status to skip health check when neo4j isn't installed (avoids 5s timeout for users without neo4j).

When spawning agents via spawn_agent, always use the `agent` parameter to reference a named agent definition. Agent definitions already contain the correct spawn configuration (mode, timeout, prompt, workflow, etc). Only use ad-hoc parameters (mode, prompt, max_turns) when explicitly told to. The agent record has all the info needed.
The call_tool MCP proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter (field required). Calls without this parameter will fail with validation errors: 'Field required [type=missing]'.
Interactive and autonomous execution modes share identical tmux/CLI spawn paths; their distinction is behavioral (who launches and how it's monitored) rather than technical implementation.

Definition hashing: compute on-the-fly in Python (SHA256 of JSON string, microseconds overhead) rather than storing in database. Justification: few hundred definitions max, TemplateHashCache already handles template-side caching on template side, no schema change needed.

Observer detect_plan_mode_from_context already exists and runs on BEFORE_AGENT event setting mode_level=0; condition helper is_plan_file exists and is registered as allowed function. These can serve as single source of truth for plan mode state instead of broken hook-based approach.

InternalToolRegistry context injection pattern: Tools that need session context (like canvas tools) declare a `_context: Any = None` parameter. The `_` prefix causes it to be excluded from the JSON schema (internal.py:166). When `call()` is invoked with a `context` dict, it's injected as a `types.SimpleNamespace` for tools that declare `_context`. The context flows: GobbyDaemonTools.call_tool() builds {session_id, conversation_id} from session lookup -> ToolProxyService.call_tool(call_context=...) -> InternalToolRegistry.call(context=...). The session's `external_id` is the WebSocket `conversation_id` (set in chat.py:224).

Claude Code persists large bash output to disk instead of returning it inline when output exceeds ~130KB. Tool result shows 'Output too large (SIZE). Full output saved to: /path' message. This prevents full output visibility in tool_result callbacks but avoids token bloat.

The gobby call_tool MCP endpoint requires a 'server_name' field in the arguments. Multiple attempts failed with 'Invalid arguments' errors until Turn 46 revealed validation error: 'server_name: Field required'. This parameter is mandatory for successful tool invocation.
Gobby's existing infrastructure (agents, pipelines, cron, P2P, memory, Slack integrations) is sufficient for continuous daemon prototype; no additional infrastructure build required

Expansion spec validation (non-empty subtasks, valid dependency indices) must be MCP step, not agent-driven. Wire `affected_files` from spec into task tree via `TaskAffectedFileManager`. This removes validation from the LLM loop, improving reliability.

The Claude Agent SDK handles EnterPlanMode and ExitPlanMode as CLI-internal tools that bypass both the can_use_tool permission callback AND SDK hooks (PreToolUse/PostToolUse). Neither fires for these tools. Plan approval for web chat must be triggered via PostToolUse hook on Write/Edit to .gobby/plans/*.md files, not by intercepting ExitPlanMode. The plan mode context injection tells the agent to write the plan file and stop, rather than calling ExitPlanMode.

In gobby's hook system, the tool-failure-recovery rule enforces that dirty files (uncommitted changes) must be committed before calling certain MCP tools like closing tasks. When a tool call fails due to a pre-tool-use hook error about dirty files, you must commit the pending changes first before retrying the tool call.

CodeMirror + Vite duplicate module crash: The web file editor's CodeMirror component crashes with 'Unrecognized extension value in extension set... multiple instances of @codemirror/state are loaded, breaking instanceof checks.' even though npm ls shows all @codemirror/state properly deduped to one version. The fix is adding resolve.dedupe to vite.config.ts: resolve: { dedupe: ['@codemirror/state', '@codemirror/view', '@codemirror/language'] }. This is a well-known Vite dev server issue where dependency pre-bundling creates separate module copies.

Rule group derivation: After the per-file YAML restructure (one rule per file in subdirectories), the group field is derived from the subdirectory name during sync_bundled_rules(). E.g., task-enforcement/require-task-before-edit.yaml → group: 'task-enforcement'. Explicit group: in YAML takes precedence. This powers list_rules_by_group(), /api/rules/groups, and CLI rules export.

The list_workflows endpoint drift information already covers variables as one of the four entity types (rules, pipelines, agents, variables). Do not create a separate route for variable drift detection.

useWebSocketEvent is the established singleton hook pattern in the codebase, used by 8+ hooks for WebSocket interactions. Follow this pattern rather than creating new hooks for WebSocket-related fixes.

Installation mechanism: rule templates' `enabled` field is read directly during installation (sync_rules.py). Templates default to `enabled: true`. If a database row already exists, installation skips it without overwriting.

The gobby `call_tool` endpoint requires `server_name` as a mandatory parameter (validation error: Field required). Earlier attempts with different parameter names resulted in 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors, indicating strict parameter validation in the Arguments schema.
TypeScript compilation: `npx tsc` not found from project root; resolve by calling `web/node_modules/.bin/tsc` directly instead of using npx from parent directory

The gobby project uses a branch naming convention with ticket IDs: format is `gobby-#<ticket_number>` (e.g., `gobby-#8352`, `gobby-#8353`). Commit messages follow the format `[gobby-#<ticket>] <type>: <description>` where type is feat/fix/etc.
Safe-delete is being implemented as a new MCP tool (not leveraging existing capability) to provide trash/recovery mechanism before rules engine can enforce rm-rf blocking

Prompt file creation in prompt_manager.py requires: (1) sanitize session_id to prevent path traversal by stripping separators/disallowed chars or using uuid4, (2) use thread-safe lock (_atexit_lock) around _atexit_registered check to prevent race conditions in atexit registration, (3) store safe path in _prompt_files_to_cleanup for cleanup on exit.
python3 -m pip provides direct pip access without requiring PATH modification or installation steps, avoiding need to update shell configuration files like ~/.zshrc

In test_install_command.py TestInstallCommand class: use autouse fixture to mock install_qdrant() and install_neo4j() globally. Override with explicit @patch decorators in specific tests (e.g., test_install_neo4j_default, test_install_no_ext_services) that require assertion validation—@patch decorators properly override fixture mocks.
The Gobby mcp__gobby set_variable tool requires 'session_id' in the Arguments field. Omitting it causes validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'.

The call_tool MCP proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter as a required field in arguments. Missing it causes validation error: '2 validation errors for call_toolArguments\nserver_name\n  Field required'.

React/Ink setup wizard pattern: synchronous operations (runGobby uses spawnSync, saveState uses writeFileSync) must NOT be placed directly in render bodies — they cause infinite re-render loops when combined with setState. Fix pattern: use useEffect(() => { syncWork(); setState(...) }, [phase]) with a spinner return for the loading phase. This ensures React flushes the render before blocking. Same pattern applies to setTimeout side effects in JSX — use useEffect with clearTimeout cleanup instead of IIFEs.
ContextVar migration work is complete and committed on branch 0.3.2 - future sessions referencing this should check 0.3.2 rather than investigating open work

All 8 missing skills contain substantive, current content (e.g., tasks v2.0.0 with comprehensive management features, sessions with full lifecycle, merge with tier system). Deleting DB records without file restoration would break live functionality.
OutputCompressor scope explicitly excludes code_index case - prevents wasteful investigation into why compression isn't applied there when considering gsqz integration.

When calling `call_tool` in gobby, the `arguments` parameter must be valid JSON string format. Attempts to pass URL-encoded or non-JSON formatted arguments (e.g., `task_id=#10454&...`) result in 'Invalid JSON in arguments' error.

sync_export can silently skip writing tasks.jsonl if the content_hash in .gobby/tasks_meta.json is stale or mismatched with the actual DB state. Workaround: delete tasks_meta.json to force a full re-export. The hash comparison logic is around line 200 in the export code.

The `call_tool` MCP function requires `server_name` and `tool_name` as top-level parameters (not nested). Multiple failed attempts with other parameter structures caused validation errors: 'Field required' for both server_name and tool_name.

Gobby rules system enforces one block effect per rule: attempting multiple block effects (e.g., multiple state transitions) in a single rule triggers a tool-failure-recovery error. Each rule is limited to a single action block.

Claude Code suppresses stderr output for hooks with exit code 0 (successful execution). SessionStart hooks returning exit code 0 do not display visual output in normal mode, even though stdout (JSON response) is added as context. Stderr only displays when hooks fail (non-zero exit code). Block messages use exit code 2 and display stderr as rendered text.

_ensure_unified_compose() in installer skips file copy if target already exists (at ~/.gobby/services/docker-compose.yml), preventing template changes from propagating to existing installations. Template moved to named volume gobby_qdrant_data:/qdrant/storage but existing installs retain outdated bind mount ./qdrant:/qdrant/storage.

In the gobby project, the hook_dispatcher.py pre-tool-use hook enforces file-level write permissions. When writing new files during a decomposition, the first Write to create a new file may succeed but subsequent Writes (e.g., updating the original file) can be blocked with a 'You must claim' error. The agent needed to use mcp__gobby__call_tool to claim files before writing, and even that call can be blocked by the same hook system, requiring multiple retry strategies.
Gobby doctor diagnostic tool is being moved from skills system to CLI
Executor emits pipeline_completed and pipeline_failed events. Registry notifications should be added by calling registry.notify() immediately after the _emit_event() calls for these events.
google-genai and openai are already in lockfile as transitive dependencies—do not add them as new direct dependencies if imports fail; verify pip install instead

Pipeline execution snapshots not stored in database. Only `pipeline_name` string is kept on execution records, losing historical pipeline config state. Full pipeline definition state only available through original session/input data.
Skills are being restructured as behavioral/teaching guides without tool inventories, rather than API reference material. This is the design pattern for future skill development.

Bug: event.metadata never gets populated with 'project_path', but hook_manager.py lines 768-769 check for and expect 'project_path' in the arguments dict. The metadata population for events occurs in event_handlers/_session.py, which needs to be updated to propagate project_path into event metadata to close this gap.

DaemonClient.get errors during pytest execution are caused by the 'gobby compress' pytest wrapper/shell alias being invoked, not by code changes. This is a pre-existing test environment issue. Verify code correctness through full pipeline test suite execution rather than debugging these errors as code problems.

Hub skills contain no Gobby session_id references; local Python development skills do contain session_id parameters. Local skills are pattern-focused examples, not Gobby-dependent. This distinction prevents wasted investigation into hub skills during refactoring.

API key config fields are UPPERCASE: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY (in VoiceConfig), EMBEDDING_API_KEY (in SearchConfig), QDRANT_API_KEY (in PersistenceConfig). The is_secret_key_name() function in storage/config_store.py lowercases the last segment before checking _SECRET_SUFFIXES to handle both cases. SecretStore internally lowercases names on storage/lookup, so get('elevenlabs_api_key') works for a secret stored as 'ELEVENLABS_API_KEY'. The $secret: reference uses the raw output of config_key_to_secret_name() which preserves case.

The 'gobby agents kill' command requires a RUN_REF positional argument (obtained from 'gobby agents list'), not a --session flag. Attempting --session results in error: 'No such option: --session'. Correct syntax: gobby agents kill [OPTIONS] RUN_REF

Variable `resume_step_id: str | None` in chat session code (line 305-324 range) has mypy type-narrowing issue: mypy doesn't recognize type narrowing through `if not resume_step_id:` early return guard at line 317. Initialized as None in else branch (line 305), set to str on lines 302/308/315. Still reports `str | None` error at line 324. Fix: add explicit `assert resume_step_id is not None` immediately before line 324.

Frontend chat mode persistence architecture: The backend persists chat_mode to the sessions table (migration v126, commit a19e1417). The sessions HTTP API returns chat_mode via Session.to_dict(). The frontend reads it from two layers: (1) synchronous from webChatSessions list on conversation switch (App.tsx effect), (2) async correction from GET /sessions/{id} fetch in switchConversation/continueSessionInChat (useChat.ts). The App.tsx effect uses sessionsHook.isLoading guard to avoid firing before sessions load. New conversations fall back to settings.defaultChatMode (persisted to localStorage). The backend also broadcasts mode_changed with reason='session_restored' as a third backup layer, already handled by useChat.ts mode_changed handler.

set_variable tool exists on FastMCP server backend but is NOT registered in Claude Code's deferred tools list, causing invisibility in CLI tool discovery despite being promoted to top-level MCP proxy tool in commit 0aa7440d. Registration gap exists in MCP proxy layer between server deployment and CLI client tool discovery.

Gobby parent tasks should remain open and unclaimed during subtask execution, not closed. When require-task-close rule triggers on a parent task, unclaim it (don't close/commit) to maintain parent-child context while allowing subtasks to proceed independently.

Implement MCP tools `wait_for_pipeline(execution_id)` and `wait_for_agent(run_id)` that call `completion_registry.wait()` internally. These block until completion event fires and return result, keeping session actively occupied to prevent stop hook triggers during orchestrated pipeline waits.

External validator cleanup scope: Remove use_external_validator column from tasks table, delete external_validator.py + tests, remove ~7 config references across ~10 files. Feature code exists but task code never actually calls it.

The gobby MCP 'call_tool' endpoint requires a mandatory 'server_name' parameter in the arguments schema. Omitting it produces a 'Field required' validation error. This was discovered through multiple failed attempts with different parameter structures.

skill-scanner (Cisco, PyPI) real API: `run_scan()` in `skill_scanner.analyzers.pipeline` requires LLM provider or VirusTotal API key. For local no-API-key scanning, use `run_static_rules()` from `skill_scanner.validation.static_rules` which does regex-based detection (prompt injection, exfiltration, obfuscated commands, filesystem attacks, credential harvesting). Risk scoring via `evaluate_risk()` in `skill_scanner.scoring.risk`. Models in `skill_scanner.models.{targets,findings,reports}`. The original scanner.py was written against a hallucinated API (StaticAnalyzer, BehavioralAnalyzer classes that don't exist).
ToolSearch tool causes pre-tool-use hook errors in gobby environment (error references ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py). Use mcp__gobby_* namespaced tools instead to access gobby functionality.

The 'arguments' parameter in call_tool calls must be valid JSON format, not query-string format (e.g., 'task_id=#10454&sess'). Query string format fails with 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter' error (Turn 42).

Session #1749 summary: Implemented 5 fixes from plan (commit 517cc2f3) plus a follow-up correction (commit e1606a88). Closed 8 tasks total: #9026 (plan not shown), #9027 (no artifact), #9028 (ExitPlanMode gate), #9029 (sentence collision), #9030 (web SDK auto-approve), #9031 (task-close loop), #9037 (umbrella), #9040 (template fix). Key files modified: chat_session_permissions.py (ExitPlanMode blocking gate), websocket/chat.py (has_sent_text + plan_ready callback), session_control.py (plan approval routing), useChat.ts (plan_pending_approval handler), ChatPage.tsx (artifact integration), require-task-close.yaml (when guard + template), sessions.py (title synthesis with haiku), all LLM providers (max_tokens param).

The gobby `call_tool` function requires arguments to be valid JSON with a mandatory `server_name` field. Multiple failed attempts showed 'Unknown parameter' errors when this field was missing or when arguments were passed in non-JSON format (e.g., URL parameters). The validation error explicitly states: 'server_name: Field required'.

Claude Code's Agent tool with `isolation: "worktree"` configuration creates isolated subagent worktrees at `.claude/worktrees/agent-<hex-id>/` with branch names matching `worktree-agent-<hex-id>`. These are distinct from Gobby project worktrees stored at `.gobby/worktrees/` or `.gobby/clones/`.

Rules/workflow scoping uses explicit opt-in, NOT auto-derivation. When moving workflows to project scope, users must explicitly move or create new project-scoped copies — the system does not automatically derive project versions from installed templates.

Pipeline has a `wait_researcher` checkpoint that pauses to await researcher agent specification completion. The researcher executes asynchronously in tmux and does not block commit operations—commits can proceed while the pipeline is in this waiting state.

Deprecated rules and variables created during YAML refactoring are automatically soft-deleted on next daemon restart. sync_bundled_rules() (lines 392-411) and sync_bundled_variables() (lines 744-764) perform cleanup by collecting valid names from disk YAML and soft-deleting DB entries with source='template' if rule/variable name is not in collected set.

Bug #8055: The auto-task workflow in the 'gobby' project ignores 'assigned_task_id' and requires fixing of the 'session_task' variable. The issue might involve the workflow not accepting 'N' or '#N' for 'session_id' in the 'mcp' call.

If gobby command not found when accessing from outside the repo, the issue is the missing binary, not PATH misconfiguration. The launchd daemon PATH is already correctly configured through _build_path() in service.py (lines 157-163), adding ~/.gobby/bin to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.gobby.daemon.plist line 23.

Session identity (Gobby Session ID, External ID) is injected two ways: (1) the adapter/hook route response provides it for CLI clients during session-start, and (2) the workflow inject_context in on_before_agent provides it for SDK clients (claude_sdk, claude_sdk_web_chat) that never get on_session_start. The on_session_start inject_context was removed in #8752 to avoid duplication for CLI clients.

gobby-core Rust crate Phase 1 should extract exactly 5 duplicated components (~500 LOC total): Config resolution, Daemon HTTP client, SQLite connection setup, bootstrap.yaml parsing, Secret store integration. This foundation-layer extraction enables Phase 2 storage migration.
The gobby project has no existing installs/users other than the developer (josh), so breaking changes do not require migration infrastructure or backward compatibility support.

Index metadata includes both `project_id` and `root_path` alongside `file_path`. Path resolution during search queries depends on which field drives lookup logic (universal project_id vs location-specific root_path) — this relationship was not fully explored and would determine whether reindexing is necessary when cloning projects.

find_callers and find_usages gobby-code tools require Neo4j to be running and configured; without it, call graph analysis will fail. These are part of the Neo4j-dependent features in the index architecture.

Pipeline-worker configuration refactoring: When migrating from `before_tool` rules to inline steps, retain `session_start` inject rules and keep `inject-pipeline-instructions.yaml` in place. Only deprecated rules are removed; session injection mechanisms remain unchanged.

Gobby worktrees are stored at ~/.gobby/worktrees/gobby/<branch-name>, not in the typical git worktree location. The .git file in the worktree points back to the main repo's .git/worktrees/<branch> directory.

Proxy session_id handling: All 3 top-level tools (call_tool, set_variable, get_variable) require session_id in JSON bodies. There is no edge case about which tool 'teaches' the proxy first—_request() always has session_id available from the tool's JSON body regardless of call order.

Bug: install.py uninstall function at line 1065 used Path.home() / '.gobby' / 'hooks' to delete global hooks, ignoring the GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR env var. This meant any test calling the uninstall CLI command would delete real ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py. Fix: use os.environ.get('GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR', str(Path.home() / '.gobby' / 'hooks')). The install path (shared.py:install_global_hooks) already used GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR correctly — only the uninstall path was broken.

search_symbols() uses name-biased search (_sources: ["name"]), matching test method names containing the search term rather than finding production code definitions/usage. When searching for field names like 'task_type', results are dominated by test files with matching method names.

The mcp__gobby__call_tool MCP wrapper requires a mandatory 'server_name' parameter in its schema. Calls without this field fail with validation error 'Field required [type=missing...]'. This was discovered after multiple parameter discovery attempts (turn 46).

Session variable persistence works correctly across daemon restarts via `hooks.py:117` (load) and `hooks.py:139` (persist) using SQLite `session_variables` table. If variables appear lost after restart, the issue is session ID resolution timing in `session_lookup.py`, not data loss.

Sync system's export-side hash-skip optimization (using hashlib) prevents redundant file *writes* only—provides zero benefit to import efficiency. Import-side implements independent Last-Write-Wins (LWW) using timestamps, doesn't depend on export hashes.

The `source` field (line 36) is defined as plain `str` with no Enum or Literal type constraints. Valid values are: `installed`, `agent`, `project`. No validation exists in the `create()` method (line 116), allowing invalid values like `template` to persist in the database.

Orphan skill cleanup in sync.py:339-349 only removes skills from the database if they're no longer referenced by any bundles. A skill can exist in the database without a corresponding file if a bundle still claims it, preventing accidental deletion.

Edit tool requires reading a file before writing to it - attempting to Edit without a prior Read results in 'File has not been read yet' error. This is a strict ordering requirement in the tool usage protocol.

Pre-push hook 'type_check' blocks all push operations including `git push --delete` for remote branch deletion, even though branch deletion doesn't involve code changes. This interference occurs in the gobby project and is a pre-existing issue blocking normal git workflows.

When extracting methods from a @dataclass into a mixin class, mypy will report attr-defined errors for all instance attributes accessed by the mixin. Fix by adding class-level type annotations as stubs in the mixin (e.g. `conversation_id: str`, `_approved_tools: set[str]`). The actual field values stay on the dataclass; the stubs just satisfy the type checker. Used this pattern for ChatSessionPermissionsMixin in chat_session_permissions.py.

The no-truncate-interactive rule command_pattern needs care: (1) `\btruncate\b` matches the word in file paths, not just as a command — use `(?:^|&&|;|\|\|)\s*truncate\b` to anchor it, (2) `>\s*['"]?/` matches `2>/dev/null` — add `(?!dev/null)` negative lookahead. Both fixed in d0be5333.

MCP gobby tool integration (mcp__gobby_*) consistently fails with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown par...' errors. Reliable workaround: use Bash CLI directly with gobby commands (e.g., 'gobby tasks reopen TASK_ID', 'gobby agents list', 'gobby agents kill RUN_REF').
Static image files (`.png`) can be served from `web/public/` directory and referenced in the UI via root-relative paths (e.g., `/logo.png`). No code modifications required.

Stale global install at ~/.local/bin/gobby (Python 3.13) bypasses project .venv (Python 3.14). When gobby subprocesses execute (e.g., gobby compress), they use the global install instead of venv, preventing recent source-level compressor fixes from taking effect. Requires venv activation or wrapper reconfiguration to use correct Python.

Constraint mechanism pattern already validated by existing `kill_agent` step—reference this implementation as proof-of-concept for blocking/enabling agent actions via tool elimination. Same constraint framework applies across different pipeline targets.
QA review agent runs without worktree context, unable to see actual code changes under review. Must propagate worktree context to QA agent for functional code review. Blocking bug for Stage 1.

Config loading bug: load_config Phase 2 (with config_store) used DB values exclusively when flat_db was non-empty, completely ignoring YAML. This caused any keys only in YAML (like llm_providers.claude.*) to be lost. Fix: always load YAML as base layer, then deep_merge DB on top so DB wins conflicts but YAML fills gaps. The config_store DB only stores keys that were explicitly set via API, not a full mirror of YAML.

pip-audit dependency audit is scoped to run only on main and dev branches, skipped on feature branches to prevent unnecessary CI/CD delays. Check this configuration if pip-audit doesn't run on a feature branch.

hook_dispatcher.py is located at ~/.gobby/hooks/ (outside the repository). When editing out-of-repo files, Gobby's [require-task-close] enforcement gate must be satisfied by closing the associated task, not by attempting a git commit.

Ruff is misconfigured to lint non-Python files (.yaml, .md) as Python, generating ~199 false positive errors. Pre-existing configuration issue. Workaround: run linting on only Python files to get accurate validation results. Root cause and fix remain out of scope.

SQLite DROP COLUMN operations validate trigger validity. Migration 179 failed because trigger `code_symbols_ai` on `code_symbols` table referenced the `summary` column from `_setup_code_symbols_fts()` function. Fix: modify `_drop_summary_column()` in migration 179 to drop triggers BEFORE attempting the column drop.

For commit messages containing special characters like parentheses, use file-based approach (write message to file, read from file) instead of inline string to avoid shell execution failures during tool invocation.

The `call_tool` MCP function requires a `server_name` parameter in its arguments schema. Calling it without this field results in validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]'. This is a required parameter that isn't obvious from typical tool invocations.

render_string Jinja2 pattern in render_mcp_arguments calls str(template.render()), which converts ALL values to strings — causing type loss for lists, dicts, numbers from pure expressions. This is the gotcha source for expression coercion bugs.

Gobby task closure requires a 'changes_summary' field in the request payload. Omitting this produces error 'missing_changes_summary'. This is a mandatory parameter for successful task completion operations.

The call_tool function in the mcp__gobby_ endpoint requires a mandatory 'server_name' field in the arguments object. Multiple attempts to call it without this parameter resulted in validation errors ("Unknown parameter", "Invalid arguments"). The final error (Turn 46) explicitly states: "Field required [type=missing]" for server_name.

Trust prompt auto-dismiss system in src/gobby/agents/prompt_detector.py (PromptDetector class) integrated into src/gobby/agents/lifecycle_monitor.py via _prompt_detector instance and check_trust_prompts() method. Detects 'Do you trust the files' prompts via regex patterns and auto-dismisses by sending hard-coded key sequence '2\n' (selects 'Trust parent Folder' option). Tracks dismissed prompts by run_id using set[str] for single-fire-per-session behavior. Inserted as first check in _check_loop for rapid unblocking.

EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode flow is working correctly as of 2026-02-23. Tested via task #9039 (trivial unused import cleanup). The flow correctly: entered plan mode, allowed read-only exploration (Read, Glob, Grep), allowed writing plan to .gobby/plans/, exited plan mode with user approval, then was ready to execute. No issues observed.

compress-bash-output.yaml line 45: Jinja2 filter shell escaping presents theoretical security concern. Baseline protection via '--' command separator is present. Decision: valid concern but insufficient urgency; defer to separate security review.

The MemoryManager class in ~/Projects/gobby/src/gobby/memory/manager.py was missing a `reindex_embeddings` method that was being called from maintenance.py. The method was added to retrieve all memories (optionally filtered by project_id) and re-embed them using the existing `_embed_and_upsert` private method, processing them with asyncio.gather for concurrent embedding.

Enhancement proposal: Add `gobby daemon install` or `gobby install-daemon` CLI command that auto-detects OS and writes appropriate config files: launchd plist for macOS, systemd unit file for Linux, Task Scheduler for Windows. Would eliminate manual per-platform setup burden.

In epic #10955, subtask #10962 ('Add router rule caching and LRU thread map eviction') has incomplete status and its details were truncated during the prior retrieval session. Requires follow-up review for complete status confirmation.
Memory injection in gobby is implemented as a blocking before_agent call.

Bug #4 in command-listener pipeline: PipelineExecutor.execute() method signature requires `session_id` parameter (implementation line ~164 uses it in execution context dict), but at least one caller is not passing it. This causes incomplete execution context. Affects multiple files; requires systematic audit of all execute() call sites to pass session_id correctly.

All `init-*` rules (e.g., `init-auto-task-ref.yaml`) are located in `src/gobby/install/shared/rules/session-defaults/deprecated/` and are deprecated/inactive. If debugging rule-related issues, check this directory first to identify if the feature has been superseded.

In src/gobby/code_index/graph.py: add_relationships() requires line_start parameter and SET clause s.line_start = $line_start; find_blast_radius() queries need affected.line_start AS line in RETURN clauses for line-level code dependency tracking. Verification via: uv run gobby restart → gcode index → gcode blast-radius with --format text flag.

ExceptionGroup exception handling duplicated verbatim across 3 files (claude_streaming.py, chat_session.py, chat_session_helpers.py). Consolidate into shared utility in sdk_compat.py or helpers module.

File annotations in orchestrator-v2 should be treated as predictions, not contracts. Update via post-hoc git diff with `annotation_source='observed'` to capture observed file touches for learning feedback loop. Avoid mid-execution re-analysis to prevent complexity issues.
Rule/authorization system has no update or patch method—existing rules cannot be modified in place. To change rule constraints, must delete the existing rule and recreate it with new parameters.
unsloth on Apple Silicon requires the MPS variant: install with `pip install "unsloth[mps]"` or `uv add "unsloth[mps]"` instead of standard CUDA version

Pre-existing test failure: tests/hooks/test_skill_manager.py::TestHookSkillManager::test_discover_core_skills_finds_sessions_skill fails because src/gobby/install/shared/skills/sessions/SKILL.md was deleted from disk. The test expects 'sessions' in discovered skills but it no longer exists on the filesystem.

Agent depth tracking bug (fixed): get_session_depth() in agents/session.py walked the full parent_session_id chain counting every link, including user continuation sessions and handoffs. This inflated depth to 10+ for sessions with long parent chains. Fix: use the stored `agent_depth` field from the DB instead (set at session creation: 0 for user/pipeline sessions, parent_depth+1 for agent sessions).

Web chat title synthesis race condition: The frontend's `useSessions` polls every 30s, but title synthesis fires immediately when streaming completes. For new chats, the session isn't in the list yet so `find()` returns undefined and synthesis is silently skipped. Fix: use `dbSessionId` (pushed via WebSocket `session_info` message) to call the API directly instead of looking up in the stale sessions list. Files: `web/src/App.tsx`, `web/src/hooks/useChat.ts`, `src/gobby/servers/websocket/chat.py`.

Some Gobby tools (e.g., mark_task_review_approved) require a 'triage' session variable to be set first. Call set_session_variable('triage', true) before invoking dependent tools. Failure to set triggers hook errors and tool-failure-recovery rule.

useChat.ts currentModeRef was initialized to 'accept_edits' but the actual default mode (set in useSettings.ts:22 and chat_session.py:93) is 'plan'. Fixed in #9049 to 'plan'. This ref is used for internal tracking only (the false-positive setPlanPendingApproval fallback that read it was also removed in the same commit). The frontend default mode is sent to the backend on every conversation switch via App.tsx:376-377.

Claude Code doesn't return tool_result in post-tool-use hooks (unlike other interfaces). This is a source integration difference that affects hook logic—workflows should account for this platform-specific behavior.

Claude Code systemMessage field renders unreliably in hooks - should display as ⎿ SessionStart:startup says: banner but often doesn't. GitHub issues #9090 (SessionEnd) and #15344 (VS Code) confirm non-rendering across hook types. This was used for daemon connectivity verification; if systemMessage stops displaying, it's a Claude Code limitation, not a Gobby bug.

test_uses_model_from_provider_config and test_model_override_takes_precedence fail when auth_mode defaults to 'api_key' without a configured API key. Fix: explicitly pass auth_mode='adc' in the provider config.
Arguments to gobby tools must be passed as valid JSON objects, not URL-encoded or query string format. Invalid JSON in the 'arguments' parameter causes parsing failures.

The call_tool mcp__gobby endpoint requires arguments to be valid JSON format. Passing arguments as query strings (e.g., 'task_id=#10454&sess') or other formats fails with 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter' error.
In gobby, @dagrejs/dagre is a shared dependency used by both workflowSerialization.ts and DependencyGraph component - it cannot be removed even if the workflow builder feature is deleted.
hook_dispatcher.py enforces require-task-close rule via stop hook: tasks in in_progress status block pushes until closed via commit with [gobby-#NNNN] message format

Pre-flight check pattern for local agent spawning: query /v1/models endpoint, detect if required model is absent, POST to /v1/models/load if needed, verify success before spawning agent. Avoids manual verification bottleneck.

In gobby's memory search architecture, Qdrant vector search results and Neo4j graph search results are merged using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) with formula score = 1/(k + rank) where k=60. This was chosen over simple interleaving or weighted scoring approaches.
In gobby, exit_condition is defined in agent YAML files. Transitions are evaluated at line 1160 of the orchestrator. The evaluator uses a `context` dict for transition conditions.

Latest database migration in gobby is v98. When investigating task status or schema changes, migrations are located in the standard migrations directory and can be searched with grep for 'approved' or specific status values to track schema evolution.
The gobby development environment has Python 3.14.3 available, NOT Python 3.13. Pre-commit hooks configured with language_version: python3.13 will fail. Must use python3 or python3.14 instead.

When installer tests mock Path.home() for isolation, they must ALSO set GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR to match (e.g. mock_home/.gobby/hooks). Otherwise protect_production_resources sets GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR to a different temp dir, and installers that read GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR (codex, gemini via install_global_hooks) write hooks to the wrong location, causing assertion failures. Pattern: patch.dict(os.environ, {"GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR": str(mock_home / ".gobby" / "hooks")})

Pre-existing test failure: test_skills_coverage.py::TestHubTools::test_search_hub_success fails consistently (confirmed in full test run of 1334 tests). This is unrelated to MCP consolidation work. Do not investigate as a regression in future sessions.

Bug in `require-schema-before-call` hook [tool-failure-recovery]: incorrectly gates top-level proxy tools (e.g., `set_variable`) behind server schema validation. Top-level tools are client-side and should bypass schema requirements.

Identified risk areas in LiteLLM implementation: model string formatting inconsistencies, streaming vs non-streaming response handling differences, tool use support gaps between providers. These rough edges exist due to lack of real-world testing with local models.

Component mapping for Codex extraction: codex-rescue agent → Gobby Agent definition workflow; gpt-5-4-prompting skill → Gobby skill (straight port). Use these mappings if porting additional Codex components later.

Claude Code's client-side rejections completely bypass the hook system. When Claude Code validates/rejects a tool call before execution (e.g., 'File has not been read yet'), ZERO hooks fire—not pre-tool-use, post-tool-use, or post-tool-use-failure. This creates a blind spot in instrumentation-dependent mechanisms.

Gobby task/session ID resolution: Short IDs like #1802 or #9080 sometimes fail with 'not found in project' errors. Using full UUIDs (e.g., '35768d76-c7ee-4922-8fb7-7dd6e571af84' for tasks, '6b74ef26-0722-46e8-8a61-6a35b0d0e1f3' for sessions) works reliably. Also, create_task with session_id as short form (#1802) fails with FOREIGN KEY constraint — must use full UUID for session_id. The create_task call itself returned the UUID and seq_num, but close_task couldn't find the task by #seq_num — needed the full UUID.

Codex is a subprocess bridge to GPT-5.4, not embedded execution. The codex-companion.mjs file spawns Codex CLI as a subprocess using the pattern: codex --model gpt-5.4 --write "prompt". The /codex:rescue pattern is the core orchestration, not a Claude Code implementation.

Editing YAML workflow/pipeline template files in the repo does NOT affect running workflows—the database is the source of truth. Pipeline edits must be made directly in the database; template file changes are ignored. This caused confusion when user edited pipeline expecting changes to take effect.

Discord 429 responses with global=true are now detected and trigger backoffs for all channels of the same type in CommunicationsManager via the rate limit callback. X-RateLimit-Reset headers now adjust for clock skew using the Date header if available.

Gobby's `block-grep-on-indexed-files` rule blocks glob usage and forces `gobby-code` for file discovery. However, `gobby-code` may not work with non-code files like .txt files. Workaround: use `bash ls` + `Read` to discover and read non-code files (e.g., report files).

When removing a schema column, verify all dependencies by checking: (1) Model class doesn't reference it in from_row() and to_dict() methods, (2) SQL statements (e.g., upsert_symbols) don't read/write it, (3) No other codebase references exist. This prevents breaking changes.

Parentheses in commit message strings cause shell parsing failures when passed as inline arguments. Workaround: use file-based commit messages (write message to file, then pass file path) instead of inline -m strings to bypass shell escaping issues.
DaemonProxy.call_tool() in src/gobby/mcp_proxy/stdio.py implements dynamic timeout for run_check tool using arg_timeout + 60s buffer pattern for ReadTimeout handling

When writing tests that reference project_id in the skills table, you must create a project row first to satisfy FK constraints. Use: INSERT OR IGNORE INTO projects (id, name, created_at, updated_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'), datetime('now')). The skills SKILL.md files that were deleted from disk: sessions, tasks, workflows, metrics, merge, usage, worktrees, agents. Tests should reference existing skills like 'memory', 'plan', 'gobby', etc.

Task #8906 (Add sources field to skill audience filtering) has a complete implementation plan at .gobby/plans/8906-sources-field.md. Plan was approved. TDD approach: 6 tests across test_parser.py and test_injector.py, changes to parser.py (SkillAudienceConfig dataclass + frontmatter extraction + to_dict) and injector.py (_matches_audience source check). Follows exact same pattern as existing task_categories field.
Gobby's `require-error-triage` stop-hook validates changes by type and exempts documentation-only modifications from lint/test requirements—gates only enforce validation for executable code changes.

Phase 4 (Persistent Conductor + Cron Broadcast) landed in commit c15c6d2c on branch 0.2.28. Key components:

- ConductorConfig at src/gobby/config/conductor.py (enabled, model, tick_interval_seconds, idle_timeout_seconds, skip_if_busy)
- ConductorManager at src/gobby/conductor/manager.py — lazy ChatSession init, idle timeout teardown, skip-if-busy, error recovery. Implements CronHandler interface (__call__) for direct registration with CronExecutor.
- System cron job 'gobby:conductor-tick' auto-created when conductor.enabled=true
- CronScheduler.on_run_complete callback for event broadcasting (src/gobby/scheduler/scheduler.py)
- setup_cron_event_broadcasting() in runner_broadcasting.py
- CLI cron add now accepts 'handler' action type (was missing)
- agent_definition resolution in _execute_agent_spawn: resolves via resolve_agent(), prepends preamble, uses agent provider as fallback
- YAML templates: conductor.yaml, spawn-developer.yaml, spawn-qa.yaml
- Deferred items doc: docs/plans/conductor-deferred.md (notifications, observability, ~600 lines cleanup)

.gobby/tasks_meta.json causes merge conflicts on every branch merge because it contains content_hash (differs per branch's task state) and last_exported (differs per export timestamp). The file is a local cache used only to skip redundant exports in TaskSyncManager.export_to_jsonl (src/gobby/sync/tasks.py:239). It should be added to .gitignore.

Handler return signature inconsistency: gobby-tasks handlers return plain dicts {"id": "...", "seq_num": ..., "ref": "..."} while gobby-skills/workflows/agents handlers return dicts with success key {"success": True, "skill": ...}. This inconsistency causes double-wrap bugs when proxy unconditionally wraps all returns.
The close_task tool requires a mandatory 'changes_summary' field. Omitting it returns error code 'missing_changes_summary'. This parameter must be provided to successfully close any task in gobby.

The gobby `call_tool` RPC function requires `server_name` as a mandatory parameter. Omitting it produces validation error: 'Field required [type=missing, input_value={...}]'. This field requirement is not obviously documented and caused multiple failed invocation attempts.

In gobby, tests/conftest.py sets GOBBY_HOOKS_DIR as an environment variable fixture to prevent installer tests from writing to real hook directories. When adding new installer modules (like codex.py, copilot.py under src/gobby/cli/installers/), both the installer and its tests must account for this env var protection.

Daemon restart on macOS uses the OS service manager, not direct process spawning. Use `gobby daemon restart` or equivalent which internally calls the service manager (output shows 'Daemon started via macos service').

YAML template files are decoupled from database state—deleting/renaming YAML rules or variables does not immediately remove DB entries. Changes propagate only when daemon restarts and runs sync functions that detect orphaned entries.

plan_mode string literal appears in 7 rule conditions across 5 rule groups: block-edits-plan-mode, require-task-close, codex-review-gate, require-epic-tree-close, require-memory-review-before-tool-execution, plus 2 additional rules. Significant conditional impact for system behavior.

workflow_definitions table has a unique index idx_wf_defs_name_project on (name, COALESCE(project_id, '__global__'), source). The source column was added in migration 119 to allow bundled and custom items with the same name to coexist - required for the use-as-template feature. Without source in the index, creating a custom copy from a bundled template fails with UNIQUE constraint violation. The baseline schema at migrations.py:800 and migration 119 at migrations.py:1198 both reflect this.

yaml.YAMLError's string representation includes descriptive formatting with source location info, following the pattern: 'error message\n  in "<source>", line X, column Y'. This makes str(e) suitable for including directly in HTTP error responses for workflow import failures.

Daemon watchdog process conflicts cause crash-restart cycles. Stale watchdog and MCP proxy processes from previous daemon shutdowns persist across restarts (observed: PID 5113 watchdog ~11 days old since Feb 20, PIDs 17182/17207 MCP proxy ~12 days old since Feb 19), competing for PID file control and destabilizing even a healthy daemon. Current watchdog implementation lacks process cleanup/reaper logic on exit. Symptoms: daemon constantly restarting despite being otherwise responsive.
Secret storage uses $secret: prefix syntax in configuration objects to reference secrets stored separately, rather than storing plaintext values

tests/workflows/test_task_enforcement_rules.py has 16 pre-existing failures: _sync_bundled(db) + manager.get_by_name() returns None for all task-enforcement rule names (block-native-task-tools, require-task-before-edit, etc.). The bundled rule sync mechanism changed but these tests weren't updated. Tracked as #9011.
ToolSearch tool fails with PreToolUse hook errors and cannot be used for tool discovery. Workaround: use mcp__gobby_ endpoint to fetch available tools and their schemas directly (Turns 5, 36).
Gobby worker safety rule blocks agent-initiated git pushes (intentional behavior, not error). Merge agent handles pushing instead. User must manually execute `git push` to push code.

Root cause of MCP server crash: event_enrichment.py runs on every hook event and has a session lookup with an except handler. Narrowing it from `except Exception` to `except (sqlite3.Error, OSError)` (commit eb26fa5b) caused the hook chain to crash when session storage threw an unexpected exception type. The fix is to keep the handler broad (`except Exception`) since this is a defensive guard during shutdown/edge cases. General rule: never narrow except handlers in code that runs on every hook event — it's a critical path that must never crash.

`is_discovery_tool` in `src/gobby/workflows/enforcement/blocking.py` is used in two distinct contexts: (1) gate enforcement (blocking.py) where it exempts read-only tools from stop gates, and (2) triage routing where it identifies tools that gather info vs. perform actions. These are semantically different needs that were conflated into one function/set, causing bugs when the triage gate fired on discovery tools. Fix requires splitting into separate functions with separate tool sets.

Session-level summary system is fully intact and operational: summary_markdown field and generate_session_summaries() function both present. This is distinct from the removed code_symbols.summary column—session infrastructure was never removed.

The call_tool function in gobby's MCP interface requires a 'server_name' field as a mandatory parameter. Omitting it produces validation error: 'Field required [type=missing]' (discovered in Turn 46).

Rules tab added to WorkflowsPage (commit ac8e63f8). WorkflowsPage now has TabBar with Rules/Transitions/Pipelines/Agents tabs. Rules tab is fully functional; Transitions shows workflow_type='workflow', Pipelines shows workflow_type='pipeline', Agents is placeholder. New files: web/src/components/RulesTab.tsx (grouped cards, event badges, toggles, when conditions, expand detail), web/src/hooks/useRules.ts (fetchRules, fetchGroups, toggleRule, fetchRuleDetail). Backend: _rule_summary in _rules.py now includes description, when, tags fields. TabBar CSS and RulesTab CSS added to WorkflowsPage.css. Removed overview cards and type filter chips from WorkflowsPage since tabs handle type routing now.

ToolSearch tool fails with 'PreToolUse:ToolSearch hook error' in gobby environment (turns 5, 36). Workaround: use mcp__gobby_* MCP endpoint tools (e.g., mcp__gobby_available_tools_tools) instead to discover and interact with gobby tools.

Bmad integration should be implemented as custom plugin rather than skill hub content. Rationale: Bmad comprehensively exercises all Gobby primitives (agent definitions, skills, step workflows, variables, pipelines/rules), making it the ideal first custom plugin showcase.

The `call_tool` MCP proxy tool requires a `server_name` parameter as a mandatory field. Calls without it fail with validation error: 'Field required [type=missing, input_value={...}]'. This is a required argument for the tool_name parameter structure.

Blank/missing output from vitest runs via gobby compress is caused by ANSI escape codes in vitest output (e.g., \x1b[32m) blocking regex pattern matching in compress.py (e.g., '^\s*✓' fails to match). Root cause: subprocess.run(capture_output=True) creates non-TTY conditions, causing vitest to emit raw ANSI codes. Solution: strip ANSI codes early in the compress.py processing pipeline before pattern matching occurs.

The gobby MCP tool's `arguments` parameter must be passed as valid JSON objects, not strings or query strings. Passing non-JSON formatted arguments produces 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter' errors, and the error message does not provide clear schema validation details. This contrasts with the generic call_tool() string→dict coercion at line 190 — gobby's MCP proxy does not perform this coercion.

User preference: Do not include arbitrary human-time estimates (weeks, months) in plans. When agents are building, time estimates based on human developer speed are meaningless. Frame plans around dependency order and parallelizability instead.

In gobby, .claude/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py is referenced by all Claude Code hooks (pre-tool-use, post-tool-use, stop). If this file or its symlink target is deleted/broken, ALL tool use is blocked and the agent enters an unrecoverable deadlock — it cannot use Bash or any tool to fix itself. The stop hook also fires in an infinite loop preventing clean session exit. The only recovery is manual intervention from a separate terminal.

Existing integrations.md file covers GitHub/Linear integrations only, not communications. New communications integrations guide created at docs/guides/comm-integrations.md to prevent scope duplication.

gcode and gsqz are Rust CLI binaries in the gobby-cli monorepo (github.com/GobbyAI/gobby-cli). Local clone at ~/Projects/gobby-cli, submodule in gobby at deps/gobby-cli.

Crates (crates.io): gobby-code (binary: gcode) — code index CLI (AST search, symbol nav, dependency graph, embeddings). gobby-squeeze (binary: gsqz) — output compressor for LLM token optimization.

Why Rust: Subagents (Claude Code Agent tool) can't use MCP — only Bash. gcode gives them AST-level code intelligence via CLI. gsqz compresses tool output to save tokens. Both need Rust perf (~9ms overhead vs ~527ms Python startup).

Architecture (gcode): Reads/writes gobby's databases directly (no daemon needed) — SQLite (gobby-hub.db) for symbols/FTS5/config, Neo4j (HTTP API) for graph queries, Qdrant (REST API) for semantic search via llama-cpp-2 (nomic-embed-text-v1.5 GGUF), Tree-sitter for code parsing (16 languages), Fernet decryption of gobby's SecretStore for Neo4j auth.

Key decisions: Monorepo (was standalone repos), coupling is convention-only. Cargo feature: embeddings (default on) wraps llama-cpp-2. Install fallback chain: submodule → GitHub releases → cargo-binstall → cargo install. Binaries at ~/.gobby/bin/, version stamps at .gcode-version / .gsqz-version.

Python integration lives in gobby repo at src/gobby/cli/install_setup.py.

Both `session-lifecycle.yaml` and `headless-lifecycle.yaml` contain the `synthesize_title` action and need to be updated together when modifying title synthesis behavior. The action appears after `detect_plan_mode_from_context` in the workflow sequence.
gcode CLI development: Sprint 1 delivered FTS + CLI wiring, Sprint 2 added Neo4j + Qdrant hybrid search integration

Claude Code suppresses stderr output on successful hook execution (exit code 0). Stderr-based workarounds for missing systemMessage field will fail silently—stderr content won't be visible when hook completes successfully.

In the gobby project, the pre-tool-use hook (hook_dispatcher.py) can block Edit operations with a 'You must...' error. The workaround is to call the appropriate mcp__gobby_ MCP tool first (which returns a seq_num), then use Write instead of Edit to modify the file.

Claimed task context injection pattern: `handle_session_start()` in `src/gobby/hooks/event_handlers/_session.py` injects claimed task context from session variables via `_build_claimed_task_context()` (formatted with "Claimed Tasks (Persisted)" header) into prompts as additional_context when session_id/project_id exist but task_id is absent. Enables persistent task awareness across session lifecycle without per-message re-injection.
test_session_defaults.py has 2 pre-existing failures (variable count mismatch: 23 vs 21 expected) unrelated to plan mode changes; verified by running baseline code without modifications

dev-loop.yaml has separate worktree_id and clone_id inputs. Do NOT conflate them — they are distinct isolation resources routed through dispatch_batch/spawn_agent to different storage backends (worktree_storage vs clone_storage). An earlier approach to make worktree_id a 'unified resource ID' was reverted because it caused semantic confusion and error masking.

Task #10436 (commit db56c2cc): Fixed memory search integration in tool dispatch system. Three changes: (1) added `inject_result: true` to search_memories rule in YAML so results are captured instead of discarded, (2) implemented `prompt_text` → `query` field mapping in `_dispatch_mcp_calls` so search_memories receives actual prompt text as its query parameter, (3) added dedicated `search_memories` case in `_format_discovery_result` to format results as `<project-memory>` XML tags instead of raw JSON.
The Sessions tab and Terminals tab are being deprecated in favor of the Chat page (#chat). The Chat page is the primary UI going forward. Chat v2 replaces (not coexists with) the old UI.
