Workflow definitions use a template→installed state pattern: template rows are protected/read-only, modifications must be made to the installed state row. The schema tracks this via a `state` column in workflow_definitions table (values: 'template' and 'installed').

Session #1742 got stuck in plan mode due to the artifact panel approval bug. Root cause: a path resolution issue in chat_session_permissions.py where _read_plan_file() resolved paths relative to the daemon's CWD instead of project_path, causing the artifact panel update to block. ExitPlanMode was called but user couldn't approve because ArtifactPanel doesn't render PlanApprovalBar. The UserPromptSubmit hook keeps re-injecting plan-mode status=active, blocking all writes. Another agent was dispatched to fix the artifact panel approval UI separately.

Test failures during SessionContext migration were caused by test code passing `_context=context` parameter to functions that no longer accepted it in their signatures. When updating similar parameter-based patterns, check test invocations for stale parameter references.

Gobby's link_commit tool requires full 40-character commit SHAs. Short SHAs (e.g., 8 characters like '6eaea48f') return 'Invalid or unresolved commit SHA' error. Use `git rev-parse <short-sha>` to get the full SHA before calling link_commit.

Database schema modified to eliminate intermediate template rows. All test data source references changed from 'source="template"' to 'source="installed"' as part of sync_* function rewrite. Impacts test data generation and schema understanding.

MCP tool calls to mcp__gobby_* fail consistently with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors. Workaround: use Bash CLI directly instead. Tested commands that work: `gobby tasks reopen [TASK_ID]`, `gobby agents list`, `gobby agents kill [RUN_REF]`. CLI approach is reliable.

gobby-orchestration MCP server removed in commit d3e281be (task #8882). Its 11 tools across 5 modules (orchestrate, wait, monitor, review, cleanup) are replaced by the pipeline system. Key capability preserved: worktree merge-back-to-base now lives as merge_worktree in gobby-worktrees (backed by WorktreeGitManager.merge_branch). merge_clone_to_target was renamed to merge_clone. gobby-clones gained 7 new parity tools: claim_clone, release_clone, get_clone_by_task, link_task_to_clone, get_clone_stats, detect_stale_clones, cleanup_stale_clones. Storage layer got count_by_status, find_stale, cleanup_stale.

Memory and code index modules don't store embedding BLOBs in SQLite—this is specific to the semantic_search embedding implementation. Understanding which modules use SQLite-backed embeddings vs. other approaches is important for refactoring.

In `require-schema-before-call.yaml`, strict 'block' validation strategies can be converted to `inject_context` nudge approaches to provide guidance while maintaining operational flexibility. This YAML pattern improves progressive discovery server initialization.

User decision: For config tools architecture, chose 'New gobby-config server' approach over ad-hoc additions to existing patterns. This architectural direction was documented in jazzy-stargazing-map.md plan file.
After attempting to kill an agent with 'gobby agents kill [RUN_REF]', the user should verify the agent's status by running 'gobby agents list'.

Gobby's worktree system uses `.gobby/worktrees/` or `.gobby/clones/` — distinct from Claude Code's `.claude/worktrees/` — to avoid confusion between two separate systems when encountering worktree directories.
The mcp__gobby_call_tool MCP tool requires 'server_name' as a mandatory field. Tool calls fail with validation error 'Field required [type=missing]' if omitted.

The `gcode` crate in crates.io registry is NOT the Gobby gcode CLI—the registry crate has version history back to 2018 (predates Gobby project). Gobby's gcode and gsqz CLIs are published under different crate names. Authoritative names are in gobby-cli monorepo Cargo.toml.

Web UI tmux terminals: tmux attach handler in src/gobby/servers/websocket/tmux.py configures session options (status off, mouse on) before starting the PTY reader. Frontend TerminalView in web/src/components/terminals/TerminalsPage.tsx has VIEW/EDIT modes — VIEW mode forwards mouse escape sequences (SGR \x1b[< and X10 \x1b[M) but blocks keyboard input.
Tool-failure-recovery hook errors at `~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py` occur during stop sequences via Gobby dispatcher but are recoverable using documented protocols

~/.gobby/services/qdrant/ contains stale bind-mount data leftover from pre-named-volume Docker architecture. Live Qdrant data now stored in gobby_qdrant_data Docker volume. Stale directory should be deleted before implementing PACK_DIRS to prevent double-packing.

JSONL import (commit 68843861) deliberately regenerates seq_nums on import rather than preserving values from the file. This is intentional design for cross-machine portability—the same data imported on different machines receives different #N shorthand references. seq_num values are not stored in JSONL format.
Commit message formatting: avoid heredoc-style messages (<<<EOF...EOF) when committing via agent tools—they cause shell expansion/escaping issues. Use inline commit message format instead.
gobby tool parameters must be flattened at the top level, not nested in objects. Nested structures fail with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors.

Gobby's pre-tool-use hooks enforce a clean git worktree when approving tasks. If sibling task files are dirty (e.g., from a stash pop), you must stash them before the approve call will pass the hook check.

Single worktree per epic implementation: pipeline state tracks `_worktree_id`, reuses same worktree across iterations with branch naming `epic-{seq_num}` (only creates on first iteration, subsequent iterations skip creation)
Workaround for stop hook [require-task-close] block: the stop gate will auto-open after max retry attempts, eventually allowing shutdown to proceed despite the blocking rule.

ProjectDetailView.tsx contains an existing 'Code' tab that uses FilesPage component for file browsing. Codebase features (search, graph visualization, index status indicator) should be integrated within this existing Code tab as multiple view modes (Browse/Search/Graph), not as separate sidebar tabs.
tree-sitter Query API breaking change: Query.matches() method no longer exists; API refactored in recent version, need to identify replacement method for executing queries against AST nodes
Rule `strip-skip-validation-with-commit` rewrites `skip_validation: false` to compliant form (not just blocking). Includes `inject_context` nudge explaining validation expectations.

Template source references updated throughout test suite and codebase: all instances of source="template" changed to source="installed" as part of refactoring removing cascade logic and install_all_templates functionality.

Schema modifications to Neo4j require full reindex: when code changes what data is written to nodes (e.g., adding line_start fields), use `gcode index --full` instead of regular `gcode index`. Incremental indexing skips files with unchanged content hash, leaving existing nodes without new fields.

When calling gobby MCP tools like `claim_task`, you must first call `mcp__gobby__call_tool` with `get_tool_schema` to discover the required parameters before invoking the tool itself. Calling the tool without knowing the schema will result in a PreToolUse hook error.

In the gobby project, a pre-tool-use hook (hook_dispatcher.py at ~/.gobby/hooks/) blocks Write operations with 'You must create a task first' error. To unblock, you must create and claim a task via the gobby-tasks MCP server using the create_task tool before any file writes will succeed.

When closing a task via close_task, if the task has validation criteria defined, the call fails with validation_failed error: 'Critical criteria still referenced'. Workaround: call update_task_validation_criteria first to clear/remove those references, then close_task will succeed.

Export caching bug: The export system compares a content_hash against the value stored in tasks_meta.json. If the stored hash is stale, export succeeds but tasks.jsonl doesn't update despite DB changes (silent failure). Workaround: Reset/clear the hash in tasks_meta.json, then re-run sync_export. This is a silent-fail caching mechanism, not an obvious error.
maintenance.py lines 81 and 139 contain gcode index calls missing the --project parameter, causing indexing to target the wrong project directory. This is the primary bug.

When agents need to discover files (especially .txt report files), use Bash ls + Read instead of glob. The project has a `block-grep-on-indexed-files` rule that blocks glob operations and forces gobby-code, which may not work reliably with .txt files. Bash ls (to list files) + Read (to access them) is the recommended pattern for file discovery in agent implementations.

gobby install already supports optional feature patterns: --no-ext-services flag (Docker deps), individual CLI flags for optional tools, fallback toolchain chains (gsqz, gcode via cargo/GitHub). Local embeddings should follow this existing opt-in gate architecture rather than new paradigm.

ToolSearch tool throws 'PreToolUse:ToolSearch hook error' when used (observed twice in turns 5 and 36). This is a known issue with the hook dispatcher at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py. Use mcp__gobby_ tool instead to discover available tools.

The gobby MCP tool enforces a result size limit of approximately 270k characters. When this limit is exceeded, it returns an error and saves the full output to a file instead of returning it in the response. This affects large-scale operations like complete workspace audits.

Pre-commit hook 'require-task-close' in hook_dispatcher.py blocks commits when task is in 'in_progress' status. Task must be transitioned to 'closed' or 'review_approved' state to satisfy the hook and allow commit to proceed.
docker-compose.neo4j.yml is deprecated and outdated - pinned neo4j:5.26 with isolated volume layout, causing gobby install to bypass unified service configuration

When merging branches in gobby, `uv.lock` frequently becomes dirty due to uv regenerating it. Must `git checkout -- uv.lock` (or stash) before merging to avoid 'local changes would be overwritten' errors. After checkout/stash, uv may re-dirty it again, requiring repeated cleanup.
CLI `cron add` command at cli/cron.py:70 missing 'handler' in click.Choice list, though backend fully supports handler jobs. This is a CLI-only constraint blocking user-facing handler job creation.

After code invalidation/re-indexing, RELATES_TO_CODE edges between memory entities and code index are NOT automatically recreated. Edges are only recreated when memories are created or updated, not during code index rebuilds. Edge recreation logic is in knowledge_graph.py:391-447 (_link_entities_to_code).
When validating changes to skill definition files, run test_skill_sync.py plus the broader skills test suite together for comprehensive validation (~128 tests total).

plan_mode is a boolean flag ('are edits blocked?') distinct from mode_level (UI-facing tri-state slider). plan_mode used in 7 rule conditions across 5 groups: block-edits-plan-mode (blocks when active), require-task-close (skips when not plan_mode), codex-review-gate (skips in plan_mode), require-epic-tree-close (skips in plan_mode), and 3 others.

LiteLLM's built-in `model_cost` dictionary (accessible via gobby's existing litellm.py executor dependency) should be the primary source for model pricing lookups. Static `cost_table.py` should serve as a secondary override layer for corrections, custom pricing, and models not yet in LiteLLM's map.

TDD sandwich pattern implemented in execute_expansion() (src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/tasks/_expansion.py). When tdd=true, wraps each phase of code/config tasks with [TEST] and [REF] bookends. Phase detection uses '### Plan Section: N.N' headers in subtask descriptions. Pattern: [TEST] → [IMPL] → [IMPL] → ... → [REF] per phase, with Phase N [REF] → Phase N+1 [TEST] chaining. Non-TDD categories (docs, research, planning, manual, test, refactor) pass through unwrapped. expand-task pipeline defaults tdd=true. Subtasks without plan sections are grouped as a single phase.
BMAD agent definitions are located in `_bmad/bmm/agents/` directory. BMAD personas (bmad-dev, bmad-pm, etc.) map to Gobby agents as preamble + optional skills/rules/workflows combinations.

Rule engine block messages now include the rule name. In rule_engine.py line 119, after Jinja rendering, block_reason is prepended with 'Rule enforced by Gobby: [{_row.name}]\n'. This means all hook error messages from rule blocks show which rule fired. The change is in the single code path where block effects are applied (the evaluate() method's block handling loop).

The call_tool MCP tool requires a 'server_name' field as a mandatory parameter. Calls without this field fail with validation error: '2 validation errors for call_toolArguments - server_name Field required'

OTel span status values: UNSET (default, no explicit status set), OK (explicitly marked successful), ERROR (explicitly marked failed). Gobby spans show as UNSET because the code doesn't explicitly set span status — this is normal for instrumentation that only records structure without success/failure judgments.

Stop hook dispatcher at ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py enforces require-task-close rule, blocking stop when tasks remain in in_progress status. This is the current mechanism preventing stop during active tasks.

After directly modifying gobby's workflow_definitions table in SQLite, you must call mcp__gobby_reload_workflows to clear the workflow cache. Without this, changes won't take effect until the next process restart.

CronExecutor handler action type: registered via `cron_executor.register_handler(name, async_callable)`. Handler name referenced in `action_config["handler"]`. System cron jobs use `gobby:` prefix convention (e.g., `gobby:pipeline-heartbeat`). PipelineHeartbeat queries across ALL projects (`all_projects=True`) because the daemon is single-instance serving all projects. Heartbeat auto-created on startup if missing via `cron_storage.get_job_by_name()`. Key files: `src/gobby/scheduler/executor.py` (CronHandler type + register_handler), `src/gobby/workflows/pipeline_heartbeat.py` (PipelineHeartbeat class), `src/gobby/storage/cron.py` (get_job_by_name), `src/gobby/storage/pipelines.py` (get_stalled_executions with all_projects flag).

Pre-existing bug: require-schema-before-call rule's when condition evaluates to allow (not block) in RuleEngine integration tests. The condition uses `is_tool_unlocked(tool_input)` and `is_discovery_tool(tool_input.get('tool_name'))` helper functions. The rule works at the YAML/Pydantic level but fails in RuleEngine.evaluate() - likely the helper functions aren't registered in the engine's evaluator context. Affects 3 tests in TestRuleEngineIntegration in test_progressive_disclosure_rules.py: test_call_tool_blocked_before_schema_lookup, test_full_disclosure_flow, test_full_round_trip_via_rule_engine. The get_tool_schema blocking tests pass, suggesting the issue is specific to `is_tool_unlocked`/`is_discovery_tool` helpers.

Workaround for orchestrator steps referencing invoke_pipeline results: check .status field from wrapper (e.g., 'dev_loop.output.status == "completed"') instead of trying to access specific child output fields that aren't unwrapped. Status is available in wrapper, child outputs are not.

GGML's Metal tensor API requires M5 or A19+ Apple Silicon chips. M4, M3, M2, and M1 Macs cannot use Metal tensor operations and must fall back to CPU. This affects embedding operations in gobby-cli/gcode.
ToolSearch tool triggers PreToolUse hook errors in gobby environment. Direct mcp__gobby_* tool calls must be used instead to discover available tools.

gobby MCP tools expect flattened parameter structures (all params at top level) rather than nested argument objects. Multiple failed attempts with nested structures produced 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors before realizing parameters must be flattened.

Agent definition CRUD now includes update_agent_rules and update_agent_variables MCP tools (registered in agents.py) and PATCH /api/agents/definitions/{id}/rules and /variables HTTP endpoints (in routes/agents.py). The MCP tools are in agent_definitions.py. Rules uses add/remove lists, variables uses set_vars dict and remove list. Both modify the workflows.rules and workflows.variables fields in definition_json.

Stderr capture in Claude.py subprocess calls is not working - exit code 1 occurs with no stderr output, making it difficult to debug CLI failures. The CLAUDECODE env var approach for capturing errors may not be functioning as expected.

When hooks fail during commits and rollback occurs, tasks.jsonl becomes unstaged causing git stash conflicts. Resolution: format committed files, explicitly stage tasks.jsonl, create new commit to replace failed commit.

The `call_tool` MCP tool requires `server_name` as a mandatory parameter field in the arguments object. Validation will fail with 'Field required' error if omitted. This is a required field distinct from the tool name and its arguments.

Agent definition redesign vision (task #8982): Restructure AgentDefinition.workflows from dict[str, WorkflowSpec] to a flat struct with: workflows.pipeline (one pipeline name, DB-backed not file), workflows.rules (list of rule names), workflows.variables (pre-seed session vars, override rule defaults). Pipeline auto-start is configurable. YAML files are templates, DB is source of truth. Agents become orchestration containers. Depends on generic→default rename (#8974) and variable layer cleanup.
MCP tool access follows progressive disclosure pattern—discovery tools must be available before other MCP tools become accessible

Gobby MCP tools require explicit session_id parameter. Calling mcp__gobby__call_tool or similar gobby tools without passing session_id returns error: 'session_id is required. Pass the session ID explicitly...'

When closing gobby tasks via mcp__gobby_ tool, even tasks being closed as duplicates require a commit SHA if they have associated file edits. The API returns a 'missing_commits_for_edits' error without it. Provide the same commit SHA from the primary task to resolve this.

gcode/gsqz project architecture and fastembed feedback should be extracted/migrated by a gobby-cli agent (in its own memory namespace), not by gobby project cleanup. Memories are project-isolated by path—gobby memories at ~/.claude/projects/-Users-josh-Projects-gobby/ are invisible to gobby-cli sessions, preventing cross-project memory pollution.

System project IDs in storage/projects.py follow a fixed UUID pattern: ORPHANED=...0000, MIGRATED=...0001, GLOBAL=...0002, PERSONAL=...60887. When code needs a project_id for system-wide/global resources (like MCP servers from ~/.gobby/.mcp.json), use GLOBAL_PROJECT_ID — never a bare string like 'global' since mcp_servers.project_id has a FK constraint to projects(id).
SkillPort hub is located at repository `gotalab/skillport`

gobby tools have a `consecutive-tool-b` restriction preventing rapid sequential tool calls. Multiple `call_tool` attempts in succession trigger cancellation errors with 'Cancelled: parallel tool call' message.

Task claiming in gobby: `task_claimed` variable is set ONLY in `src/gobby/workflows/rule_engine.py:133` on the backend. Frontend TaskPanel.tsx reads it. Python backend is clean regarding task claiming logic — no other backend sources set or check this variable.

Gobby variables persist across daemon restarts when scoped to 'session'. Persistence is handled through get_variables(session_id: str) for reading and merge_variables(session_id, updates: dict) for writing. The session_id is the key parameter that maintains state across daemon restarts.

The branch '0.2.18' has no upstream tracking branch configured. Pushing requires explicit upstream setup with 'git push --set-upstream origin 0.2.18' or similar flag, which caused a fatal error (exit code 128) during the session.
Bundled variable count in _session.py is 18 (not 15). Test assertions in test_session_defaults.py require updating to expect 18 variables. This code/test mismatch was resolved in commit 679f5d63.

The gobby project has 3 failing import tests in tests/servers/routes/test_configuration_routes.py::TestExportImport::test_import_confi* that predate recent work. These are pre-existing test failures, not caused by current changes.

MCP tool arguments must be passed as valid JSON objects in the 'arguments' parameter. URL-encoded or string-literal formats fail with 'Invalid JSON' error (Turn 42: error when passing 'task_id=#10454&sess...').

In gobby's hook system, `event.metadata` never gets `project_path` populated during session events. The `project_path` is only injected as a fallback into the `arguments` dict in `hook_manager.py:768-769`, not into `event.metadata`. Session event handlers (`_session.py`) populate metadata with `_platform_session_id` and `_modified_input` but not `project_path`. This means hooks that look for `project_path` in `event.metadata` won't find it.
After running `uv sync` for dependency updates, restart the daemon service to pick up changes.

## Skill Injection Architecture Decision (In Progress)

The skill auto-injection pipeline is currently broken end-to-end:
- `discovery` skill has `alwaysApply: true` but is never injected at session start
- `inject-skills-on-start` rule is `enabled: false` and its template `{{ skills_list }}` references a variable the rule engine never populates
- `SkillInjector.select_skills()` is fully implemented but never called in the session-start path
- `_db_skill_to_parsed()` doesn't reconstruct `audience_config` from DB, so 15+ audience-scoped skills lose their config
- `_inject_context_aware_skills()` is an intentional stub returning empty string
- `canvas` skill's `conditions:` frontmatter is dead data — parser ignores it

**Proposed direction (Josh thinking on it)**: Instead of `alwaysApply` on skills, make the default agent always apply to web sessions. The agent becomes the composition unit that bundles the right skills for its context. "Always-apply the agent, not the skill." Users could create custom web chat agents with different prompts/skills. This eliminates the need for a skill injection path that works outside agent scope.

**Key insight**: `discovery` skill not injecting may explain why agents struggle with MCP progressive disclosure.

**Open question**: Chicken-and-egg — need to wire up the default agent as the web UI agent that always starts on web chats. If that's done, no need for agent-less skill injection path.

gobby-cli Rust binaries (Neo4j and Qdrant driver crates) connect via network ports (Neo4j: 7474/7687, Qdrant: 6333/6334), not Docker Compose volume names. Volume naming is strictly a Docker Compose orchestration concern with no gobby-cli impact.

gobby installer modules (codex.py, copilot.py, claude) share common test patterns in tests/cli/test_installers_shared.py. When modifying installer interfaces (e.g., removing _is_dev_mode), both the individual installer tests AND the shared test file must be updated together.

The mcp__gobby_call_tool function requires both 'server_name' and 'tool_name' as required parameters. Turn 40 shows explicit validation error: 'server_name Field required'. Multiple failed attempts with different parameter structures (turns 8-24) were resolved only when this requirement was identified.

compress-bash-output rule updates require synchronizing three locations: Template YAML, Installed DB row (which the rule engine actively evaluates), and Template DB row (for future deployment consistency). Missing any of these updates causes configuration inconsistency and rule engine may not pick up the change.
No `spawn_agent` step type exists in pipelines. Agents spawn via `mcp` steps calling the `spawn_agent` tool on `gobby-agents` MCP server.

Gobby file edit operations require task association enforced by hook_dispatcher (~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py). Personal/non-project files cannot be edited through Gobby tools—the 'tool-failure-recovery' rule blocks edits without task context. Manual edits bypass this constraint.

Gobby _mem0_start() in daemon.py now resolves OPENAI_API_KEY from the SecretStore and injects it into the docker compose subprocess env. This keeps the API key out of .env files and off disk. The flow: instantiate LocalDatabase + SecretStore from config, call secret_store.get('OPENAI_API_KEY'), pass via env dict to subprocess.run(). The compose file references ${OPENAI_API_KEY} which gets resolved from the subprocess environment.

Gobby tool output compression architecture: Bash/native tools rewrite via PreToolUse updatedInput with 'gobby compress --' (mirrors RTK pattern); MCP tools replace output via PostToolUse updatedMCPToolOutput. Requires new rule engine effect types: rewrite_input (PreTool) and output replacement (PostTool).

The mcp__gobby_call_tool proxy requires a 'server_name' parameter as a required field. Calling it without this parameter fails with validation error 'Field required'. This is necessary for invoking gobby tools.

When closing a gobby task via mcp__gobby_ tool, tasks with file edits require commit SHAs even when closing as a duplicate of another task. Omitting commits returns error 'missing_commits_for_edits'. Provide the relevant commit SHA from the parent/original task to resolve.

Agent death match R1 battle report written to ./agent-death-match-r1.md. Blue Team (#1810) vs Red Team (#1809) design improvement game over P2P messaging. Subject: 'Read Your Mail' rule. Blue Team won. Key design outcomes: (1) Jinja2 templated block reasons, (2) hot-path boolean / cold-path count helper split, (3) zombie message maintenance job, (4) wildcard agent_scope support, (5) universal _render_template refactor for rule engine.

`generate_handoff` (src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/sessions/_actions.py:51) and `create_handoff` (_actions.py:164) are redundant tools performing essentially the same function with different signatures. Cleanup/deduplication task #9112 was created.

Plans must be tight and concise. No verbose thinking, open-question analysis, or stream-of-consciousness reasoning in plan files. If there are open questions, resolve them before writing the plan. Plans are for execution, not exploration.

Editing YAML pipeline templates does NOT affect running pipeline behavior. The DB (cron_jobs.action_config) is the actual source of truth for cron-triggered pipelines. To change pipeline parameters (e.g., developer_provider), you must update the cron job's action_config in the database, not just the YAML file.

_install_gcode() at line 797 in installer writes gcode binary to Path.home() / ".gobby" / "bin". This path is hardcoded identically in trigger.py. No environment variables or shutil.which() fallbacks—the hardcoded path is intentional and consistent across installer and runtime.

In gobby's agent event handlers, the chain to access workflow state is `self._workflow_handler.engine.state_manager` (WorkflowHookHandler has `self.engine`, which has `state_manager`). This specific object path is needed to access workflow state from within hook handlers.
sync.py refactor target: reduce from ~420 lines to ~100 lines by eliminating template/installed shadow management logic

Proposed architectural fix: in _process_session_transcript function, create stub/empty transcript files when transcript JSONL is missing, allowing sessions to complete gracefully instead of indefinite retry loops. Replace current 'defer and retry' logic with this graceful degradation approach.

Progressive discovery enforcement: The preseed rule (preseed-progressive-discovery.yaml) was removed because it pre-populated listed_servers with internal servers, defeating the require-server-listed-for-schema block rule. All servers now follow the same discovery chain: list_mcp_servers -> list_tools -> get_tool_schema -> call_tool. The tools field on RuleDefinitionBody acts as a pre-filter (skips rule evaluation when current tool doesn't match), while tools on RuleEffect is used by _should_block for tool-specific blocking.

When no project.json exists, the hook manager should fall back to personal workspace mode rather than throwing an error. This is tested via mocking _resolve_project_id in test_session_start_context_injection (tests/hooks/test_hooks_context.py). Tests need proper @patch decorators on _resolve_project_id to verify this fallback behavior works.
Git commit messages containing parentheses cause shell escaping failures with -m flag. Workaround: use 'git commit -F filename' approach, writing the commit message to a file instead.

The gobby project has a systematic import aliasing issue across workflow modules: `common_helpers` module is imported as `helpers` in pipeline_executor.py and approval.py, but the actual module name is `common_helpers` in src/gobby/workflows/. This causes 17 'Cannot find reference' errors across workflow files. The fix requires adding `from gobby.workflows.common_helpers import get_workflow` to storage/pipelines.py and consistent aliasing across all workflow modules.
Installed template skills follow directory structure: src/gobby/install/shared/skills/{template_name}/ (e.g., src/gobby/install/shared/skills/agents/ for agents skills)

ToolSearch tool fails with PreToolUse hook errors in the gobby environment (observed in turns 5 and 36). Use mcp__gobby_ prefix tools directly to discover and interact with gobby tools instead (e.g., mcp__gobby_tools).
`gobby agents kill` requires a RUN_REF argument (e.g., `run-713d1038`), not a `--session` flag. To find the RUN_REF to terminate, first run `gobby agents list` to discover available agent processes.

The call_tool MCP function requires a 'server_name' field in the arguments—this is a required parameter. Omitting it causes a 'Field required' validation error. For the gobby MCP, the server_name should be set to 'gobby'.
gobby's call_tool endpoint requires 'server_name' as a mandatory parameter in the schema. Omitting it causes 'Field required' validation errors (Turn 46).

call_tool 'Unknown parameter server_name/tool_name' bug root cause: LLMs sometimes flatten call_tool's own parameters (server_name, tool_name) into the arguments dict/string instead of passing them as separate MCP parameters. Fix: strip these keys from the arguments dict in tool_proxy.py (the convergence point for all call paths). Three entry points exist: server.py (HTTP server), stdio.py (Claude Code's actual path via DaemonProxy → HTTP), and tool_proxy.py (convergence). The stdio process is NOT restarted by gobby restart — it's spawned by Claude Code. Daemon restart only restarts the HTTP server. Commit 8f0c6600.

`gobby start` must kill pre-existing watchdog processes before spawning new ones. Without this, stray watchdogs accumulate (persist for days) and trigger daemon restart loops every ~30s. Fixed in commit 72f08c2b.

index_folder and index_repo MCP tools should migrate to CLI commands due to being setup/maintenance operations with unbounded execution time; enables composability (e.g., `gobby index . && gobby index ~/other-project`) and streaming progress output. invalidate_index should possibly remain as MCP tool (fast operation, useful for agent-driven state cleanup). Design decision pending implementation approval.
The gobby CLI command is not available in the shell environment, producing error 127 ('command not found'). This occurred in turns 14 and 38.

Neo4j password configuration differs between template and installed: installed uses ${GOBBY_NEO4J_PASSWORD:-gobbyneo4j} with fallback default, template uses ${GOBBY_NEO4J_PASSWORD} without fallback causing empty value if unset. Missing fallback affects both password and healthcheck configs and can cause startup failures.

Vitest unit tests located at: web/src/components/canvas/__tests__/A2UIRenderer.test.tsx (canvas rendering), web/src/components/chat/__tests__/ToolCallCard.test.ts (tool call cards). Convention uses __tests__ subdirectory within component directories.

`gobby agents kill` requires a RUN_REF positional argument (format: run-XXXXX from `gobby agents list` output), not --session or other named options. Discover available RUN_REFs by running `gobby agents list` first.
Test fixture must include session_transcripts table with proper schema for _maybe_snapshot_transcript function. Its absence breaks transcript snapshot operations and causes test failures.

Bmad currently uses dual-layer architecture: (1) personas implemented via prompt injection through skills stubs loading agent definitions, (2) step workflows for sequential execution. This is the existing pattern to understand for integration.

Expansion pipeline 4-step structure: (1) spawn agent with Glob/Grep/Read access, (2) validate spec via MCP validation step (not agent), (3) atomic execution via `execute_expansion()`, (4) wire affected_files via TaskAffectedFileManager MCP call

suggest-skills-on-prompt rule fires on before_agent hook with search_skills(top_k=5) to contextually discover and inject relevant skills. This mechanism already handles conditional skill loading based on conversation context.

Three work items deferred pending parallel session's 'blocking wait retooling' completion: (1) parallel dispatch refactoring, (2) synthetic e2e test design, (3) function renaming from `dispatch_developers` to `dispatch_agents`. Do not resume until blocking wait retooling is complete.

The `[require-error-triage]` Gobby rule blocks daemon stop operations via `hook_dispatcher.py --cli=claude --type=stop`, requiring lint and tests to pass before shutdown. This gate ensures uncommitted errors are properly triaged before daemon stops.

Orchestrator expand_epic step will fail with 'No subtasks in spec' error if expander produces zero subtasks, preventing any straightforward single-task support. Would require conditional fork in orchestrator logic to detect epic vs standalone task and conditionally skip expansion.

The clear-memory-review-on-create rule in memory-lifecycle templates has enabled: false by design (all templates do). The memory-review-gate stop hook fires when pending_memory_review is set, but the clearing rule only works if it's been installed and enabled in the DB. When implementing rules that depend on other rules clearing variables, verify the dependent rule is actually active.
RTK is ~6 weeks old with focused pattern matcher set (git, pytest, cargo, npm) rather than years of accumulated heuristics. Smaller scope affects integration feasibility.

The workflow engine's rule-checking system expects `check_rules` attribute on WorkflowStep objects. This is part of the lifecycle rule evaluation flow and must be present (even as empty list) on all step mocks to avoid runtime failures during workflow execution.

ToolSearch tool is consistently blocked by a PreToolUse hook from hook_dispatcher.py, returning 'PreToolUse:ToolSearch hook error'. Attempting to use ToolSearch fails repeatedly - consider skipping this tool and using mcp__gobby_* tools directly instead.

Task closure enforces clean git working tree via task-enforcement rule. Attempting to close a task fails if untracked or dirty files exist. User had to `git stash` untracked TimeReportEntry files before task #10136 could transition to closed state.
pre-push-test.sh lines 58-67: frontend tests execute via 'cd web && npx vitest run --coverage', positioned between TypeScript type-check and Bandit security scan

Rules require tag:gobby in definition to be included by default agent's rule_selectors. Rules created mid-session not picked up until restart because _active_rule_names functions as session-start whitelist.

The `gobby agents kill` command requires a RUN_REF parameter (e.g., 'run-713d1038'), not a --session flag. Using `--session` returns a 'No such option' error. Correct syntax: `gobby agents kill <RUN_REF>` where RUN_REF can be obtained from `gobby agents list` output.

Gemini CLI shows keyboard shortcut tips and settings prompts that block agent input in tmux sessions. Suppressed by ui.hideTips=true in ~/.gemini/settings.json (auto-set by gobby install as of #10978). The previous Escape safety net (check_gemini_tip_prompts) was removed in #10984 because the pattern matched normal generation progress indicators, causing a death loop of cancelled requests. The daemon health check timeout in hook_dispatcher.py is 5.0s (was 0.5s) and _MAX_DAEMON_FAILURES is 5 (was 3) — agents need 25s+ of sustained daemon unreachability before self-terminating.

Gobby MCP Progressive Disclosure: The flow is STRICT and hook-enforced: list_mcp_servers() → list_tools(server) → get_tool_schema(server, tool) → call_tool(server, tool, args). Each step gates the next. The gobby MCP system exposes 15 proxy tools (including add_mcp_server, call_tool, get_tool_alternatives, get_tool_schema, get_variable, import_mcp_server, list_mcp_resources, read_mcp_resource, remove_mcp_server, and others). These proxy tools are top-level deferred tools (e.g. mcp__gobby__get_tool_schema). ToolSearch does NOT return these proxy tools — they must be called directly or discovered via introspection. Never skip list_tools — the hook will block get_tool_schema if you haven't called it for that server. NOT all 15 tools are directly accessible as top-level proxy tools in Claude Code: add_mcp_server, remove_mcp_server, and call_tool work as direct proxies, but at least 3 (status, get_tool_alternatives, and one other) are not directly accessible. Full accessibility mapping requires systematic testing.

When pre-commit hook fails with formatting errors despite `ruff format` reporting 'unchanged', run `gobby hooks run pre-commit` directly to diagnose. The failure may be caused by pre-existing formatting violations in files outside the current change set (e.g., mcp.py, handlers.py). These must be fixed per project policy before commit succeeds.
ToolSearch tool fails consistently with 'PreToolUse hook error' referencing '~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py'. Use mcp__gobby_list_tools instead for tool discovery in gobby projects.

ClawHub CLI: Package renamed from `clawdhub` to `clawhub` (npm). Current version: v0.7.0. Key CLI differences from what was originally coded:
- Version flag: `--cli-version` or `-V` (not `--version`)
- `search` has no `--json` output — text only, format: `slug  vX.Y.Z  summary`
- `explore --json` for browsing remote registry (not `list` which is local lockfile only)
- `inspect <slug> --json` for skill details (not `info` which doesn't exist)
- `install <slug>` has no `--json`, uses `--force` for non-interactive
- Registry: clawdhub.com / clawhub.ai
- Docs: https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/blob/main/docs/cli.md
When gobby HTTP API tool invocations fail during task operations, use CLI recovery via: uv run python "~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py" --cli=claude --type=stop
useWebSocketEvent singleton hook in usePipelineExecutions.ts consolidates WebSocket lifecycle management; replaces ~35 lines of manual code and eliminates reconnects triggered by filter changes

Tool-result WebSocket matching uses `tool_use_id` column in `session_messages` table, persisted via `_persist_tool_call()` function in `_messaging.py`. Modified `useChat.ts` WebSocket handler to match on `tool_use_id` instead of positional array indexing. Database-backed approach survives connection resets and client restarts.

The `arguments` parameter passed to `call_tool` must be valid JSON string format, not URL-encoded or plain parameter strings. Passing non-JSON like 'task_id=#10454&session...' causes 'Invalid JSON in arguments parameter' errors.

Parent tasks (epics) in `src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/tasks/_lifecycle.py` skip commit requirements and LLM validation if all children are closed. If children exist with any open, close fails immediately. Non-parent tasks still enforce had_edits session checks. This enables clean epic lifecycle management without unnecessary commit friction.

The mcp__gobby call_tool function requires a 'server_name' parameter in its arguments schema (call_toolArguments). Omitting this field results in validation error 'Field required [type=missing]'. Arguments must be passed as valid JSON objects, not URL-encoded strings.

File decomposition patterns used to bring 4 files under 1,000 lines:

1. **Package extraction** (spawn_agent.py 1016→5 files, max 540): Convert single file to package with __init__.py re-exports. Best when file has 4+ distinct responsibility groups. Follow existing pattern (tasks/ was already a package).

2. **Module-level function extraction** (runner.py 1068→807 + 2 modules): Extract groups of standalone methods into separate modules as functions. Used runner_broadcasting.py (160 lines) and runner_maintenance.py (120 lines). Works when methods are self-contained utilities called during init.

3. **Mixin extraction** (chat.py 1153→774 + session_control.py 439): Extract methods into a new mixin class, add to WebSocket server's inheritance chain. Follow existing mixin pattern (HandlerMixin, AuthMixin, etc.). Use TYPE_CHECKING for cross-mixin method references.

4. **Helper deduplication + setup extraction** (install.py 1114→942 + install_setup.py 119): Extract repeated output formatting into helper functions (_echo_install_details, _echo_uninstall_details). Move setup logic to separate module. Use re-export for backward compatibility with test patches: `_ensure_daemon_config = ensure_daemon_config`.

Key lesson: When moving functions that are patched in tests, either (a) keep the call site in the original module so patches still work, or (b) update all test patch targets. Option (a) is safer when there are many patches.

Decision: SkillSearch should be removed as a standalone component in gobby. It currently wraps UnifiedSearcher (which takes embed_fn), but should be refactored to use the same inject-embed-fn-at-runtime pattern used by memory (VectorStore) and code indexer (CodeIndexer).

In gobby, the PreToolUse:Write hook (hook_dispatcher.py) can reject file writes with a validation error. To write plan files, you may need to first call the set_session_variable MCP tool to set required session state before the Write tool will be permitted by the hook.

Issue affects all tools with optional parameters in Gobby's tool schema serialization - systemic problem requiring fix in protocol layer, not individual tools. Solutions: recursive exclude_none logic, scrub null from inputSchema dicts before serialization, or configure Pydantic for deep field cleaning.

When adding new tables to BASELINE_SCHEMA in migrations.py, fresh databases apply baseline only (no migration functions run). If the migration function includes data import logic (like bundled YAML import), that logic must also be called from _apply_baseline(). Extract shared logic into a separate function callable from both places. The _import_bundled_workflows() pattern demonstrates this.

WorkflowsPage layout was restructured: title row ("Workflows" + context-aware create button) is separate from the filter row (search, Hide Built-in toggle, source select, refresh). The enforcement kill switch (rules.enforcement_enabled) was moved from RulesTab to ConfigurationPage as a 'Rules Engine' section at the top of the config form tab. useConfiguration hook gained rulesEnforcement + setRulesEnforcement. The RulesTab no longer has the enforcement toggle - only the bulk Enable All toggle for installed/project rules remains.

Sequential integration tests in gobby use a DB-backed WorkflowLoader (not file-based). The fix for task #8366 involved switching test_sequential_integration.py to use DB-backed WorkflowLoader, committed in v0.2.17 (5730410e).

NEVER rm -rf clone directories or worktree directories without asking the user first. Clones contain independent commits that don't exist in the main repo. Deleting a clone permanently destroys uncommitted agent work.

The `synthesize_title` action in session-lifecycle.yaml uses two prompts: `prompt` (first prompt) and `continuation_prompt` (for 'continue' input). The handler always extracts `title_prompt` from context (defaults to `continuation_prompt`), making it impossible to use the first-prompt behavior when a plan is resumed with 'continue'. This causes 'continue' to become the literal title instead of generating a contextual one.
gobby agents kill command outputs "Failed: None" when it actually succeeds. This is misleading UX—don't misinterpret as actual failure.

FastEmbed (both Python and Rust crates) has a known bug where nomic-embed-text-v1.5 embeddings are not properly L2-normalized despite the model requiring Matryoshka normalization (GitHub issue qdrant/fastembed#204, unresolved as of fastembed v0.8.0). This produces subtly wrong similarity scores. Decision made to pivot gcode from fastembed to llama-cpp2 (Rust llama.cpp bindings) for embeddings before sprint 2.
The PATH in launchd's plist is static (captured at install time) - any PATH entries added after service installation won't be visible to the launchd daemon without reinstalling the service

A2UI Canvas architecture: Components are defined in a flat surface map keyed by unique IDs (not nested). Parent components reference children via `children: { explicitList: ["child-id"] }`. Data binding uses BoundValue objects: `{ literalString: "text" }` for literals, `{ path: "field/name" }` for data model binding. The render pipeline is: A2UIRenderer -> RenderComponent -> Component -> RenderChildren (for containers) -> RenderComponent (recursive). Container components (Row, Column, Card, List) must pass both `updateField` and `depth` props through to RenderChildren, otherwise nested form inputs silently break. Backend canvas.py uses in-memory state (_canvases dict) with canvas_present copying HTML files to get_gobby_home()/canvas/. Files at web/src/components/canvas/ (frontend) and src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/canvas.py (backend).

hook_dispatcher.py does not persist schema state across WebSocket reconnections; previously fetched schemas become stale after reconnection and require explicit re-fetch for correct tool command execution
Index file paths are stored as relative paths (indexer.py:191 uses path.relative_to(root)), which enables index reusability across cloned repos with identical directory structures.

Multi-agent error-handling convention: when discovering lint/test failures, check `git diff --name-only` to determine code origin. Uncommitted code (WIP from another agent) → create bug task and move on. Committed code → agent owns fix, must fix in place and commit. Set `pre_existing_errors_triaged=true` after verification passes.

Pattern for gated attempt-limited blocking: pair 'when' condition checking variable count (error_triage_blocks < 3) with set_variable effect incrementing via {{ variables.get('error_triage_blocks', 0) + 1 }} before block effect in same rule
mcp__gobby_call_tool endpoint requires a 'server_name' parameter as a mandatory field. Validation error occurs if missing: 'Field required [type=missing]'

In gobby, Claude Code hooks use a symlinked hook_dispatcher.py at .claude/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py (invoked via 'uv run python' with '--type=pre-tool-use|post-tool-use|stop' flags). If the symlink target is deleted (e.g., by deleting old per-CLI dispatcher files in src/gobby/install/), ALL hooks break simultaneously—pre-tool-use blocks all tool calls, and the stop hook enters an infinite error retry loop, making the session unrecoverable without manual terminal intervention.

Gobby's summarization pipeline requires synchronized updates across three files: compact.md (concise prompt), session_end.md (session summary template), and summarize.py (code). Both compact and full summaries must have equal access to claimed_tasks, session_memories, and digest_turn data. In summarize.py, _generate_full_summary() must call _get_claimed_tasks(), _get_session_memories(), _extract_digest_turns() and pass results in context dict to match compact summary capabilities.

Three-tier storage pattern for prompts follows the same approach as rules and skills: bundled (seeded from install/shared/prompts/ on startup), user (overrides in DB), project (project-scoped overrides). Tier precedence: project > user > bundled. Key files: storage/prompts.py (LocalPromptManager), prompts/sync.py (sync_bundled_prompts), prompts/loader.py (configure_default_loader). Migration v102 in storage/migrations.py.
validate_commit_requirements executes before parent task checks and blocks epics lacking their own commits — this execution order may cause unexpected validation failures in epic task workflows
The call_tool MCP proxy requires 'server_name' as a mandatory field parameter. Omitting it produces validation error: 'Field required'. This is essential for correct gobby tool invocation.

Pipeline condition bug: Jinja2 renders boolean `false` as the string "False" which is truthy. Pipeline conditions like `${{ steps.X.output.completed if steps.X.output else false }}` always pass because the else branch renders "False" (non-empty string). Fix: use `${{ steps.X.output.completed if steps.X.output and steps.X.output.completed else '' }}` to ensure empty string on failure.
Glob patterns fail for compression test file discovery; use absolute file paths to run compression tests instead
gsqz resolves HTTP API base_url via `resolve_daemon_url()` which chains: config file → `GOBBY_PORT` environment variable → default values

LLM generate_text interface now supports max_tokens parameter across all providers (base.py, claude.py, gemini.py, litellm.py, codex.py). SDK path in claude.py post-truncates since ClaudeAgentOptions doesn't support max_tokens. LiteLLM paths pass max_tokens directly to acompletion. Default is 8000 when not specified. Session title synthesis uses model='haiku' with max_tokens=30 to prevent unbounded output.

SessionSource enum values: old scheme used `embedded`, `headless`; new scheme uses `interactive`, `autonomous`, `web_chat`. SessionSource.EMBEDDED does not exist—code referencing it will fail and must use one of the new values instead.

TDD enforcement uses two rules: `enforce-tdd-block.yaml` (before_tool) and `enforce-tdd-track-tests.yaml` (after_tool), configured via variables in `gobby-default-variables.yaml`: `enforce_tdd` (bool), `tdd_nudged_files` (list), `tdd_tests_written` (list)

Unresolved: whether tool output compression integrates upstream (before gobby-tests executes) or within gobby-tests summarization layer. Affects where compression logic is implemented relative to existing test/lint process capture.

Pre-existing unresolved test isolation issue: HookManager._dispatch_session_summaries generates unawaited coroutine with KeyError: '__import__' during test teardown in tests/mcp_proxy/tools/test_tasks_coverage.py. Triggered by test_unknown_model_returns_zero in cost table tests. Not related to _lifecycle_close.py changes.

Embedding formula in indexer.py:_embed_symbols() was changed from `name + signature + docstring + summary` to `name + signature + docstring` by migration 179. This is the Python daemon embedding pipeline only; gcode's Rust-based embeddings were handled separately.
When querying the rules registry, each compression rule appears twice in output (disabled template + enabled installed version). This is expected correct behavior, not a duplicate or error.

Git timestamp timezone gotcha: When using git --since/--until with timestamps parsed from the database (which stores UTC with +00:00 offset), you MUST include the timezone offset in the formatted string (use %z). Without it, git interprets bare timestamps as local time, causing incorrect filtering. Also, session.jsonl_path parent (~/.claude/projects/...) is NOT a git repo — use project.repo_path from the projects table instead.

SessionStart hook incomplete output pattern: `is_first_hook` branch can execute successfully but fail to populate full startup metadata block (agent details, rules count, variables, skills). Not caused by daemon unavailability; investigate conditional guards and state initialization within the branch.

LM Studio doesn't support NV-Embed-v2 model family in its built-in model catalog or GGUF/MLX converters. Alternative: use nomic-embed-text-v1.5, which is more portable and can be packaged as part of installation.

When sessions crash during workflow engine refactoring, new sessions often pick up the same work and commit under new task IDs, leaving original tasks stale in in_progress state. To triage: cross-reference git log task ID tags against open task list, run the actual tests mentioned in bug tasks to check if issues still reproduce, then close as already_implemented (with commit_sha from the actual fix commit) or duplicate. close_task with reason='already_implemented' or 'duplicate' auto-skips validation but still needs commit_sha if the original session had edits.
Correct Playwright MCP package is @playwright/mcp@latest, NOT @anthropic/mcp-server-playwright (latter doesn't exist). Package name mismatch causes connection failures.

Never do 'simplest fix' — always do the best, most correct fix. When a tool is missing functionality that a sibling tool has (e.g., clones missing task_id resolution that worktrees has), add proper parity rather than working around the gap.
skill-scanner was identified as stale dependency and removed during uv sync. Document reason before potential re-addition.

The full Agent Definition Migration was completed across 3 sessions with 6 commits:
1. 16b94f1f - Rename 'generic' agent to 'default' (#8974)
2. 2d045d74 - Evolve AgentDefinitionBody schema with AgentWorkflows (#8982)
3. d24fb489 - Consolidate spawn_agent factory and delete _v2.py (#9023)
4. 0221d236 - Fresh agent YAMLs, simplify sync (#9023)
5. 4ccee279 - Delete AgentDefinition model and all conversion code (#9023)
6. 1df11664 - Add agent definition CRUD tools to gobby-agents MCP server (#9024)

Key architecture: AgentDefinitionBody (15 fields) is now the single source of truth. Old AgentDefinition (29 fields), WorkflowSpec, AgentDefinitionLoader all deleted. Agent defs stored in workflow_definitions table with workflow_type='agent'. Bundled YAML sync parses directly as AgentDefinitionBody. MCP tools for CRUD in agent_definitions.py.

Triage rule anti-pattern in Gobby workflow: 'Fix bugs or confirm none' creates path-of-least-resistance non-compliance not from agent laziness but from lack of verification feedback loop. Fix requires: (1) prescriptive steps instead of vague instructions (e.g., `ruff check`, `pytest` explicitly), (2) distinction between 'bugs introduced by my changes' (must fix) vs 'pre-existing issues' (create task), (3) remove easy escapes by requiring verification before confirmation. Consider MCP-based automated verification to make assertions checkable rather than self-reported.

Current architectural gap: Workflows store templates on filesystem with source='installed' in DB (no template rows); Skills still use source='template' with enabled=False in DB. Workflows already have hashing and drift detection implemented. Aligning skills requires moving templates from DB to filesystem following same pattern.

test_stop_failure requires patching get_service_status() in the test mock. Real implementation runs unpatched and detects actual installed launchd services, causing stop command to return success before stop_daemon_util can be tested. Partial mocking of service manager alone is insufficient.

SessionStart hooks silent in Claude Code execute successfully (additionalContext injected in session context proves execution). Daemon returns exit 0 with valid system_message/context/hookSpecificOutput JSON. Handler runs hook_manager.py:343-346, adapter maps systemMessage at line 191-192. Root cause is Claude Code UI display, not daemon/handler/config. Check additionalContext injection as diagnostic for hook execution before re-investigating daemon systems.
Gobby's indexing tools default to 5-minute timeout, which is insufficient for large codebases and requires configuration adjustment

Docker Compose unification: `neo4j.py` and `qdrant.py` installers refactored to use shared `docker-compose.services.yml` instead of isolated compose files. Previously `neo4j.py` used isolated `docker-compose.neo4j.yml` with pinned `neo4j:5.26`.

The tool_block_pending mechanism exists in rule_engine.py (lines 177-183, 192-193) but is hardcoded. Rules cannot block on tool failures if the failure hooks never fire (see hook bypass issue)—making rule-based fixes inherit the same architectural blind spot.

When .gobby/tasks.jsonl diverges between local and remote (e.g., seq_nums renumbered during import), recovery procedure: 1) cp tasks.jsonl tasks_backup.jsonl, 2) grab remote version, 3) clear out tasks, 4) resync, 5) cherry-pick new local-only tasks from backup. The import bug can renumber hundreds of tasks (356 observed in one case).

Vercel's AI Chat SDK demo implements the 'New Chat' button using a Button component with variant='outline' and a PlusIcon SVG component. Rediscovering this specific pattern requires browsing the GitHub repository and fetching component files (~6-8 minutes of web search + file navigation).

The `call_tool` proxy function in gobby requires a 'server_name' parameter. Calls without this parameter fail with validation error 'Field required'. This was discovered when attempting to invoke mcp__gobby_ tools.

CodeRabbit review commit 5faa48cb changed exception handling in unified_evaluator.py from `except (ValueError, Exception)` (catch-all) to split handlers that re-raise unexpected exceptions. This broke the MCP server by crashing the workflow evaluation hook chain on any unexpected error during condition evaluation. The `except (ValueError, Exception)` pattern is intentionally a catch-all — workflow conditions run user-defined expressions and must never crash the hook pipeline. Additionally, the same commit changed _session.py to return early on non-allow workflow responses during SESSION_START, which could short-circuit session initialization. Both changes together caused MCP server connection failures.
Gobby [require-task-close] stop hook enforces that tasks in in_progress status must be explicitly closed via close_task() before commit is allowed

When closing a task that was routed to review (needs_review status), you cannot directly close it. You must first approve it (mark_task_review_approved) then close it. The state machine enforces needs_review → review_approved → closed transitions.

The `set_variable` invocation within the task-transitions skill had malformed/incorrectly parameterized arguments (fixed in commit a716297a3). Recommendation: audit ALL `set_variable` invocations across all workflow definitions to prevent similar parameter mismatches, as this is a recurring pattern risk.

Double success wrapping bug: gobby-workflows, gobby-skills, gobby-agents modules return {"success":true,"result":{"success":true,...}} instead of {"success":true,"result":{...}}. Affected tools: get_skill, run_pipeline, get_pipeline_status, wait_for_completion, get_pipeline, list_rules, create_rule, list_agents. Root cause: response handlers wrap results in success field, then outer handler adds another success envelope.

Isolation mode 'current' was renamed to 'none' across the entire codebase. CurrentIsolationHandler was renamed to NoneIsolationHandler. The Literal type in definitions.py, isolation.py, _factory.py, _implementation.py all use Literal['none', 'worktree', 'clone']. Default fallbacks changed from 'current' to 'none'. Mode=self override uses 'none' instead of 'current'.

Gobby compression infrastructure: `compress-bash-output` rule uses `before_tool` hook, `compress-mcp-output` rule uses `after_tool` hook. Design decision: inject compression context directly into output (e.g., 'Output compressed by gobby — trust exit code') rather than relying on agent memory.

Session #1775 completed the Agent System Improvements plan across all 4 sections: (1) Removed TerminalType enum and terminal field from entire codebase - everything uses tmux, (2) Replaced old form grid with two-column AgentEditForm.tsx component, (3) Renamed isolation 'current' to 'none' everywhere + added worktree_id reuse + IsolationTargetSelector.tsx, (4) Added update_agent_rules/update_agent_variables MCP tools + PATCH endpoints + AgentRulesEditor.tsx chips + AgentVariablesEditor.tsx key-value editor. All 227 tests pass, mypy clean, tsc clean. New files: web/src/components/agents/{AgentEditForm,IsolationTargetSelector,AgentRulesEditor,AgentVariablesEditor}.tsx. ~30 files modified across backend and frontend.

Bug: RegistryContext in src/gobby/mcp_proxy/tools/tasks/_context.py line 123 had self.db instead of self.task_manager.db. RegistryContext has no db attribute - it accesses the database through task_manager.db (see __post_init__ line 59). This broke any gobby-tasks MCP tool that calls resolve_project_filter (list_tasks, save_expansion_spec, etc). Fixed in commit 70a74b4d.

The protect_production_resources fixture in tests/conftest.py previously scanned ALL sys.modules on every test setup/teardown to find 'rogue references' to load_config/save_config. Only 7 modules actually do top-level imports: gobby.config, gobby.runner, gobby.cli, gobby.cli.utils, gobby.cli.tasks._utils, gobby.mcp_proxy.stdio, gobby.watchdog. All others use lazy (in-function) imports. The scan was replaced with a hardcoded _KNOWN_CONFIG_IMPORTERS list.

Full Gobby MCP serialization pipeline: LMStudio → DaemonProxy._request() (HTTP call) → daemon returns dict with None values via resp.json() → FastMCP converts to JSON string via pydantic_core.to_json() → nulls embedded in text reach LMStudio. Root cause is the daemon response dict, not the protocol layer.

gobby MCP tool integration (mcp__gobby_* calls) consistently fails with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown par...' errors. Reliable workaround: use Bash CLI commands instead. Tested commands: 'gobby tasks reopen #TASK_ID', 'gobby agents list', 'gobby agents kill RUN_REF'. Prevents wasted debugging on broken proxy calls.

MCP server config (mcp_config.py) requires absolute paths for tool execution, not relative paths. This was a specific requirement that necessitated explicit path conversion in the configuration setup.

ToolSearch tool fails in gobby environment with 'PreToolUse:ToolSearch hook error' (observed in turns 5, 36). The hook dispatcher crashes rather than returning tool schemas. Use mcp__gobby_ tools directly for tool discovery instead.
SIGTERM signaling to orphaned PIDs must be added when lifecycle_monitor.py detects dead tmux sessions to properly clean up stranded processes and prevent resource leaks.

gobby's call_tool MCP function requires a 'server_name' field in the request; arguments must be passed as valid JSON objects, not query strings or other formats. Multiple format attempts ('Unknown parameter' errors, invalid JSON) were made before discovering this requirement.

gobby: Install globally for interactive shell use with `uv tool install -e /path/to/gobby/repo`. The `-e` flag creates an editable install that automatically reflects source code changes, eliminating need for reinstallation after code modifications.
A standard sidebar width is specified as 260px, and this template is applied in updating 'nifty-snacking-waterfall.md'.
bitsandbytes is CUDA-only and does not support MPS; 4-bit quantization via bitsandbytes is unavailable when using unsloth[mps] on Apple Silicon

Gobby [require-task-close] hook blocks exit when a task is in_progress. Task closure requires BOTH: (1) files staged and committed with git, AND (2) explicit close_task() call. The hook will intercept exit attempts until both are satisfied.

Agent cards in AgentsTab.tsx use `agent-def-card` CSS class (not `workflows-card` like Pipelines/Rules). The agent card has no padding — content padding comes from `.agent-def-header` (16px) and `.agent-def-card > .workflows-card-footer` (10px 16px 16px). The `isDb` variable (`item.source.endsWith('-db')`) was previously used to gate Edit/Delete buttons, but installed agents have source='installed' with valid db_id, so the condition was changed to just check `item.db_id`. Pipelines and Rules always show Delete for non-template items.

Pipeline clones stored at `/tmp/gobby-clones/{task_id}-{description}` during execution. Git log in clone directory is the reliable reference point for monitoring pipeline progress and agent phase transitions.

After installing llama-cpp-python via `uv sync --extra local-embeddings`, daemon must be restarted with `uv run gobby restart` to activate local embeddings. Package installation alone is insufficient without daemon restart.

Claude Code's `updatedInput` hook does a **shallow merge** (not deep merge) of provided fields into the current input. This means nested objects are replaced entirely rather than recursively merged. This behavior is undocumented and must be accounted for when designing PreToolUse hooks that modify input parameters.

Claude memory blocking architecture: Three installed rules (block-claude-memory-read, block-claude-memory-search, block-claude-memory-write) redirect all memory operations to gobby-memory MCP via UserPromptSubmit hook. Native Claude memory instructions appear in system prompt but actual filesystem operations to ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/ are blocked by rules. This is intentional design to force all memory through Gobby's MCP.

The gobby migration system (migrations.py) runs migrations sequentially by splitting SQL on `;` and executing each statement. Migration 106 failed because it tried to CREATE TABLE prompts, but an older prompts table already existed from migration 105 with a different schema (included tier, source_type, source_id, message_preview columns). The fix required adding DROP TABLE IF EXISTS prompts; before the CREATE TABLE statement in migration 106.

E2E daemon test fixture has a teardown bug: it doesn't clean up the daemon PID lockfile properly, causing 'stale PID' errors on subsequent daemon startup attempts. This requires manual daemon restart or lockfile deletion to recover.

Deprecated reset-stop-cycle-on-prompt.yaml implemented correct pattern: variable lifecycle managed by YAML rules, not Python code. Future fixes should restore rule-based reset behavior instead of Python event handler resets.

Gobby infrastructure already supports autonomous overnight execution daemon pattern via: agent spawning with isolation (worktrees/clones), pipeline executor with step orchestration, cron scheduler, P2P messaging between agents, memory/learning system for cross-session state, Slack integration via webhooks, and multi-provider LLM abstraction supporting local models via LiteLLM

Static pricing tables become unmaintainable due to constant model releases and frequent price adjustments—requires delegating primary lookups to maintained external sources (like LiteLLM's model_cost) rather than attempting comprehensive in-app tables.

Orchestrator is epic-only by design: all four scan steps (scan_open, scan_in_progress, scan_needs_review, scan_approved) filter by parent_task_id. Standalone tasks have no children, so scans return empty lists, all_closed check sees zero tasks, and exits with success—silently completing zero work with no error/warning. This is the primary blocker for single-task support.

Tree-sitter QueryCursor(query).matches(node) returns list[tuple[int, dict[str, list[Node]]]]. Capture dictionary values are always lists of nodes, not single nodes. Extracted nodes require [0] indexing to get single values.

The gobby mcp__gobby_call_tool function requires a mandatory 'server_name' parameter. Attempts without this field produced validation errors ('Field required [type=missing]'). Earlier error messages ('Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter') didn't clearly indicate the actual schema requirements, making discovery difficult.

Engine behavior for `tool_block_pending` auto-management is documented in `docs/guides/workflow-rules.md` (lines 69-70 region). The mechanism: when a rule blocks a `BEFORE_TOOL` event, the engine automatically sets `tool_block_pending` and clears it on STOP or BEFORE_AGENT state transitions (never on success events — commit 3139577).

Web UI chat view: ToolCallCard.tsx supports tool call grouping. Consecutive same-type tool calls (2+) are grouped into collapsible sections via `groupToolCalls()` pure function. Groups show a single header with count badge, composite status icon, and a format displaying call count with participant names (e.g., '3 tool calls: Agent, 2 Glob'). `render_surface`, `AskUserQuestion`, and `pending_approval` calls are never grouped. `ToolCallItem` accepts a `nested` prop for border styling inside groups. Group expand state uses stable keys (`${firstCallId}-${toolName}`) to survive streaming recomputation. Tests in `web/src/components/chat/__tests__/ToolCallCard.test.ts`.
handle-plan-mode-entry.yaml: replaced dead after_tool/EnterPlanMode trigger with before_agent trigger using guard condition 'plan_mode and not plan_skill_loaded' for plan skill injection
GOBBY_TEST_PROTECT environment variable is patched in kill_all_gobby_daemons test suite to prevent accidental daemon termination during testing
The command `gobby agents list` displays running agents and their RUN_REF identifiers (e.g., 'run-713d1038').

In gobby worktrees, pre-commit hooks fail (git commit exits code 1) because they require python3.13 which is not installed on the system. Lint and format checks pass, but the hook still fails. The main project venv at ~/Projects/gobby/.venv/bin/python3 does not provide python3.13.

Working deduplication pattern exists in memory injection system via `injected_memory_ids` session variable. Apply same pattern to skills: introduce `injected_skill_suggestions` (set of skill names already shown), add `when` guard to `suggest-skills-on-prompt` rule to skip if results already in set, update set after injection.

When task modifications involve out-of-repo files (e.g., ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py), close the task with a note rather than seeking a commit—no git diff available since changes are system-level and not repo-tracked.

In gobby, the hook_dispatcher.py in .claude/hooks/ is a symlink pointing to actual implementation files elsewhere in the project. When refactoring or deleting dispatcher files, you must update or recreate the symlink BEFORE deleting the old target files, otherwise hooks break immediately and irrecoverably within the current session.

Task sync recovery procedure when local/remote diverge: (1) cp .gobby/tasks.jsonl .gobby/tasks.jsonl.backup, (2) fetch remote DB copy, (3) clear local .gobby/tasks.jsonl, (4) resync from remote, (5) cherry-pick new local tasks from backup using jq. Requires code fix (scoping memory gates to interactive sessions) to deploy first.

Code index database (~/.gobby/gobby-hub.db) is centralized and keyed by project_id, shared across all project clones. When a project is cloned in a workflow, it receives the same project_id from the copied project.json and reuses the existing index without re-indexing.

"auto_heal" code references located at: hook_manager.py (lines 569, 615, 635), dispatchers/mcp.py (line 55), tool_proxy.py (line 36). Pattern concentrated in hook management (3 refs) and dispatch/proxy infrastructure (2 refs).
ToolSearch tool has broken pre-tool-use hooks that fail with errors (seen in Turns 5, 36). Use `mcp__gobby_*` tools instead to discover available tools—these work reliably for querying tool schemas.

The _LazyBool class in task_enforcement_actions.py lacks __eq__ and __ne__ operators, causing expressions like 'session_has_dirty_files == False' to skip thunk evaluation. Add __eq__(self, other) and __ne__(self, other) methods that coerce self to bool via bool(self) before comparison to ensure lazy evaluation is triggered and comparisons return correct True/False semantics.

Expansion sub-pipeline constraint profile: 4-step structure with (1) Research—Read/Glob/Grep/MCP allowed, Write blocked, gate: `save_expansion_spec` called; (2) Validate—MCP only, validates spec against source plan; (3) Execute—MCP only, creates tasks, gate: `execute_expansion` succeeds; (4) Wire deps—MCP only, stores affected_files and runs overlap analysis. Step 1 creative (exploration freedom), Steps 2-4 deterministic (funneled into specific MCP calls).

gobby index CLI (src/gobby/cli/code_index.py) is now standalone — constructs CodeIndexer directly against LocalDatabase, no daemon required. Uses lazy imports for tree_sitter-dependent modules (CodeIndexer, CodeParser, CodeIndexStorage) to avoid import failures when tree_sitter isn't installed. Pattern matches gobby memory CLI.

The `_resolve_session_id()` closures and `_context` tools cannot be fully deleted from the SessionContext ContextVar infrastructure (#11038) because they remain required by cross-session tools (agents, handoff, messages) which are still pending migration. Attempted deletion would break existing functionality.

Storage layer bug: list_memories() in storage.py (line 408) orders results by created_at DESC, but temporal decay model uses updated_at as freshness signal. Must change to updated_at DESC to align decay calculations with query ordering, else rankings will be inconsistent.

skill_install.py has no unit test coverage. Explicit grep searches for test functions (test_skill_install, test_install_shared_skills) and broader pytest patterns in tests/ all returned no results. If modifying this module in the future, know there are no existing tests.
ToolSearch tool fails with PreToolUse hook errors (hook_dispatcher.py-related). Use mcp__gobby_* MCP server tools instead to discover available gobby tools and their schemas.

In gobby's test_manager_graph_search.py, MemoryManager's `_kg_service` is instantiated as a real object (not auto-mocked like other services), so it must be explicitly mocked/patched in tests to avoid real connection attempts and AttributeError failures.

Tool normalization architecture: `normalize_tool_fields()` in `src/gobby/hooks/normalization.py` is the primary entry point for all CLI adapters. It runs two phases: (1) field alias normalization (function_name→tool_name, toolName→tool_name, toolArgs→tool_input with JSON parsing, parameters→tool_input, args→tool_input, mcp_context→mcp_server/mcp_tool), (2) delegates to `normalize_mcp_fields()` for mcp__ prefix parsing, call_tool inner extraction, and tool_result/tool_response→tool_output. All 6 adapters (Claude Code, Gemini, Cursor, Copilot, Windsurf, Codex) plus web chat now use this shared function. Cursor has additional handling for beforeMCPExecution/afterMCPExecution hooks (JSON string parsing + MCP server/tool extraction from tool_input + result_json→tool_output). Windsurf's _extract_tool_info uses mcp_server_name/mcp_tool_name with fallback to server_name/tool_name.

Two-track development approach approved: Workstream A (Python SDK compat fixes, immediately actionable - addresses _internal.message_parser monkey-patch and ExceptionGroup handling) and Workstream B (TypeScript chat engine rewrite, requires scope definition before proceeding). Plan created during architectural assessment.

Gobby has a find_free_port() function that needs investigation/fixing - identified during session analysis as part of a 4-task improvement plan (fix find_free_port, verify port binding logic, update config handling, add integration tests).

Current error message 'run get_tool_schema first' is misleading when actual issue is improper tool invocation method — should use direct tool invocation (e.g., mcp__gobby__set_variable) instead of call_tool with invalid server_name.

Agent runs can report 'success' status with zero recorded tool_calls_count and turns_used despite agent successfully executing work and committing code. Symptom: empty result string, tool_calls_count=0, turns_used=0, but git commit exists in clone (~/.gobby/clones/{epic-id}). Example: run-9a8af1e5 with clone-25c1de reported success but showed 0 tool calls despite commit 495ee1d7 existing. Indicates tool call recording/tracking failure in agent execution layer.
set_variable tool is located on gobby-workflows MCP server, not gobby-memory server. Required for clearing gates like require-memory-review that block shutdown via hook_dispatcher.py
MCP tools replace plugins as the extension mechanism in the project.
Auto-subscribe invoking session to continuation passes by calling `_auto_subscribe_lineage()` in `runner._rerun_pipeline()` post-execution (after pipeline runs, before returning)

_safe_send() helper in _messaging.py wraps websocket.send() to catch ConnectionClosed exceptions, sets ws_connected = False flag, and breaks the streaming loop gracefully. All 7+ send calls in the event loop plus the approval emitter refactored to use this wrapper.

HookManager._dispatch_mcp_calls bug (fixed in #9186): The hook manager subprocess has no persistent event loop, so MCP call effects from rule engine evaluation were silently dropped. The fallback path logged 'no event loop for blocking call' at DEBUG level and returned without executing. Fix: added asyncio.run() fallback when neither a running loop nor self._loop is available. This pattern matches how WorkflowHookHandler.evaluate() handles the same sync-to-async bridge. Key files: src/gobby/hooks/hook_manager.py (_dispatch_mcp_calls), src/gobby/workflows/hooks.py (WorkflowHookHandler.evaluate). The rule engine itself was confirmed working correctly via direct test — the issue was purely in dispatch.

When gobby migrations encounter table conflicts between sequential migrations, the solution is to add DROP TABLE IF EXISTS <table_name>; at the start of the later migration rather than modifying the earlier one, since the migration runner executes statements in order and users may have already run earlier migrations.

In `src/gobby/cli/workflows/inspect.py` line 62, the `--all` flag filtering logic checks `if not show_all and not wf_enabled` to skip disabled workflows. This means --all shows both enabled AND disabled workflows, while the default only shows enabled ones (wf_enabled=True). The 'enabled' field comes from the YAML's `enabled:` key (line 58), defaulting to True if not present.

When an agent with `_assigned_pipeline` fires `session_start` rule executing `run_pipeline` via `mcp_call`, the pipeline runs server-side independent of the original agent's session and can autonomously spawn agents, call tools, or execute commands.

The pre-push git hook can detect `git push --delete` operations by checking if stdin contains all-zeros SHA, and should skip verification in such cases to avoid hook verification failures during branch deletion

CLI commands init, install, and uninstall now support -C/--path to specify target directory. Before this, running from source required: `cd target && uv run --project ~/Projects/gobby gobby init`. Now: `uv run --project ~/Projects/gobby gobby init -C ~/Projects/target`. The option uses click.Path(exists=True, file_okay=False, path_type=Path) and resolves to replace Path.cwd(). initialize_project() already accepted a cwd parameter so no downstream changes were needed.
`prompt` pipeline steps are single-shot LLM calls via `llm_service.get_default_provider().generate_text(prompt)` (handlers.py:109-125) with no tools or conversation context—not conversational.

All Docker volumes standardized to gobby_* naming convention (removed Docker project prefixes). Specifically, gobby_neo4j_logs volume was added to prevent anonymous Neo4j log volume creation. Committed in 3992eaddc.

ToolSearch tool has a broken pre-tool-use hook (error: 'uv run python ~/.gobby/hooks/hook_dispatcher.py --cli=claude --type=pre-tool-use' fails). Workaround: use mcp__gobby_ MCP tools directly to access gobby functionality instead.

Gobby tool parameters must be flattened (not nested in a sub-object). Multiple attempts to pass parameters in nested structures failed with 'Invalid arguments: Unknown parameter' errors. Parameters should be passed as direct function arguments.

Web chat localStorage audit: Messages are dual-written (DB + localStorage) but frontend only reads from localStorage on initial load. DB hydration exists for conversation switches (useChat.ts lines 730-750) but not for initial page load. Other localStorage keys that should migrate to DB: `gobby-settings` (font/model/theme → config_store), `gobby-project` (selected project → config_store), `gobby-oversight-{taskId}` and `gobby-perm-overrides-{taskId}` (→ tasks table columns). Panel widths and view prefs are fine in localStorage. Full plan: docs/plans/web-chat-db-load.md. Tasks: #9132-#9138.
EnterPlanMode has no tool call—it's an internal state change detected via before_agent observer from system reminders, not a real tool invocation
